title: Task 2: Create wave-planning skill
design:
## Goal
Create skills/wave-planning/SKILL.md — a new skill that analyzes the Parallelism Map from a bd epic and creates batches of parallelizable tasks (waves), sets beads dependencies between them, and runs SRE refinement on each wave batch.

## Context
Completed bd-7yn: The brainstorming skill now produces epics with a Parallelism Map section containing independent work streams, stream dependencies, suggested waves, file ownership boundaries, and a parallelism assessment. The wave-planning skill consumes this Parallelism Map to create actionable task batches.

This skill sits between brainstorming and team-executing-plans in the workflow:
```
brainstorming → sre-task-refinement → wave-planning → team-executing-plans
```

## Implementation

### Step 1: Study existing skill patterns
- Read skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md — the solo equivalent of wave-planning (creates tasks from epics)
- Read skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — understand the STOP checkpoint pattern
- Read skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md — understand SRE integration
- Note the standard skill structure: frontmatter, skill_overview, rigidity_level, quick_reference, when_to_use, the_process, examples, critical_rules, verification_checklist, integration, resources

### Step 2: Create the skill file
Create skills/wave-planning/SKILL.md with the standard skill structure.

**Frontmatter:**
```yaml
---
name: wave-planning
description: Use after brainstorming when Parallelism Map shows 3+ independent streams - creates waves of parallelizable tasks with beads dependencies for team execution
---
```

**Core Process (the_process section):**

1. **Load Epic and Parallelism Map**
   - `bd show <epic-id>` to load epic
   - Extract Parallelism Map section
   - Verify 3+ independent streams exist (error if not — should have taken solo path)

2. **Validate File Ownership Boundaries**
   - Check that no two streams in the same wave share exclusive files
   - Shared files in the "Shared (needs coordination)" column: either create a separate coordination task or sequence the streams into different waves
   - Flag any ambiguities for user resolution

3. **Create Wave 1 Tasks**
   - ONLY create Wave 1 tasks (not all waves — iterative, matching executing-plans philosophy)
   - For each independent stream in Wave 1, create a bd task:
     - Title: "Wave 1: [Stream Name]"
     - Design: Goal, file ownership boundaries, implementation steps, success criteria, anti-patterns from epic
     - Include teammate context: which files this task owns, which files it must NOT touch
   - Set beads dependencies: all Wave 1 tasks depend on epic (parent-child), no blocking deps between Wave 1 tasks (they're independent)

4. **Run SRE Refinement on Wave Batch**
   - Run hyperpowers:sre-task-refinement on each Wave 1 task
   - This strengthens criteria, adds edge cases, ensures each task is teammate-ready

5. **Present Wave Summary and STOP**
   - Show: wave composition, task list, file ownership matrix, dependency graph
   - STOP for user review before team-executing-plans spawns agents

**Key Design Decisions:**
- Only create one wave at a time (matches epic anti-pattern: NO creating full task trees upfront)
- Each wave task includes file ownership boundaries in its design (teammates need to know what NOT to touch)
- Wave tasks reference epic requirements and anti-patterns (teammates need the contract)

### Step 3: Add wave sizing heuristics
The skill should include guidance for wave sizing:
- Maximum tasks per wave: 4-5 (more becomes hard to coordinate)
- Minimum tasks per wave: 2 (below this, use solo execution)
- If a stream has high complexity + shared files: consider making it solo (its own wave)
- If all streams are low complexity with no shared files: can batch more

### Step 4: Add examples
Include 2 examples:
1. **Good example:** 5 streams, 2 waves (infra wave + parallel features wave)
2. **Bad example:** Developer creates all waves upfront instead of iteratively

### Step 5: Add integration section
Document the call chain:
- Called by: brainstorming (team path handoff)
- Calls: sre-task-refinement (for each wave task), team-executing-plans (handoff after wave approved)
- Uses: bd commands (create, dep add, show, ready)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] skills/wave-planning/SKILL.md exists with standard skill structure (frontmatter, overview, rigidity, quick_reference, when_to_use, the_process, examples, critical_rules, verification_checklist, integration, resources)
- [ ] Skill reads Parallelism Map from epic and validates 3+ independent streams
- [ ] Skill validates file ownership boundaries (no exclusive file conflicts within a wave)
- [ ] Skill creates ONLY Wave 1 tasks (not all waves — iterative creation)
- [ ] Each wave task includes: goal, file ownership (owns + must-not-touch), implementation steps, success criteria, anti-patterns from epic
- [ ] Skill runs SRE refinement on each wave task before STOP
- [ ] Skill presents wave summary with file ownership matrix and dependency graph
- [ ] Wave sizing heuristics documented (2-5 tasks per wave, complexity considerations)
- [ ] Two examples included (good wave planning + bad upfront wave creation)
- [ ] Integration section documents full call chain
- [ ] STOP checkpoint after wave presentation (mandatory before team execution begins)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO creating all waves upfront (only Wave 1 — subsequent waves created after Wave 1 results, matching epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO skipping SRE refinement on wave tasks (each task needs corner-case analysis)
- ❌ NO allowing exclusive file conflicts within a wave (two tasks claiming same exclusive file = merge conflict)
- ❌ NO skipping file ownership in task designs (teammates need to know their boundaries)
- ❌ NO skipping STOP checkpoint (user must approve wave before team execution)
- ❌ NO creating tasks without epic reference (each task must reference epic requirements and anti-patterns)

## Key Considerations (FROM SRE REVIEW OF TASK 1)

### Edge Case: Shared Files Between Streams
The Parallelism Map has a "Shared (needs coordination)" column. Wave-planning must handle this:
- Option A: Create a separate coordination task that runs BEFORE the streams needing the shared file (becomes a Wave 0/1 dependency)
- Option B: Sequence the conflicting streams into different waves (one edits shared file in Wave N, other uses it in Wave N+1)
- Present both options to user when shared files detected

### Edge Case: Streams with Different Complexity
Some streams may be low complexity (1 hour) while others are high (8 hours). Wave-planning should note this:
- Short tasks finish early, leaving agents idle
- Consider grouping low-complexity streams together or combining them
- Document this in wave sizing heuristics

### Reference: Epic Anti-Pattern
Epic bd-6t7 explicitly forbids: "NO creating full task trees upfront (waves are created iteratively based on learnings from previous waves)." The wave-planning skill must enforce this by creating ONLY the current wave's tasks.
close_reason: All 11 success criteria verified. Wave-planning skill created with standard structure (frontmatter, overview, rigidity, quick_reference, when_to_use, the_process, examples, critical_rules, verification_checklist, integration, resources). Reads Parallelism Map and validates 3+ streams. Validates file ownership boundaries with Option A/B conflict resolution. Creates ONLY current wave tasks (not all waves). Each task includes epic context, file ownership (owns + must-not-touch), implementation steps, success criteria, anti-patterns. Runs SRE refinement on every wave task. Presents wave summary with composition table, file ownership matrix, dependency graph. Wave sizing heuristics documented (2-5 tasks). Two examples included (correct planning + bad upfront creation). Integration section documents full call chain. Mandatory STOP checkpoint before team execution.

title: Task 4: vbc doc-only evidence taxonomy + epic-close gate + hook-bypass MUST + boundary reconciliation
description: vbc gains doc-only taxonomy (section + quick_ref row), reviewer-APPROVED epic-close check, canonical hook-bypass rule, executor-Boundaries carve-out
design:
## Goal
verification-before-completion gains a doc-only evidence taxonomy (new section + quick_reference row), an epic-close reviewer-APPROVED gate check, the canonical hook-bypass MUST, and the executor-boundary reconciliation of the Current Changes Assumption.

## Why
Every evidence class in vbc today is code-artifact evidence (tests, builds, bug repro), so the Iron Law is vacuously satisfiable in markdown repos (epic bd-x7z3 R4). The epic-close checklist omits the end-of-epic reviewer gate that finishing-a-development-branch now greps epic notes for, and still uses `bd dep tree`, childless for epics in bd 0.50.3 (R5). Hook-bypass (--no-verify, editing .git/hooks) is prohibited nowhere in the plugin — vbc is the settled canonical home (R6). The Current Changes Assumption's "Just fix the error directly" contradicts executor task Boundaries (R7). vbc is the plugin's verification spine; these four gaps let false completion claims through.

## Context
Read before starting:
- skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md in full (361 lines). Landmarks: quick_reference table 18-24, Gate Function 50-80, critical_rules 261-302 (Rules That Have No Exceptions start 265; Current Changes Assumption 294-301), verification_checklist 304-329 (epic-close block 322-328).
- skills/common-patterns/loop-interfaces.md:25-29 — the completion gate-state marker line `Verdict: APPROVED (end-of-epic reviewer, <date>)` cited by edit 3.
- Wording contract (epic Settled Decisions, shared verbatim with epic bd-go7m): "Fix in-boundary failures directly; if the fix requires edits outside your Boundaries, return NEEDS_HELP."

## Implementation
1. New section inside <the_process>, after "The Gate Function" (i.e., after line 79, before the closing </the_process> tag): heading "## Doc-Only Changes: The Evidence Taxonomy". Content — one framing sentence: markdown repos have no compiler or test suite for prose, so greps ARE the test suite and the Iron Law still applies; then a two-column table (Claim about a doc change | Evidence):
   - Old wording is gone | repo-wide grep for the old phrase returns zero, or only intended survivors — list them
   - New wording is in place | grep for the new phrase hits every intended site
   - Cross-references resolve | every cited file path/section/anchor exists (test -f the path; grep the heading)
   - Forbidden terms eliminated | sweep greps return zero across the stated scope
   - Executable surface touched (hooks/) | contract tests pass: ./hooks/test/contract-test.sh
   Close the section with: "Rereading the diff is not evidence for any row."
2. quick_reference table (18-24): add the row
   | **Doc change correct** | Grep evidence per the Doc-Only Evidence Taxonomy (old-gone, new-present, refs resolve) | Rereading the diff, "it's just markdown" |
3. Epic-close checklist (322-328): replace line 325 "- [ ] Ran `bd dep tree bd-1`" with "- [ ] Ran `bd list --parent bd-1`"; and insert before the "- [ ] THEN closed epic" line:
   - [ ] Epic bd notes contain `Verdict: APPROVED (end-of-epic reviewer, <date>)` (completion gate-state, `skills/common-patterns/loop-interfaces.md` — finishing-a-development-branch greps for it)
4. critical_rules "Rules That Have No Exceptions" (numbered rules 1-4 at 265-282): append rule 5 with the canonical text:
   5. **Never bypass a failing hook** → Never `git commit --no-verify`. Never edit `.git/hooks` to get past a failing check. Fix the failure, or surface it and stop — a bypassed hook is a verification you chose not to run.
   (This is the canonical home; agents/executor.md carries a one-line mirror owned by epic bd-go7m — do not edit executor.md.)
5. Current Changes Assumption (294-301): keep the heading and the never-pre-existing lines (296-299); replace line 300 "- Just fix the error directly" with these two lines:
   - Fix in-boundary failures directly; if the fix requires edits outside your Boundaries, return NEEDS_HELP.
   - (Contexts without task Boundaries — lead sessions, ad-hoc work — fix the failure directly.)
   The first line is verbatim-contracted with epic bd-go7m: character-for-character as written above.

## Verification
- `grep -n "no-verify" skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md` returns a hit inside critical_rules
- `grep -Fc "Fix in-boundary failures directly; if the fix requires edits outside your Boundaries, return NEEDS_HELP." skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md` returns exactly 1
- `grep -Fn "Verdict: APPROVED (end-of-epic reviewer" skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md` returns 1 hit inside the epic-close checklist
- `grep -c "dep tree" skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md` returns 0 (the non-contiguous form catches "bd list/dep tree" phrasings too — a Stage-2 finding: the resources Verification-patterns line evaded the contiguous grep)
- `grep -Fc "bd list --parent" skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md` returns 2 (epic-close checklist + resources Verification patterns)
- `grep -n "Doc-Only" skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md` shows both the section heading and the quick_reference row
- `test -f hooks/test/contract-test.sh` succeeds (path cited by the taxonomy exists)
- Pre-commit hooks passing

## Boundaries
Only skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md. Do not edit agents/executor.md (mirror owned by epic bd-go7m), loop-interfaces.md, finishing-a-development-branch, or anything in hooks/. Do not weaken the Iron Law or existing rules 1-4 — all edits are additive or the exact replacements specified above.

title: Task 3: Audit secondary files for old execution model references
description: Find and update all references to the old iterative execution model across skills, agents, commands, CLAUDE.md, and README.md.
design:
## Goal
Remove all references to the old execution model (iterative task creation, per-task SRE, project memory bridge, COMPLETE/ESCALATION/CONTEXT_LIMIT envelopes, propose-next-task) from files outside executor.md and executing-plans/SKILL.md.

## Why
The executor (bd-5hd) and executing-plans (bd-w59) have been rewritten, but other files still describe the old model. Users and Claude instances reading these files will get contradictory instructions about how execution works.

## Effort Estimate
15-20 minutes (grep, read, edit across ~6-8 files)

## Context
- Executor now returns DONE/BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP one-liners (not COMPLETE/ESCALATION/CONTEXT_LIMIT envelopes)
- Executing-plans now reads upfront task list, classifies simple/medium, dispatches with dynamic model, two-stage per-task review
- No project memory bridge in execution loop
- No per-task SRE refinement
- No "propose next task" flow — tasks planned upfront

## Implementation guidance
1. Grep for old-model terms across the entire repo (excluding executor.md and executing-plans/SKILL.md which are already done):
   ```bash
   grep -rn "CONTEXT_LIMIT\|ESCALATION\|propose.*next.*task\|project.memory\|memory.bridge\|idles.*out\|teammate" --include="*.md" . | grep -v "executor.md\|executing-plans/SKILL.md\|memory/"
   ```
2. Candidate files to check manually even if grep is clean:
   - skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md — workflow descriptions
   - skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md — Step 8 handoff description
   - skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md — references to execution model
   - agents/reviewer.md — may reference old executor output format
   - agents/code-reviewer.md — may reference old model
   - CLAUDE.md — Agent System and Core Workflows sections
   - README.md — if it describes execution model
   - commands/execute-plan.md — already updated by bd-w59, verify
3. For each file with old references:
   - Update to reflect new model (upfront tasks, DONE/BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP, dynamic model, two-stage review)
   - Paraphrase forbidden terms even in negation context (per bd-5hd learnings)
4. Final verification grep to confirm zero old-model terms remain

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO rewriting files that are in scope for later tasks (grand planner, writing-plans, SRE batch mode are separate tasks)
- ❌ NO changing the substantive behavior of any skill — only update descriptions of the execution model
- ❌ NO mentioning forbidden terms even to negate them — paraphrase instead

## Edge Cases

**File describes execution model but is scheduled for full rewrite in a later task:**
- Only update the execution model references, not the whole file
- Example: brainstorming Step 8 handoff should describe the new model, but the sizing gate is a separate task

**Grep finds term in a memory file:**
- Skip it — memory files are historical records, not instructions

**README.md has extensive execution model description:**
- Update it to match the new model, but keep the same level of detail — don't expand or shrink

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Zero matches for old-model terms across repo (excluding memory/ files): grep -rn "CONTEXT_LIMIT\|propose.*next.*task\|memory.bridge" --include="*.md" . | grep -v "memory/" returns 0
- [ ] CLAUDE.md Agent System section reflects new executor (84 lines, DONE/BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP) and new executing-plans (upfront tasks, dynamic model, two-stage review)
- [ ] CLAUDE.md Core Workflows section reflects new pipeline
- [ ] Brainstorming Step 8 handoff describes new execution model
- [ ] All candidate files checked (using-hyper, brainstorming, finishing-branch, reviewer, code-reviewer, CLAUDE.md, README.md)
- [ ] No behavioral changes to any skill — only description updates
close_reason: Secondary files audited. Zero old-model terms remain. CLAUDE.md, README.md, brainstorming Step 8, reviewer.md all updated. Commit 3dd2d42.

title: Task 6: executing-plans — sharpened review lenses, pre-dispatch HEAD, promotion honor
description: Stage 1 and Stage 2 get non-overlapping lenses (lead: epic coherence; reviewer: spec+quality); diffs run against a recorded pre-dispatch HEAD instead of HEAD~1; dispatch honors Executor: opus including auto-promotion on re-dispatch after failure.
design:
## Goal
skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md runs both per-task review stages with distinct lenses, diffs against a recorded pre-dispatch commit, and routes promoted tasks to Opus.

## Why
Stage 1 currently duplicates Stage 2's spec-match while nobody owns epic-coherence; git diff HEAD~1 silently shows only the last commit when an executor makes several (finding F7); and the ratified promotion mechanism needs its dispatch hook plus the auto-promote escalation rung.

## Context
File: skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md (~334 lines). Sites to read before editing:
- quick_reference table (~14-27): Stage 1 row says "git diff HEAD~1"; Stage 2 row says "Agent tool (Sonnet)".
- Step 3 dispatch block (~63-86): the Agent tool template with model: "sonnet".
- Step 4 DONE handling (~87-134): Stage 1 and Stage 2 definitions and the re-dispatch feedback templates.
- BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP handling (~136-173) and escalation thresholds (~155-160).
- critical_rules 3/4/6/7 (~274-291) and verification_checklist (~301-317).
Reference: skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md (Task 1) for the promotion flag definition.

## Implementation
1. Step 3: before dispatching, record the current commit ("git rev-parse HEAD" saved as the task's base SHA). Dispatch model rule: model is "sonnet" unless the task spec contains the line "Executor: opus" — then "opus" (cite skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md).
2. Stage 1 (lead, on every DONE): read "git diff <base-SHA>" and check ONLY what the lead can see and the reviewer cannot — violations of the epic's anti-patterns, watering-down of immutable requirements, contradictions with other tasks' completed work. Spec-match wording moves out of Stage 1.
3. Stage 2 (unchanged dispatch, sharpened statement): the fresh reviewer owns spec-match and code quality; keep the existing prompt, which already covers both.
4. Re-dispatch rungs: on the FIRST re-dispatch of a task after BLOCKED or Stage-2 CONCERNS, if the task is not already promoted, add "Executor: opus" to the re-dispatch prompt and note the promotion in bd. Existing consecutive-BLOCKED escalation thresholds stay as the rung above.
5. Sync quick_reference rows, critical_rules 3/4 wording, and verification_checklist items ("Stage 1: lead read the diff against the recorded base SHA for epic coherence").

## Additional Sites (ADDED BY SRE BATCH REVIEW)
6. Mainline references (epic requirement: pipeline stated identically in all six files — this file is one of the six; rg finds no claim that writing-plans creates the task tree). Note there are TWO model: "sonnet" dispatch blocks (~lines 70 and 102) — apply the item-1 model rule to both.
   - when_to_use ~line 30: "Use after writing-plans produces a complete task tree for an epic." becomes brainstorming as the tree producer.
   - Integration "Called by" ~line 323: "after writing-plans produces the task tree" becomes after brainstorming produces the task tree.
   - ~lines 32 and 51: "return to writing-plans" is still the correct routing (spec repair is writing-plans' post-rework role) but reword so no mainline implication remains, e.g. "route the spec-less tasks through writing-plans (the off-mainline spec repair utility)".

## Lead Addendum (from live execution evidence, 2026-07-06)
Item-4 refinement: the auto-promotion rung targets capability-class failures — BLOCKED returns and correctness/quality CONCERNS. For purely cosmetic or convention-level concerns (formatting, citation style, naming), the lead re-dispatches WITHOUT promotion at its discretion. Write the rule in the skill text with this distinction (two re-dispatches in this very epic were convention fixes where promotion would have been waste).

## Verification
- [ ] rg 'HEAD~1' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md returns zero
- [ ] rg 'rev-parse HEAD' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md returns 1 or more
- [ ] rg -c 'Executor: opus' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md returns 2 or more
- [ ] Stage 1 text mentions anti-patterns/requirements/cross-task coherence; spec-match named only in Stage 2
- [ ] rg 'writing-plans produces' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md returns zero (ADDED BY SRE BATCH REVIEW)
- [ ] rg 'return to writing-plans' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md returns zero (ADDED BY SRE BATCH REVIEW)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Boundaries
- Keep Stage 2 as a dispatched fresh reviewer (ratified decision — do not merge the stages)
- Do NOT change the completion/reviewer sections or the escalation protocol structure
- Broader model policy beyond the promotion flag is bd-ope's scope

title: Task 2: Move Pass 7 (Dynamic View Drift) to /ponder REVIEW + remove from /intuition + renumber 8/9/10 -> 7/8/9
description: Move Pass 7 logic to /ponder REVIEW (as a discrepancy-format section, not tension-format); remove Pass 7 from /intuition; renumber remaining passes 8/9/10 -> 7/8/9 including all inline cross-references.
design:
## Goal
Move Pass 7 (Dynamic View Drift) detection logic from skills/intuition/SKILL.md into skills/ponder/SKILL.md REVIEW mode as a new "### Dynamic View Drift" output section (discrepancy-format, NOT tension-format). Delete Pass 7 entirely from /intuition. Renumber Pass 8 -> 7, Pass 9 -> 8, Pass 10 -> 9 across ALL occurrences in /intuition (section headers AND every inline cross-reference). Update Critical Rule 5 from "Run ALL 10 passes" to "Run ALL 9 passes". Update Audit Outcome report template, Linked-pass section, Verification Checklist, Examples to use new numbers.

After this task: /intuition has 9 passes documented, no Pass 7 / Dynamic View Drift content; /ponder REVIEW outputs a Dynamic View Drift discrepancy section; verification grep checks confirm.

## This task does NOT touch
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md (later task)
- agents/ponder.md (read-only — verify whether Pass 7 logic codification needed there; this task may add a small note pointing to /ponder REVIEW, but full agent rewrite is later if needed)
- CLAUDE.md / README.md (later task)
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (final-push task)
- Step 0 / Prose Focus mechanism / freshness check / Mode references (all already fixed in bd-428)
- Anti-recommendation rules 1-4 (preserved verbatim)
- Resolution Protocol Step 4 5 options
- Architect Questions

## This task DOES touch
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md (Pass 7 deletion + renumber 8/9/10 -> 7/8/9 + Critical Rule 5 + report template + Linked-pass section + Verification Checklist + Examples)
- skills/ponder/SKILL.md (add Dynamic View Drift output section to REVIEW mode summary template; add brief logic guidance for the agent)

## Effort Estimate
8-10 hours (one focused session). Mostly mechanical renumbering plus a careful Pass 7 -> /ponder migration.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN in this task)
- ❌ NO partial renumbering — every inline "Pass 9" / "Pass 10" reference inside the file (in cascade/bypass section text, dispatch prompts, tension format templates, Linked Pass section, Verification Checklist, Examples, Audit Outcome) must be renumbered along with the section headers. Run grep before AND after editing.
- ❌ NO copying /intuition Pass 7's tension format into /ponder. /ponder REVIEW outputs a discrepancy list (see /ponder/SKILL.md:287-306 for existing format). Pass 7 in /ponder becomes a "### Dynamic View Drift" section listing view-vs-code MATCH/DISCREPANCY entries — NOT Pull 1 / Pull 2 / Structural observation.
- ❌ NO weakening "Run ALL N passes" RIGID rule (rule 5). Just update count from 10 to 9; preserve the "no skipping passes; insufficient evidence produces zero tensions" semantics.
- ❌ NO modifying anti-recommendation rules 1-4 (verbatim preservation required)
- ❌ NO modifying Resolution Protocol options (5 stay)
- ❌ NO touching the freshness check or Step 0 prose focus (already fixed in bd-428)
- ❌ NO changing the meaning of Pass 8 (Rate-of-Change) or Pass 9 (Workaround Cascade) or Pass 10 (Mechanism Bypass) — only their pass numbers change
- ❌ NO leaving inconsistent linked-pass references — when Pass 10 -> Pass 9 and Pass 9 -> Pass 8, the phrase "linked Pass 9 cascade" inside the new Pass 9 (was 10) section MUST become "linked Pass 8 cascade"
- ❌ NO partial commits (commit only when ALL verification grep commands pass)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

- Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md:300-333 (Pass 7 Dynamic View Drift content — what to extract for /ponder)
- Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md:334-630 (Passes 8/9/10 — note all inline pass-number references)
- Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md:622-626 (Audit Outcome template — pass-numbered count fields)
- Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md:721-734 (Linked Pass 9+10 Findings section)
- Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md Critical Rules, Verification Checklist, Examples (any pass-number references)
- Read skills/ponder/SKILL.md:97-104 (REVIEW mode dispatch input format)
- Read skills/ponder/SKILL.md:126-129 (REVIEW summary handling)
- Read skills/ponder/SKILL.md:287-306 (existing REVIEW output format example — model the new Dynamic View Drift section after this)
- Read agents/ponder.md (verify whether Pass 7 logic needs codification at agent level, or whether SKILL.md guidance suffices)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD — RED phase)

Save as /tmp/verify-bd-task2.sh, chmod +x, run pre-edit (must FAIL) then post-edit (must PASS):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e
F=skills/intuition/SKILL.md
P=skills/ponder/SKILL.md

echo "=== /intuition: Pass 7 must be GONE ==="
! grep -E '^### Pass 7: Dynamic View Drift' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: Pass 7 Dynamic View Drift section still in /intuition"; exit 1; }
! grep -F 'Dynamic View Drift' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: 'Dynamic View Drift' phrase still in /intuition"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /intuition: 9 passes, not 10 ==="
! grep -E '^### Pass 10' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: Pass 10 section still present"; exit 1; }
grep -E '^### Pass 9: Mechanism Bypass' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: Pass 9 should be Mechanism Bypass (was Pass 10)"; exit 1; }
grep -E '^### Pass 8: Workaround Cascade' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: Pass 8 should be Workaround Cascade (was Pass 9)"; exit 1; }
grep -E '^### Pass 7: Rate-of-Change' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: Pass 7 should be Rate-of-Change (was Pass 8)"; exit 1; }
grep -E 'Run ALL 9 passes' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: 'Run ALL 9 passes' rule missing"; exit 1; }
! grep -E 'Run ALL 10 passes' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: 'Run ALL 10 passes' rule still present"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /intuition: linked-pass refs use new numbers ==="
! grep -E 'Linked Pass 9\+10' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: 'Linked Pass 9+10' still references old numbers"; exit 1; }
grep -E 'Linked Pass 8\+9' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: 'Linked Pass 8+9' should be present"; exit 1; }
! grep -E 'Pass 9 cascade|Pass 9 found|from Pass 9|Pass 10 ' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: inline Pass 9/10 references in cascade text still present"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /ponder: Dynamic View Drift logic added to REVIEW ==="
grep -F 'Dynamic View Drift' "$P" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: 'Dynamic View Drift' section missing from /ponder REVIEW"; exit 1; }

echo "=== Anti-recommendation rules preserved verbatim ==="
grep -F 'NO recommendations' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: rule 1 missing"; exit 1; }
grep -F 'NO severity rankings' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: rule 2 missing"; exit 1; }
grep -F 'NO ordering or prioritization' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: rule 3 missing"; exit 1; }

echo "=== Line count: at least 30 more lines reduced from /intuition ==="
LINES=$(wc -l < "$F")
[ "$LINES" -le 1419 ] || { echo "FAIL: /intuition line count $LINES > 1419 (started 1449; Pass 7 removal expects -30)"; exit 1; }

echo "All checks passed. /intuition: $LINES lines."
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Section A: Pass 7 logic to /ponder REVIEW**

- [ ] Read /ponder REVIEW summary format (skills/ponder/SKILL.md:287-306). Note the existing structure: `### Model-vs-Code Accuracy`, `### Missing from Model`, `### Stale in Model`, `### Quality Issues`, `### Validation`, `### Recommendation`.
- [ ] Add a new section to the REVIEW summary template: `### Dynamic View Drift`. Insert it between `### Stale in Model` and `### Quality Issues`. Format:
  ```
  ### Dynamic View Drift
  - [view-name]: MATCH — documented flow matches actual code path
  - [view-name]: DISCREPANCY — documented flow shows X -> Y, code shows X -> Z -> Y
  - None — no dynamic views to check
  ```
- [ ] Add brief logic guidance for the agent in skills/ponder/SKILL.md REVIEW mode section (around the dispatch protocol) describing what the agent does for view drift:
  > "For each dynamic view in docs/arch/views/data-flows/, trace the equivalent path in the codebase using codebase-investigator. Compare documented flow to actual flow. Mark MATCH if equivalent, DISCREPANCY with brief explanation otherwise."
- [ ] Update the example REVIEW output (skills/ponder/SKILL.md:287-306) to include a sample `### Dynamic View Drift` section with at least one MATCH and one DISCREPANCY entry.
- [ ] Read agents/ponder.md. If REVIEW logic in the agent file does not currently mention dynamic views, add a short bullet: "REVIEW: also compares documented dynamic views (docs/arch/views/data-flows/) against actual code paths. Outputs `### Dynamic View Drift` section with view-by-view MATCH/DISCREPANCY entries." If agents/ponder.md already covers dynamic views or doesn't enumerate REVIEW steps, no change needed.

**Section B: Delete Pass 7 from /intuition**

- [ ] skills/intuition/SKILL.md:300-333 — delete the entire Pass 7 (Dynamic View Drift) section.
- [ ] Delete any preamble line at line ~298-299 introducing Pass 7 (look for "Pass 7:" or "Dynamic View Drift" in surrounding lines).

**Section C: Renumber Pass 8 -> 7, Pass 9 -> 8, Pass 10 -> 9**

This is the highest-risk part. Run `grep -nE '\bPass [0-9]+\b' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` BEFORE editing to enumerate ALL occurrences. Update each. Run again AFTER editing to confirm only Pass 1-9 references remain.

Section headers (must update):
- [ ] `### Pass 8: Rate-of-Change Mismatch` -> `### Pass 7: Rate-of-Change Mismatch`
- [ ] `### Pass 9: Workaround Cascade Detection` -> `### Pass 8: Workaround Cascade Detection`

- [ ] In Pass 9 (was Pass 10): every "Pass 10" -> "Pass 9", every "Pass 9" -> "Pass 8". Specifically:

## Success Criteria

**Verification grep checks (must all pass):**

**Content preservation checks (must all pass):**

- [ ] `grep -F 'NO severity rankings' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (rule 2 verbatim)

**Quantitative check:**

**Manual checks:**

- [ ] /ponder REVIEW summary template includes a `### Dynamic View Drift` section between Stale in Model and Quality Issues
- [ ] /ponder REVIEW example output (lines ~287-306) shows a sample Dynamic View Drift entry
- [ ] agents/ponder.md mentions dynamic view drift in its REVIEW description (or no change needed if existing description covers it)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Edge Cases & Failure Modes

**Pass 7 references in code outside /intuition or /ponder:**
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md may reference "Pass 7" or "10 passes" — those are out of scope for this task; updated in later tasks. Do NOT touch them.

**agents/ponder.md REVIEW description might not enumerate steps:**

- If agents/ponder.md does enumerate REVIEW steps in detail, add a one-line bullet for dynamic view drift.

**Cascade reference in linked-pass section that uses Pass 9 to mean both old and new pass:**

- Old Pass 10 (Mechanism Bypass) becomes new Pass 9.
- After renumber: "Pass 9 (Mechanism Bypass) is the linked pass that depends on Pass 8 (Workaround Cascade) output." NOT "Pass 9 (Mechanism Bypass) depends on Pass 9 cascade."
- This is the most likely place for partial-renumber bugs. Read the renumbered section in full after edit.

**Verification script line count check might fail if Pass 7 was already shorter than expected:**
- Pass 7 section was approximately lines 300-333 = ~33 lines. Plus a few report-template / linked-section lines deleted. Reasonable expected reduction is 30-50 lines.
- Set threshold at ≤1419 (1449 - 30) as conservative pass.

**Concurrent edits:**

title: Executor Refresh: Teammate-per-Task with Health Monitoring and Memory
description: Restructure the executor pattern from a long-running teammate that persists across all tasks in an epic to a fresh teammate spawned for each individual task. Executors currently die silently from context exhaustion (auto-compact is OFF), and the lead fails to notice or intervene. This epic implements teammate-per-task lifecycle, two-tier health monitoring (10+5 min), project memory for cross-task learnings, and updates all referencing skills/agents.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. Executor is spawned as a fresh teammate for EACH task (not one executor for entire epic)
2. Lead performs two-tier health monitoring: 10 min silence triggers status check, 5 min no response to check triggers assumed-dead
3. Executor writes project memory with key learnings before shutdown (cross-task knowledge bridge)
4. Next executor reads epic + project memory + completed task history on startup
5. Executor sends brief progress messages at natural TDD checkpoints (RED, GREEN, REFACTOR, COMMIT)
6. Lead follows recovery protocol on executor death: assess git/bd state, respawn with checkpoint context
7. All skills and agents referencing the executor/teammate pattern are updated consistently
8. Team is created once per epic (TeamCreate), executor spawned/shutdown within it per task
9. Lead never implements code directly (delegation principle preserved)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] executing-plans skill describes teammate-per-task lifecycle with spawn/shutdown per task
- [ ] executing-plans skill includes two-tier health monitoring protocol (10 min + 5 min)
- [ ] executing-plans skill includes recovery-from-failure protocol
- [ ] executing-plans skill includes memory read/write conventions
- [ ] executor agent describes single-task scope with startup (read memory) and shutdown (write memory) phases
- [ ] executor agent includes progress message protocol at TDD checkpoints
- [ ] executor agent includes context-exhaustion self-monitoring with proactive alert
- [ ] review-implementation skill updated for new executor lifecycle
- [ ] reviewer agent updated if it references executor lifecycle
- [ ] finishing-a-development-branch skill updated for new lifecycle
- [ ] brainstorming skill handoff section updated
- [ ] using-hyper skill references updated
- [ ] writing-plans skill references updated
- [ ] sre-task-refinement skill references updated
- [ ] managing-bd-tasks skill references updated (if applicable)
- [ ] dispatching-parallel-agents skill references updated (if applicable)
- [ ] analyzing-test-effectiveness skill references updated (if applicable)
- [ ] commands/execute-plan.md updated
- [ ] CLAUDE.md agent system documentation updated
- [ ] All updates are consistent — no skill describes the old long-running executor pattern

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO long-running executor across multiple tasks (root cause of context exhaustion — the entire point of this epic)
- ❌ NO silent executor periods — executor MUST send progress messages at TDD checkpoints (silence is the symptom that led to this redesign)
- ❌ NO lead ignoring absence of messages — lead MUST fire health check after 10 min silence (the lead brushing off silence is the core user complaint)
- ❌ NO killing an active executor based on elapsed time alone — health check distinguishes 'working but slow' from 'dead/hung' (user explicitly concerned about this)
- ❌ NO implementing code in lead context — delegation principle preserved even during recovery (lead respawns executor, does not implement)
- ❌ NO skipping memory writes before executor shutdown — memory is the cross-task knowledge bridge (replaces in-context accumulation)
- ❌ NO subagent pattern for executor — must remain teammate for bidirectional communication and future worktree compatibility (user explicitly chose this)
- ❌ NO watchdog/third agent — monitoring is lead responsibility, not delegated (keep architecture simple)

## Approach

Restructure the executor pattern from a long-running teammate that persists across all tasks in an epic to a fresh teammate spawned for each individual task. The team (TeamCreate) persists for the epic, but the executor within it is spawned, completes one task, writes learnings to project memory, and is shut down. A fresh executor is spawned for the next task with clean context.

The lead gains a two-tier health monitoring protocol: after 10 minutes of silence from the executor, the lead sends a status check message. If no response within 5 minutes, the executor is assumed dead and the lead initiates recovery (assess git/bd state, respawn with checkpoint). Progress messages from the executor at TDD checkpoints provide natural health signals that reset the timer.

Project memory replaces in-context accumulation for cross-task learnings. The executor writes discoveries, gotchas, and decisions to project memory before shutdown. The next executor reads these on startup alongside the epic and bd task history.

## Architecture

**Primary files (major changes):**
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — Lifecycle rewrite, health monitoring, memory integration, recovery
- agents/executor.md — Single-task scope, progress messages, memory read/write

**Secondary files (moderate updates):**
- skills/review-implementation/SKILL.md — Reviewer dispatch for new lifecycle
- agents/reviewer.md — Executor lifecycle references
- skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md — Executor cleanup references

**Tertiary files (minor reference updates):**
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md, skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md, skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md
- skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md, skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md
- skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md, skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md
- commands/execute-plan.md, CLAUDE.md

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Executors running across multiple tasks accumulate context until they hit the context window limit and die silently. With auto-compact disabled, this is guaranteed. The lead waits for messages that never come and often brushes off user concerns about executor health. The 1M context window made this worse by extending the time before death — longer silence before the lead notices.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — Current model spawns one executor for entire epic via TeamCreate + Agent tool with team_name. Executor sends structured messages (Task Completion, Escalation, Epic Completion). Lead waits for auto-delivered messages.
- agents/executor.md — Executor has rule 9 'if context exhaustion detected, send status message' but if context is exhausted, executor can't send anything. Self-monitoring is the only detection mechanism.
- executing-plans 'When stuck' section has manual recovery (check team, respawn) but requires lead to notice first.
- Executor uses memory: project in frontmatter (Claude Code built-in agent memory)

**External:**
- Claude Code Agent tool supports teammate pattern (team_name + name params) and subagent pattern (no team)
- SendMessage supports type: message and type: shutdown_request
- TaskGet/TaskOutput available for monitoring background tasks
- Memory system supports project type memories with frontmatter format

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Teammate per task with progress protocol ✓

**What it is:** Keep teammate pattern but spawn/shutdown executor for each task. Add health monitoring (10+5 min two-tier), progress messages at TDD checkpoints, and project memory for cross-task learnings.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed teammate lifecycle tools (TeamCreate, SendMessage, TeamDelete)
- Confirmed team can persist while executor is spawned/shutdown within it
- Memory system supports the project type needed for cross-task learnings

**Pros:**
- Structural fix: context exhaustion impossible (bounded lifecycle)
- Preserves bidirectional communication (escalations work)
- Future worktree compatibility maintained
- Natural health signals via progress messages
- Memory replaces in-context accumulation

**Cons:**
- Spawn overhead per task (acceptable trade-off)
- Memory management (cleanup when epic closes)

**Chosen because:** Solves the root cause (unbounded context growth) while preserving the benefits users value (escalations, worktree future).

#### 2. Subagent per task (simpler but limited) ❌

**What it is:** Drop teammate pattern entirely. Use Agent tool (subagent) for each task. Executor runs, returns result. No bidirectional communication.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler lifecycle — subagent returns or errors, no silent death possible.

**Investigation:**
- Subagent model eliminates SendMessage entirely
- Executor cannot escalate mid-task (must return early)
- No path to worktree isolation

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Loses bidirectional communication. User explicitly wants escalation capability and worktree compatibility.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Claude Code adds a mechanism for subagents to communicate with their parent mid-execution.

#### 3. Watchdog agent monitoring ❌

**What it is:** Separate lightweight background agent monitors executor health, alerts lead on death.

**Why we looked at this:** Automated monitoring removes lead responsibility for health checks.

**Investigation:**
- Would require three concurrent agents (lead + executor + watchdog)
- Watchdog itself could fail (who watches the watchdog?)
- Adds significant complexity

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-engineered. Lead can handle 10-min health checks without delegation. Three agents adds failure modes.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Epic scale grows to point where lead is overwhelmed managing multiple executors.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Executor lifecycle change (per-task spawn/shutdown)
- Health monitoring protocol (two-tier)
- Memory convention for cross-task learnings
- Progress message protocol
- Recovery from failure protocol
- Full audit of all referencing skills and agents
- CLAUDE.md documentation update

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Worktree integration (future work, this preserves compatibility)
- Multiple concurrent executors (future work)
- Auto-compact integration (user runs with auto-compact OFF)
- Hook-based health monitoring (hooks are event-driven, not time-based)

### Open Questions
- Should epic-scoped memories be auto-cleaned when epic closes, or kept for future reference?
- Exact format of progress messages — structured or free-form?
- Should .agents/ directory be updated in this epic or handled separately?

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Executor lifecycle model? | Fresh spawn per task | Major rewrite of executing-plans + executor agent |
| Memory for cross-task learnings? | Yes, project memory | Executor writes before shutdown, next executor reads on startup |
| Lead behavior while executor works? | Active monitoring with timeout | Two-tier health check (10 min + 5 min) |
| Scope of changes? | Full audit — all referencing skills | Every skill/agent mentioning executor gets updated |
| Keep teammate or switch to subagent? | Keep teammate | Bidirectional communication + worktree future preserved |
| Health check timing? | 10 min first check, 5 min second | 15 min max downtime before respawn |
| Escalation handling? | Keep escalation via SendMessage | Teammate pattern enables mid-task escalation |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Current Executor Lifecycle
**Question explored:** How does the current executor pattern work and where does it fail?
**Sources consulted:**
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — Full lifecycle analysis
- agents/executor.md — Executor rules and communication protocol

**Findings:**
- Executor self-monitors context exhaustion (rule 9) but can't send alert when context is actually exhausted
- Lead waits for auto-delivered messages with no timeout
- 'When stuck' recovery requires lead to manually notice and intervene
- No heartbeat, timeout, or proactive health check mechanism

**Conclusion:** Structural fix needed — bounded executor lifecycle eliminates context exhaustion entirely

#### Memory System Suitability
**Question explored:** Can the memory system replace in-context accumulation?
**Sources consulted:**
- CLAUDE.md memory instructions — types, conventions, lifecycle
- agents/executor.md frontmatter — memory: project (built-in)

**Findings:**
- Project memory type fits cross-task learnings perfectly
- Memory files persist across agent spawns
- Memory rules exclude code patterns derivable from reading code (keeps memories lean)
- Agent memory: project in frontmatter is built-in Claude Code feature (separate from file-based memory)

**Conclusion:** File-based project memory bridges cross-task knowledge gap

### Dead-End Paths

#### Subagent per task
**Why explored:** Simplest lifecycle — returns or errors, no silent death
**Investigation:** Loses SendMessage, escalation, worktree compatibility
**Why abandoned:** User explicitly requires bidirectional communication

#### Watchdog agent
**Why explored:** Automated monitoring without lead overhead
**Investigation:** Three agents, watchdog can fail, over-engineered
**Why abandoned:** Lead can handle 10-min checks; simpler is better

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Killing active slow executor' → Resolved: health check, not kill timer. Active response = alive.
- 'Spawn overhead per task' → Accepted trade-off for bounded context.
- 'Lost cross-task learnings' → Resolved: project memory system bridges spawns.

title: Rewrite executor agent: single-task scope with startup/shutdown phases and progress messages
description:
Update agents/executor.md to match the new per-task executor model.

## Goal
Single-task scope, read project memory on startup, send progress messages at TDD checkpoints, write project memory before shutdown (before sending completion report).

## Implementation

1. Rewrite Startup Protocol:
   - Add: read project memories matching epic-<id>-* on startup
   - Reframe scope: 'Your scope is the task assigned in the spawn prompt ONLY'
   - After reading epic + task, read memory files for prior learnings

2. Add Progress Message Protocol to Execution Loop:
   - After RED (failing test written): send [RED] checkpoint message
   - After GREEN (test passing): send [GREEN] checkpoint message
   - After REFACTOR (cleanup done): send [REFACTOR] checkpoint message
   - After COMMIT: send [COMMIT] checkpoint message
   - Messages are one-liners, not paragraphs

3. Rewrite Completion Protocol:
   - FIRST: write project memory (epic-<id>-task-<id>-learnings.md)
   - THEN: send Task Completion Report to lead
   - THEN: wait for shutdown_request
   - Memory is written BEFORE the report, so lead shutdown_request prompt is a confirmation, not the trigger

4. Update Rule 9 (context exhaustion):
   - Single-task scope makes exhaustion much less likely
   - Defense-in-depth: if context approaching limit, commit current work, write memory, send structured status to lead, wait for respawn
   - Remove language implying executor self-monitors across multiple tasks

5. Update Rules section:
   - Add rule for progress messages (mandatory at TDD checkpoints)
   - Add rule for memory write (before completion report, not after)
   - Update rule 9 as above

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Startup Protocol includes: read epic, read task, read project memory
- [ ] Progress messages documented at RED/GREEN/REFACTOR/COMMIT checkpoints
- [ ] Progress message format is brief (one-liner)
- [ ] Completion Protocol: memory written BEFORE completion report sent
- [ ] Rule 9 updated for single-task scope (defense-in-depth, not primary detection)
- [ ] New rules for progress messages and memory write added
- [ ] No references to long-running multi-task executor pattern remain

title: Post-rework /intuition audit: tension report (14 tensions) + Step-4 resolutions
description: Full tension report from the post-rework validation audit (2026-07-06, v3.18.0); Step 4 resolution decisions pending with the architect
design:
Full tension report from the dispatched /intuition analyst (Steps 0-3), verbatim.
Scope: post-rework validation audit at v3.18.0 (all seven rework epics merged).
Step 4 (resolution protocol) pending — architect decides per tension:
accept via ADR / resolve via ADR + ticket / brainstorm / investigate.

=== REPORT ===
Focus: post-rework validation audit of the skill-system rework (pipeline skills,
common-patterns single-sources, using-hyper routing, hooks).
Model: none — codebase-only audit. Modules analyzed: ~62. ADRs: none exist.

TENSIONS (14 = 12 standalone + 2 linked Pass 8+9):
T1 Brainstorming braids design partnership and pipeline artifact production
   (Pass 1). 41 commits/6mo — hottest skill; edited in all 7 rework epics.
   Split conversational design from artifact production, or keep one context?
T2 using-hyper bundles routing catalog + enforcement policy + session duties
   (Pass 1). 19-row table couples file to full skill inventory; injected whole
   every session. Bundle rebuilt deliberately in RW6a.
T3 Three verdict vocabularies (DONE/BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP · PASS/CONCERNS ·
   APPROVED/GAPS FOUND) defined in three location classes (Pass 2). code-
   reviewer's PASS/CONCERNS exists ONLY inside executing-plans' dispatch prompt
   — agents/code-reviewer.md contains zero verdict terms. Not covered by
   loop-interfaces.md single-source.
T4 Gap-fix spec creation split: executing-plans inline path (GAPS FOUND branch,
   no spec-templates citation) vs writing-plans repair path (Pass 2). The one
   spec producer outside the two-tier single-source.
T5 Gate-state blocks written at 5 sites for fresh-session reconstruction; 0
   skills have a read step at resume points (Pass 3). Write side documented,
   read side implicit (governor's memory).
T6 brainstorming->executing-plans handoff contract ("complete task tree")
   restated in both, defined in neither's shared source (Pass 3). 8+ co-changes.
T7 .agents/ Codex pack carries pre-rework semantics; CODEX.md declares it a
   supported entry point (Pass 4). Frozen since 2026-02; contradicts v3.12-3.18
   semantics; CODEX.md:24 references a path that does not exist here.
T8 CLAUDE.md (24 commits/6mo) + README (17) restate pipeline semantics and move
   at pipeline speed (Pass 4). Appeared in gap-fix commits of 4/7 epics.
T9 Common-patterns single-sources back-reference consumers' internal step
   numbers (3 of 10 files, 4 consumer skills) (Pass 5). Foundation depends on
   layer above; renumbering silently stales the single source. All accurate today.
T10 bd task design field: shared mutable record, 4 writers (brainstorm, writing-
   plans, SRE, lead auto-promotion), implicit schema, parsed control line
   (Executor: opus) living inside prose (Pass 6).
T11 Epic bd notes: 3 record types (gate-state, PLAN-IMPACT, promotion notes) in
   one append-only prose channel; identity by prefix; 0 read procedures (Pass 6).
T12 Rate-of-change mismatch 41:1 between pipeline skills (brainstorming 41,
   executing-plans 24, using-hyper 15) and practice skills (TDD 1, root-cause 0,
   testing-anti-patterns 0) in one flat namespace, one version, one publish unit
   (Pass 7; threshold 5:1). plugin.json changed 46x tracking the hot layer.
T13 (Pass 8+9 linked) Dual-plane enforcement: 5 rules exist as prose AND hooks;
   4/5 agree exactly; 1/5 DIVERGED — skill routing: using-hyper mandates 26
   skills/agents, skill-rules.json has 19 entries (8 missing: consider,
   preordain, portent, refactoring-diagnosis, refactoring-design, intuition,

   asymmetry. Plus 3 hooks enforce rules stated in no prose. Fork point:
   hooks.json registration = second definition site.
T14 (Pass 8+9 linked) Executor guard cluster: 6 compensations on one interface
   (negative-space format rules, first-word parse, auto-promotion rung, BLOCKED
   thresholds, mandatory two-stage review, output gate). No codebase-internal
   parallel path — compensated behavior is external runtime drift, live-
   confirmed (bd-0he); executor is the only agent whose return is parsed for
   control flow.

DRIFT: no ADRs exist to drift from. Non-ADR declared-vs-observed divergences
reported as T5, T7, T13.
ARCHITECT QUESTIONS: (1) Meadows leverage: smallest structural change, largest
behavioral shift? (2) Fixes-that-fail: which obvious fix creates a new tension?
(3) Alexander: which boundaries feel imposed rather than discovered?
(4) Rao: which parts exist for maintainer legibility rather than users?

(Full verbatim analyst output retained in session transcript; this record is the

title: Task 5: analyzing-test-effectiveness body rewrite — orchestrator
description: Rewrite ATE skill head + process body as a 5-step orchestrator dispatching the analyst agent; two-tier templates; canonical batch SRE block; one numbering scheme
design:
Classification: medium

## Goal
skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md from the frontmatter through `</the_process>` is a 5-step orchestrator: scope → dispatch analyst agent → present findings → create bd epic+tasks (two-tier templates) → batch SRE review; methodology detail lives only in the agent.

## Why
R3+R4: the skill duplicates ~400 lines of the agent's methodology, says per-task SRE in its head (L3, L7, L39), and mixes three numbering schemes; its four generated task templates lack Why/Boundaries. The orchestrator restructure resolves all of this by construction and keeps verbose test-suite analysis out of the lead's context. Blocked by Task 4 (the dispatch block cites the agent's return contract).

## Context
Current structure (HEAD e4b39ee): frontmatter L1-4, overview L6-10, rigidity L12-14 ("5-phase"), quick-ref L16-46 (8 phases), when_to_use L48-61, the_process L63 onward with body headers Phase 1..7 + 4b (L70, L98, L116, L333, L365, L441, L502, L523) and legacy "Step 5.1..5.5" labels inside Phase 7 (L527, L556, L657, L680, L693). Templates: epic L529-553 (own format — keep), four task templates L558-585, L588-618, L620-654, L693-729 (non-conforming — rewrite). Near-canonical SRE block already at L657-677. Note: `</the_process>` closes at L828 — the `## Output Format` block at L734-827 (including the per-task `## SRE Task Refinement Status` checklist at L815-819) is INSIDE the body and belongs to this task, not Task 6. Canon to cite, never restate: agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md (methodology owner + Output Format return contract from Task 4), skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md (two-tier bodies), skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md (classification bands), skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md Step 7 L342-370 (canonical batch SRE dispatch elements).

## Implementation
1. Frontmatter description (L3): rewrite to reflect orchestration — dispatches the test-effectiveness-analyst agent for the analysis, creates bd epic with improvement tasks, runs batch SRE refinement against the full tree.
2. Overview (L6-10): orchestration summary; keep the skeptical-mindset framing but attribute the methodology to the agent ("the analyst agent applies ...").
3. Rigidity (L12-14): one sentence naming the 5-step orchestration as the rigid spine; methodology rigidity lives in the agent.
4. Quick-ref (L16-46): new 5-step table (1 Scope, 2 Dispatch Analyst, 3 Present Findings, 4 Create bd Epic+Tasks, 5 Batch SRE Review + handoff). Replace the restated Core Questions / criteria with a pointer to the agent file. Drop the mutation-testing command list (the agent's own section covers it — cite it).
5. Replace the_process Phases 1-7/4b with the 5 steps:
   - Step 1 Scope: confirm target suites/dirs (AskUserQuestion when ambiguous); announce the skill.
   - Step 2 Dispatch Analyst: blocking Agent-tool dispatch block in the house style (see brainstorming L348-362 for shape): subagent_type "hyperpowers:test-effectiveness-analyst", prompt states the scope and requires the full report per the agent's Output Format (Return Contract). No model override — the agent frontmatter pin governs. One line: the agent file owns the methodology; this skill never restates RED/YELLOW/GREEN criteria.
   - Step 3 Present Findings: relay executive summary + counts; agree with the user what to act on (remove RED / strengthen YELLOW / add corner cases).
   - Step 4 Create bd Epic + Tasks: keep the epic template (L529-553 format is epic-level, not governed by spec-templates). Rewrite the FOUR task templates to the two-tier bodies from spec-templates.md — every template carries ## Why; medium templates carry ## Boundaries; each template states its classification (remove-tautological: simple; strengthen-weak: medium; add-corner-cases: medium; validation: simple). Keep parent-child linking + dependency commands (current Step 5.4 content).
   - Step 5 Batch SRE Review: upgrade the existing L657-677 block with the canonical elements it lacks: the "Inputs: bd show <epic-id>, then bd show each child task" line, "preserve existing sections", the APPROVE / NEEDS REVISION / REJECT verdict enum, the epic-coverage table + per-task one-liners in the Return clause, and the closing "SRE refinement runs once against the full task tree — not per-task" sentence. Then hand off to executing-plans.
6. Body trailing block (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW): the `## Output Format` block (L734-827) sits inside `<the_process>` and is this task's territory. Align it to the orchestrator: rewrite the `## SRE Task Refinement Status` checklist (L815-819) from per-task phrasing ("All tasks reviewed with hyperpowers:sre-task-refinement" / "Category 8 ... applied to each task") to batch phrasing (one batch SRE dispatch against the full task tree; verdict recorded), and keep the rest of the report skeleton consistent with the new Step 4/Step 5 outputs.
7. One numbering scheme: "Step 1..Step 5" in every touched site (rigidity, quick-ref, body headers). No Phase labels, no Step 5.x labels anywhere in the rewritten region. (Grep guard: never end a sentence with the bare string "Step 5." — the final-sweep grep `Step 5\.` would false-positive; write "Step 5 (Batch SRE Review)." or rephrase.)

## Tests
Omit — pure documentation task.

## Verification
- `sed -n '1,60p' skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md` → description/overview/rigidity/quick-ref all say batch + dispatch, all use the Step 1-5 scheme
- `grep -in "on each task\|on all tasks" skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md` → hits only in the tail (after </the_process>) — those are Task 6's territory
- `awk '/<the_process>/,/<\/the_process>/' skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md | grep -n "Phase \|Step 5\."` → no output
- `grep -n "subagent_type: \"hyperpowers:test-effectiveness-analyst\"" skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md` → 1 hit
- `awk '/<the_process>/,/<\/the_process>/' skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md | grep -c "## Why"` → ≥4 (one per task template)
- `awk '/<the_process>/,/<\/the_process>/' skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md | grep -in "all tasks reviewed with\|applied to each task"` → no output (body's SRE status checklist reads batch, not per-task)
- The Step 5 block contains: Inputs line, preserve existing sections, APPROVE / NEEDS REVISION / REJECT, epic-coverage table, once-against-the-full-tree sentence

## Boundaries
Do not touch anything after `</the_process>` (worked example, rationalizations, red flags, checklist, integration — Task 6 aligns those to your output). Do not edit agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md or commands/analyze-tests.md. Do not restate spec-templates.md/pipeline-constants.md content — cite. Epic template format stays as-is.

title: Task 2: Wire Intuition to Ponder — Step 0 freshness check, Step 3 architect questions, Step 4 bootstrap delegation
design:
## Goal
Update skills/intuition/SKILL.md with three changes that integrate Intuition with the new Ponder system, plus update verification_checklist and integration section references.

## Implementation Checklist

1. Step 0 — Add model freshness check (insert after Mode 1 ADR loading, before Mode 2 section ~line 137)
   - When Mode 1 (model exists): check .c4 file age vs recent code commits
   - Use freshness heuristic: count code-touching commits since last .c4 modification
   - If >10 commits since last .c4 update: suggest "/ponder review" to check model accuracy
   - Suggestion only, not blocking — audit proceeds regardless
   - When Mode 2 (no model): skip freshness check entirely

2. Step 3 — Add architect questions closing section (insert after tension report ~line 723, before Step 4 header line 727)
   - Section title: "Architect Questions"
   - 4 questions: Meadows leverage, Meadows fixes-that-fail, Alexander wholeness, Rao legibility
   - Conditional: Omit when zero tensions found
   - Anti-pattern compliance: Questions ONLY, no should/must/recommend

3. Step 4 — Replace Model Bootstrapping with ponder dispatch (replace lines 800-848)
   - Remove inline .c4 creation templates
   - Replace with: dispatch ponder subagent in bootstrap mode
   - Construct structured input from tension evidence
   - Preserve conditional gate: no arch/*.c4 + Resolve decision

4. Update verification_checklist — change inline bootstrapping references to ponder dispatch

5. Update integration section — add ponder to called agents, update artifacts, update call chain

6. Check examples — verify no examples reference inline .c4 creation

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Step 0 has model freshness check (Mode 1 only, >10 commits threshold, suggests /ponder review)
- [ ] Step 3 has 4 architect questions after tensions, before Step 4
- [ ] Architect questions omitted when zero tensions found
- [ ] Architect questions contain NO recommendation language
- [ ] Step 4 Model Bootstrapping replaced with ponder subagent dispatch
- [ ] No inline .c4 creation remains in Intuition
- [ ] verification_checklist references ponder for bootstrapping
- [ ] integration section lists ponder as called agent

## Anti-patterns
- NO recommendation language in architect questions
- NO inline .c4 creation remaining
- NO blocking behavior from freshness check

title: Rework: Scaffolding consolidation
description: Rebuild using-hyper around a routing table plus hard gates; single-source the duplicated templates (two-tier specs, Impact Check, question format, classification, ceiling) in common-patterns. Structure only - RW6b does the content re-testing.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- using-hyper rebuilt around: (1) a routing table mapping task shape to skill chain — the currently missing catalog; (2) the hard meta-gates (check for skills, load via Skill tool, announce, follow) at full strength; (3) sharply reduced bulk — the three long negative worked examples and duplicated rationalization lists pruned. Hard rules stay hard; this is signal-density restructuring, not softening.
- Session-start injection carries the routing table plus gates, not essays.
- Two-tier spec templates single-sourced in common-patterns; brainstorming, writing-plans, and SRE reference one definition; field-name drift reconciled (Implementation vs Implementation guidance).
- Architecture Impact Check questions single-sourced; brainstorming and preordain reference one definition.
- Question-format guidance single-sourced (the form the RW3 epic settles).
- Classification guide and task ceiling single-sourced.
- bd create templates include --description alongside --design (fixes the warning every skill-driven create currently emits).
- plugin.json version bumped before final push.

## Boundaries
In scope: using-hyper SKILL.md; common-patterns additions; reference edits across skills; hooks/session-start.sh if injected content changes; skill-rules.json using-hyper entry cleanup.
Out of scope: guardrail content re-testing (RW6b epic); pipeline semantics (settled by the RW1 and RW3 epics — this epic consolidates, it does not redesign).

## Dependencies
Depends on: RW1 and RW3 epics (consolidates their settled shapes; the routing table must reflect the final pipeline).

## Context for Brainstorming
Finding F4 in REWORK_PRINCIPLES.md. Evidence: using-hyper lines 143-291 are worked examples; no routing table exists anywhere in the plugin; hooks/session-start.sh injects the entire 400-line file into every session. Standing user rule: MUST/REQUIRED language is load-bearing — restructure for signal density, never soften.
notes:
Breadcrumbs accumulated for this epic: (1) commands/preordain.md embeds gate values — fold into citation sweep; carry the strict citation-only rule through consolidation. (2) Five skills still carry unconditional TodoWrite mentions (analyzing-test-effectiveness, common-rationalizations, dispatching-parallel-agents, review-implementation, + check writing-plans loop mechanics) — align with the tracker-aware rule during single-sourcing. (3) Q6 prosthetic proposal from RW4: a pre-push hook verifying plugin.json version bumped when skills/agents/commands/hooks/CLAUDE.md/README changed — implement here where hooks are in scope (user visibility: hooks change system state). (4) Q6 prosthetic: epics-gone-quiet (in_progress with no commits N days) surfaced via session-start guidance. (5) RW2 reviewer suggestion: normalize Integration-section precision repo-wide (subagent hops named consistently).

DEPENDS ON
  → ◐ bd-uyc: (EPIC) Rework: Brainstorm partner mode + plan-doc ingestion ● P1
  → ✓ bd-vv8: Rework: Pipeline coherence ● P1

BLOCKS
  ← ○ bd-3j3: (EPIC) Rework: Guardrail re-baseline ● P1
Provenance (retrofit per RW3 gap fix): Source REWORK_PRINCIPLES.md, untracked working doc, 2026-07-06 — unversioned.
GATE STATE (2026-07-06, macro lead): RW6a STARTING, lead-direct per D2-AMENDED.
Where we are: decomposition posted, specs landing in bd next, then execution on epic/bd-die.
Decided: 8-task decomposition below (async veto open — object in any window, tasks adjust).
Needs you: nothing blocking, but ONE item deserves your eyes before it executes:

*** SYSTEM-STATE CHANGE AHEAD — T7 implements the version-bump guard HOOK ***
(breadcrumb 3, Q6 prosthetic proposed by RW4). A new PreToolUse hook on Bash that
fires only on git-push commands, only in repos with .claude-plugin/plugin.json at
root. If the outgoing range touches skills/agents/commands/hooks/CLAUDE.md/README
but plugin.json's version line is unchanged, the push is DENIED with a bump
reminder. Fail-open on every error path (a hook bug can never block work). Wired
in hooks/hooks.json, documented in HOOKS.md, tested with crafted stdin JSON before
commit. Sequenced second-to-last (T7 of 8) to maximize this veto window. Veto by
replying in any window or editing these notes before T7 executes.

Decomposition:
T1 spec-templates single-source — new common-patterns/spec-templates.md (two-tier
   template bodies + classification examples; canonical field name: Implementation);
   brainstorming 6c, writing-plans 2d/examples, sre-task-refinement checklist cite it;
   Implementation-guidance field drift reconciled repo-wide.
T2 impact-check + question-format single-source — new architecture-impact-check.md
   (5 questions, recording, routing) + question-format.md (RW3-settled AskUserQuestion
   form); brainstorming Step 4 and preordain Step 4 cite; preordain Step 1's stale
   lettered/Priority template (predates AskUserQuestion) replaced with the citation.
T3 citation + bd-create sweep — commands/preordain.md embedded gate values -> citation
   (breadcrumb 1); every bd create template carrying --design gains --description
   (kills the standing bd warning); bd-commands.md updated.
T4 TodoWrite tracker-aware alignment (breadcrumb 2) — review-implementation,
   analyzing-test-effectiveness, dispatching-parallel-agents, common-rationalizations,
   writing-plans loop mechanics, writing-skills/persuasion-principles.md (3 mechanical
   sites only; guardrail content re-testing stays RW6b).
T5 Integration-precision normalization (breadcrumb 5) — exact tool names everywhere
   (Agent tool, TaskOutput); stale Task-tool/AgentOutput refs killed; subagent hops in
   Integration sections named with dispatch mode.
T6 using-hyper rebuild — routing table (task shape -> skill chain, the missing catalog)
   + hard meta-gates at FULL strength; three worked examples and the duplicated
   rationalization list pruned (cites common-rationalizations.md); epics-gone-quiet
   session-start guidance (breadcrumb 4); skill-rules.json using-hyper entry removed
   (redundant — session-start.sh injects the whole skill); ~396 -> ~150 lines.
   Hard rules stay hard: signal-density restructuring, never softening.
T7 version-bump guard hook (see *** above).
T8 gated sweep — every T1-T7 gate TRANSCRIBED EXACTLY from its spec (bd-d38 ledger
   lesson: never re-derive thresholds) + plugin.json 3.16.0 -> 3.17.0.

Order: T1->T2->T3->T4->T5->T6->T7->T8, per-task commits and closes. One subagent
total: end-of-epic hyperpowers:reviewer, then gap fixes, merge, push.
Deferred here, noted for RW6b: writing-skills persuasion-principles content itself.
GATE STATE (macro lead): RW6a COMPLETE. All 8 tasks + gap-fix task closed.
Reviewer verdict: GAPS FOUND (2 critical — both in the new version-bump-guard
hook, regex-over-raw-command-text false-denies; 2 important — using-hyper
citation mismatch, stale Task-tool refs in hooks/ and root docs, outside T5's
grep scope). All fixed: hook rewritten to structural parsing (shlex tokens,
push-as-subcommand, literal branch/refspec equality), adversarial suite
expanded 6 -> 13 scenarios all passing; Skill Routing Shortcuts added to
common-rationalizations.md (single source for the pruned using-hyper excuses).
Ledger notes for RW6b: (a) reviewer catch-rate on lead-direct holds at 6/6
epics — this time a within-file logic bug in NEW code (the hook), not just
cross-file consistency: creative/code artifacts earn deeper review than doc
consolidation. (b) grep-scope lesson: T5's gates covered skills/ agents/
commands/ but the same stale names lived in hooks/ and root docs — scope
sweep greps to the whole repo unless there is a reason not to.
Merging epic/bd-die to main at v3.17.0.
close_reason: RW6a complete at v3.17.0: 8 tasks + gap-fix, lead-direct per D2-AMENDED.
