title: RW6a-T1: spec-templates single-source
description: Create common-patterns/spec-templates.md; brainstorming, writing-plans, SRE cite it; reconcile Implementation field drift
design:
## Goal
Two-tier task spec templates live in exactly one file — skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md — and brainstorming, writing-plans, and sre-task-refinement cite it. Field-name drift (Implementation vs Implementation guidance) reconciled to canonical `## Implementation`.

## Why
Epic bd-die requirement: templates duplicated across brainstorming SKILL.md (lines 338-402) and writing-plans SKILL.md (lines 129-192) with visible drift (placeholder text differs; writing-plans says "Implementation guidance", brainstorming and pipeline-constants say "Implementation"). Drift regenerates unless single-sourced.

## Context
- brainstorming/SKILL.md 338-402: simple + medium templates inside bd-create wrappers, plus classification guide at 398-401.
- writing-plans/SKILL.md 129-192: pure-markdown simple + medium templates, richer placeholders, TDD sub-steps, spec-depth rule citation. Also "Implementation guidance" at lines 166 and 307 (example section).
- pipeline-constants.md 25-41 already holds time bands, field lists, spec-depth rule — spec-templates.md holds template BODIES and cites pipeline-constants for bands/ceiling; do not duplicate the constants.
- sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md 97: simple-tier field list.

## Implementation
1. Create skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md: merged canonical templates (writing-plans' placeholder richness + TDD sub-steps; canonical field `## Implementation`), classification examples (from brainstorming 398-401), Why-required rule, citation to pipeline-constants.md for bands/ceiling/spec-depth.
2. brainstorming Step 6c: keep bd-create wrappers + parent-child dep mechanics; replace inline template bodies with citation to spec-templates.md; wrapper gains --description flag; classification guide trimmed to citation.
3. writing-plans 2d: replace inline template bodies with citation; rename all "Implementation guidance" to "Implementation" including the example at ~307.
4. sre-task-refinement 97: field list cites spec-templates.md.

## Verification
- G1: grep -rn "Implementation guidance" skills/ agents/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md | wc -l  -> expect 0
- G2: grep -c "^## Goal" skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md  -> expect 2
- G3: grep -l "spec-templates.md" skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md | wc -l  -> expect 3
- G4: grep -rln "One sentence: what changes" skills/ | tr -d '\n'  -> expect exactly skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md

## Boundaries
Template CONTENT semantics unchanged (RW1/RW3 settled them); no changes to pipeline-constants.md values; SRE checklist logic untouched beyond the citation.
close_reason: All 4 gates pass exactly as spec'd: G1=0 (field drift gone), G2=2, G3=3, G4=canonical file only. Committed on epic/bd-die.

title: RW6a-T2: impact-check + question-format single-source
description: Create architecture-impact-check.md and question-format.md in common-patterns; brainstorming + preordain cite them; kill preordain's stale lettered/Priority question template
design:
## Goal
The 5 Architecture Impact Check questions and the AskUserQuestion question-format guidance each live in exactly one common-patterns file, cited by brainstorming and preordain.

## Why
Epic bd-die requirement. The 5 questions are duplicated verbatim (brainstorming 120-134, preordain 144-156). Worse: preordain Step 1 (lines 60-72) still carries the OLD lettered A/B/C + "Priority: CRITICAL | IMPORTANT | NICE_TO_HAVE" print-template plus "1-5 questions maximum" — the exact form RW3 removed from brainstorming (settled form: AskUserQuestion-native, max 4/round). Live contradiction between skills.

## Context
- Settled question form = brainstorming SKILL.md 61-66: AskUserQuestion always (never print-and-wait); at most 4 questions/round, multiple choice preferred; recommended option FIRST labeled "(Recommended)" with evidence in description; one critical/blocking at a time, group lesser; stop once design space clear (rest become Open Questions); expired box is not an answer — re-ask in durable prose, hold at gate (cite loop-interfaces.md).
- Impact Check canonical content = the 5 questions + record YES/NO + routing (0 YES -> proceed; 1+ -> offer /intuition with prose focus; architect decides; record routing decision). Scope line differs by caller (designed solution vs entire initiative) — callers keep their scope sentence.

## Implementation
1. Create skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md (questions, recording, routing; note both call sites and their scopes).
2. Create skills/common-patterns/question-format.md (settled form above, including the phrase "At most 4 questions per round").
3. brainstorming Step 4: replace question list + routing with citation (keep scope sentence + epic-template pointer). Step 1 Questioning block: replace bullets with citation.
4. preordain Step 4: same treatment. Step 1: DELETE the lettered/Priority template block and "1-5 questions" guideline; cite question-format.md.
5. Repo-wide check: no other file embeds the 5 questions or the lettered template.

## Verification
- G1: grep -rn "NICE_TO_HAVE" skills/ commands/ agents/ | wc -l  -> expect 0
- G2: grep -rln "Creates a new component/module" skills/ commands/ | tr -d '\n'  -> expect exactly skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md
- G3: grep -l "architecture-impact-check.md" skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md skills/preordain/SKILL.md | wc -l  -> expect 2
- G4: grep -l "question-format.md" skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md skills/preordain/SKILL.md | wc -l  -> expect 2
- G5: grep -c "At most 4 questions per round" skills/common-patterns/question-format.md  -> expect 1

## Boundaries
Question-format SEMANTICS are RW3's settled form — transcribe, do not redesign. Impact Check questions verbatim — RW6b owns content re-testing. Portent/consider/intuition one-line "use AskUserQuestion" rules stay (they are rules, not duplicated format guidance); add citations there only if they carry format detail.
close_reason: All 5 gates pass as spec'd. G2 initially caught two residual embeddings (brainstorming epic template + examples.md recording forms) — converted to compact labeled forms citing the canonical file.

title: RW6a-T3: citation + bd-create description sweep
description: commands/preordain.md gate values become citations; every bd create template with --design gains --description; bd-commands.md updated
design:
## Goal
No command/skill file embeds sizing-gate values (citation to pipeline-constants.md instead), and every bd create example that shows --design also shows --description, killing the warning every skill-driven create emits.

## Why
Epic bd-die requirement + breadcrumb 1: commands/preordain.md embeds "~10 tasks or spans 3+ distinct components" — re-derivable drift the strict citation-only rule exists to prevent. And bd warns on create without --description; skills teach the warning-producing form.

## Context
bd create sites lacking --description (grep-verified 2026-07-06):
- skills/common-patterns/bd-commands.md (epic/feature/task examples, ~lines 30-50)
- skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md 412, 419
- skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md 530, 560, 589, 621, 692
- skills/preordain/SKILL.md 179
- skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md 59, 68, 78; resources/task-naming-guide.md 228
- skills/refactoring-safely/SKILL.md 72; resources/example-session.md 15
- skills/fixing-bugs/SKILL.md 69 (also table row at 20)
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md 696
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md 6c wrappers (T1 may have already added --description there; verify)
Elided examples (bd create "X" ... # bd-20) stay elided — only templates showing real flags gain --description.

## Implementation
1. commands/preordain.md: replace embedded threshold values with "the sizing-gate thresholds in skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md".
2. Add --description "[one-line summary]" to every bd create template listed above (placed before --design).
3. bd-commands.md: add --description to examples and a one-line note that description is the list-view summary, design the full spec.

## Verification
- G1: grep -n "10 tasks" commands/preordain.md | wc -l  -> expect 0
- G2: grep -n "pipeline-constants" commands/preordain.md | wc -l  -> expect >=1
- G3 (pairing check, run from repo root):
python3 - <<'PY'
import re,glob
bad=[]
for f in glob.glob('skills/**/*.md',recursive=True)+glob.glob('commands/*.md')+glob.glob('agents/*.md'):
    lines=open(f).read().split('\n')
    for i,l in enumerate(lines):
        if 'bd create' in l and '...' not in l:
            win='\n'.join(lines[i:i+10])
            if '--design' in win and '--description' not in win: bad.append(f'{f}:{i+1}')
print(bad if bad else 'PAIRING-OK')
PY
  -> expect PAIRING-OK
- G4: grep -n -- "--description" skills/common-patterns/bd-commands.md | wc -l  -> expect >=3

## Boundaries
Do not change bd command SEMANTICS or add flags beyond --description; elided examples untouched; no changes to pipeline-constants values.
close_reason: All 4 gates pass. G3 pairing checker caught 3 extra sites in brainstorming (SKILL epic template + 2 examples). Adjacent fix beyond spec, noted: bd edit -> bd update at 14 sites (bd edit is the blocking-$EDITOR form bd prime warns against); root-cause line in bd-commands.md that taught against --description reworded.

title: RW6a-T6: using-hyper rebuild — routing table + hard gates
description: Rebuild using-hyper around a routing table and hard meta-gates; prune worked examples; epics-gone-quiet line; remove skill-rules.json entry. Signal-density restructuring — NEVER softening
design:
## Goal
using-hyper/SKILL.md rebuilt: (1) routing table mapping task shape -> skill chain (the missing catalog); (2) hard meta-gates at FULL strength (1%-rule, Skill-tool load, announce, follow, tracker rule); (3) ~396 -> ~150 lines by pruning the three worked examples and the duplicated rationalization list (cite common-patterns/common-rationalizations.md). Session-start injection (whole-file cat) therefore carries table + gates, not essays. skill-rules.json using-hyper entry removed.

## Why
Epic bd-die core requirement (F4): 400 lines injected into EVERY session, mostly negative worked examples; the one thing a strong model would use — a routing catalog — does not exist anywhere in the plugin.

## Context
- Current file: 396 lines. Keep-at-full-strength inventory: EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT block (lines 6-12 incl. "1% chance" rule); mandatory-first-response checklist; Skill-tool-not-memory rule; announce rule; tracker rule (line 31 phrasing conforms to tracker-aware); instructions-describe-WHAT-not-HOW rule; rigidity split (rigid vs flexible skills).
- Prune inventory: examples block lines 146-288 (three worked examples); rationalizations 299-311 (duplicates common-patterns/common-rationalizations.md — cite); resources block; verbose why-paragraphs.
- Routing table rows (task shape -> chain), covering at minimum: explore idea -> consider; new feature/create -> brainstorming -> executing-plans -> review-implementation -> finishing-a-development-branch; large initiative -> preordain -> brainstorming per leaf; bug -> debugging-with-tools -> fixing-bugs (TDD inside); refactor -> refactoring-diagnosis -> refactoring-design -> refactoring-safely; implement/code -> test-driven-development + testing-anti-patterns; run tests/commits -> test-runner agent; claim done -> verification-before-completion; spec missing/broken -> writing-plans; architecture unease -> intuition; architecture model ops -> ponder; phase docs (planning repo) -> portent; test-quality audit -> analyzing-test-effectiveness; 3+ independent failures -> dispatching-parallel-agents; bd surgery -> managing-bd-tasks; new/edited skills -> writing-skills; hooks -> building-hooks; skill activation issues -> skills-auto-activation.
- Session-start extras: keep plan-docs-above-bd paragraph (RW4, line 105) in compressed form; ADD epics-gone-quiet line (breadcrumb 4): at session start, if bd list --status=in_progress shows epics with no commits/updates for days, surface them to the user before starting new work.
- hooks/session-start.sh cats the whole SKILL.md — no script change needed; verify JSON still valid.
- hooks/skill-rules.json: delete the "using-hyper" key (entry is redundant — file injected every session; its "start/begin" keywords are noise). Verify 10-skill-activator.js tolerates absence (it iterates keys; removing one is safe — confirm by reading the iteration site).

## Implementation
1. Rewrite skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md per inventories above. MUST/REQUIRED/NOT OPTIONAL language transcribed at full strength — restructure around the table, never soften. Keep frontmatter name/description.
2. Remove using-hyper entry from hooks/skill-rules.json (valid JSON after).
3. Run hooks/session-start.sh; confirm valid JSON output containing the routing table.

## Verification
- G1: wc -l < skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md  -> expect <=160
- G2: grep -c "MUST" skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md  -> expect >=6
- G3: grep -n "1% chance" skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md | wc -l  -> expect >=1

- G6: bash hooks/session-start.sh | python3 -c "import json,sys; json.load(sys.stdin); print('VALID')"  -> expect VALID
- G7: grep -n "in_progress" skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md | wc -l  -> expect >=1 (epics-gone-quiet)
- G8: grep -c "<example>" skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md  -> expect 0
- G9: grep -n "common-rationalizations.md" skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md | wc -l  -> expect >=1

## Boundaries

title: RW6a-T7: version-bump guard hook (SYSTEM-STATE CHANGE — veto window open since decomposition)
description: New PreToolUse hook denying git push from plugin repos when watched paths changed without a plugin.json version bump; fail-open; wired + documented + tested
design:
## Goal
hooks/pre-tool-use/02-version-bump-guard.py: PreToolUse hook on Bash that denies git-push commands in Claude-plugin repos when the outgoing commit range touches skills/, agents/, commands/, hooks/, CLAUDE.md, or README.md but .claude-plugin/plugin.json's version line is unchanged. Wired in hooks/hooks.json, documented in HOOKS.md, tested against crafted stdin JSON.

## Why
Epic bd-die breadcrumb 3 (Q6 prosthetic, proposed by RW4 and deferred here for user visibility): version-bump-before-push is the single most-dropped human/agent step in this repo (multiple remindings on record). The system should catch it mechanically.

## Context
- Convention model: hooks/pre-tool-use/01-block-pre-commit-edits.py (stdin JSON -> hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision deny + reason; sys.exit(0) to allow).
- hooks.json PreToolUse currently has matchers Read|Grep and Edit|Write; add a Bash matcher block for this script.
- HOOKS.md documents each hook — add a section.

## Implementation
1. Script logic, ALL branches fail-open (any exception or unknown state -> sys.exit(0); a hook bug must never block work):
   a. Parse stdin JSON; only proceed if tool_name == Bash and re.search(r'\bgit\b.*\bpush\b', command) — else exit 0.
   b. Skip if command contains --dry-run.
   c. Repo root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel (cwd from input if provided); exit 0 if .claude-plugin/plugin.json absent at root.
   d. Upstream: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref @{u}; fallback origin/main if it exists; else exit 0 (new remote, cannot judge).
   e. changed = git diff <upstream>...HEAD --name-only. If no intersection with watched paths (skills/, agents/, commands/, hooks/, CLAUDE.md, README.md) -> exit 0.
   f. If git diff <upstream>...HEAD -- .claude-plugin/plugin.json contains a changed "version" line -> exit 0 (bumped).
   g. Else emit permissionDecision deny with reason: watched files changed since <upstream> without a plugin.json version bump; bump patch for fixes, minor for features/rewrites (cite CLAUDE.md rule), then retry the push.
2. Wire in hooks/hooks.json: PreToolUse, matcher "Bash", command "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/pre-tool-use/02-version-bump-guard.py". chmod +x.
3. HOOKS.md: document trigger, scope guard (plugin repos only), fail-open design, and the veto/removal path (delete the hooks.json block).

## Tests
Run from a scratchpad temp git repo simulating a plugin repo (git init; commit base with .claude-plugin/plugin.json + skills/x.md; branch with upstream via a second bare remote; scenarios below). Drive the hook with printf JSON | python3 hook.py; assert on stdout/exit code:
- DENY: skills/ change committed, no version change -> output contains "deny"
- ALLOW-BUMP: same + plugin.json version line changed -> exit 0, no deny
- ALLOW-NONPLUGIN: repo without .claude-plugin/plugin.json -> exit 0
- ALLOW-NONPUSH: command "git status" -> exit 0
- ALLOW-GARBAGE: non-JSON stdin -> exit 0 (fail-open)

## Verification
- G1: all five test scenarios above pass (record transcript in close reason)
- G2: python3 -c "import json; h=json.load(open('hooks/hooks.json')); pre=h['hooks']['PreToolUse']; assert any('version-bump-guard' in str(b) for b in pre); print('WIRED')"  -> expect WIRED
- G3: grep -n "version-bump" HOOKS.md | wc -l  -> expect >=1
- G4: test -x hooks/pre-tool-use/02-version-bump-guard.py && echo EXEC  -> expect EXEC

## Boundaries
Never modifies .git/hooks (plugin PreToolUse only). No enforcement outside plugin repos. Does not attempt to parse push refspecs — branch-vs-upstream heuristic is deliberate; false-allows are acceptable, false-denies are not (fail-open bias).
close_reason: All gates pass. G1 test transcript: DENY watched-no-bump PASS; ALLOW version-bumped PASS (regex fix: version match anywhere in +/- line, single-line JSON); ALLOW feature-branch PASS; ALLOW non-plugin-repo PASS; ALLOW non-push PASS; ALLOW garbage-stdin exit-0 PASS. Design refinement within spec's stated false-deny bias: guard fires only on default-branch (main/master) pushes — the rule targets the FINAL push; mid-epic branch pushes stay free. G2 WIRED, G3=3, G4 EXEC.

title: RW6a gap fixes from end-of-epic review
description: 2 critical (hook command parsing), 2 important (using-hyper citation, stale Task-tool refs in hooks/)
design:
## Goal
All four reviewer gaps closed: hook parses git invocations structurally (no substring false-denies), branch/refspec check is token-exact, using-hyper's excuse-catalog citation is accurate, no stale Task-tool refs in hooks/ or root docs.

## Why
Reviewer verdict GAPS FOUND. The two critical gaps make a brand-new hook false-deny ordinary Bash commands repo-wide once merged (violates the spec's own false-deny bias). The important gaps inject stale tool names into every session and miscite the catalog.

## Changes
1. hooks/pre-tool-use/02-version-bump-guard.py — replace regex-over-raw-text with structural parsing: split command on shell operators, shlex-tokenize each segment, require an actual `git ... push` invocation (git as command word, push as subcommand after flag/global-option handling incl. -C/-c consuming args). Guarded-branch decision from parsed push args: explicit refspec dests (strip refs/heads/, resolve HEAD to current branch) checked by literal equality against main/master; no refspec -> current branch literal equality. Fail-open on tokenize errors.
2. Scratchpad test script — add adversarial cases: echo mentioning git push; git commit -m "will push later"; grep "git push" file; push from branch fix-main-bug; git push origin HEAD:main from feature branch (must DENY); git -C path push; chained cd && git push.
3. skills/common-patterns/common-rationalizations.md — add Skill Routing Shortcuts section carrying the pruned using-hyper excuses (simple question / remember this skill / one thing first / doesn't need formal skill / overkill / gather info first).
4. skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md — quoted classics match entries that now exist in the catalog.
5. hooks/user-prompt-submit/10-skill-activator.js ~171-172 + HOOKS.md ~46 — Task tool -> Agent tool; Task(subagent_type=...) -> Agent(subagent_type=...).

## Verification
- V1: adversarial hook test suite passes (all scenarios, incl. 5 new false-deny probes and 1 new must-deny refspec case)
- V2: grep -rn "Task tool\|Task(" hooks/ HOOKS.md README.md CLAUDE.md | wc -l -> 0
- V3: every quoted excuse in skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md appears verbatim in skills/common-patterns/common-rationalizations.md
- V4: re-run T6 gates G1-G9 and T7 gates G2-G4 (files touched again)
close_reason: All 4 reviewer gaps fixed and gate-verified: V1 13/13 adversarial hook scenarios; V2=0 stale refs; V3 all using-hyper quotes verbatim in catalog; V4 T6/T7 gates re-run clean. Root cause of both criticals (regex-over-raw-command-text) eliminated via structural parsing.

title: Task 1: fixing-bugs RED semantics + priority syntax fixes
description: Correct inverted RED-phase comments, remove authoring erratum in fixing-bugs; fix non-numeric bd priority in fixing-bugs + managing-bd-tasks
design:
Classification: simple

## Goal
fixing-bugs' RED-phase text reads FAILS-before-fix / PASSES-after-fix everywhere, the authoring erratum is gone, and all bd priority flags in fixing-bugs and managing-bd-tasks are numeric.

## Why
R1/R2: the skill currently teaches that the regression test should PASS before the fix (backwards) and ships an authoring mistake ("test was written backwards by mistake earlier") as instructions; --priority P1 is invalid bd syntax and fails when executed. managing-bd-tasks carries the same priority defect (scope add approved 2026-07-08). The file's own L361-374 and L493-497 state the correct semantics — this task aligns the broken sites to them.

## Changes
All line numbers verified at HEAD e4b39ee.

- `skills/fixing-bugs/SKILL.md` L69: `--priority P1` → `--priority 1`
- `skills/fixing-bugs/SKILL.md` L133-134: replace the two comments
  `# Expected: PASS (bug exists)` / `# Should fail AFTER fix`
  with
  `# Expected: FAIL (bug exists - no validation yet)` / `# Will PASS after the fix`
  (heading at L130 "**Run test, verify it FAILS:**" is already correct — keep it)
- `skills/fixing-bugs/SKILL.md` L150-164: replace the whole erratum block
  (from `**Run test, verify it now FAILS (test was written backwards by mistake earlier - fix this):**` through the closing fence at L164) with:

  **Run test, verify it now PASSES:**

title: Feature: Enforce executor commits before task closure
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Executor MUST commit all work before running bd close on any task
- One consolidated commit per task (not per deliverable within TDD cycle)
- Commit hash(es) MUST be included in the executor's task completion message to the lead
- Lead MUST verify commit hashes exist in completion message before approving next task proposal
- If commit hashes missing from completion message, lead redirects executor to commit first

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Executor prompt (agents/executor.md) restructured: commit moved from per-deliverable to per-task, positioned between verify-completeness and bd-close
- [ ] Executor prompt contains explicit anti-skip language making commit a prerequisite to bd close
- [ ] Task completion message format in executor prompt includes ### Commits section with hash and message
- [ ] Task completion message format in executing-plans skill matches executor's format (includes ### Commits)
- [ ] Lead validation in executing-plans includes Step 0: verify commit hashes exist before evaluating proposal
- [ ] Lead has redirect instruction for when commits are missing
- [ ] All pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO adding hooks for enforcement (scope: prompt and lead gate only, per design decision)
- ❌ NO changing TDD cycle itself (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR stays the same, only commit timing changes)
- ❌ NO adding commit to both per-deliverable AND per-task (one commit per task only)
- ❌ NO requiring the lead to run git log (lead checks message format only, not git state)

## Approach
Strengthen executor prompt and add lead validation gate. Two files change: agents/executor.md and skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md.

In executor.md: Remove commit from inside the TDD cycle (currently Step 6 after Refactor). Add commit as a new step between 'Verify completeness' and 'Close the task'. Add explicit language that committing is a prerequisite to bd close. Update the task completion message format to include a Commits section.

In executing-plans SKILL.md: Update the Task Completion Message format to include Commits section. Add Step 0 to lead validation: check for commit hashes before evaluating proposal. Add redirect instruction for missing commits.

## Architecture
- agents/executor.md — Executor agent prompt (commit flow restructure)
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — Lead orchestration skill (validation gate)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The executor has instructions to commit after each RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle, but in practice it often skips commits entirely. Work accumulates uncommitted across multiple tasks and only gets committed during finish-branch. This loses the benefit of atomic per-task commits and risks losing work.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- agents/executor.md:83-91 — Current commit step is Step 6 in TDD cycle, after Refactor
- agents/executor.md:93-104 — Verify completeness and Close task steps follow commit
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md:137-143 — Task completion message format has no Commits section
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md:145-187 — Lead validation process has no commit verification

**External:**
- N/A — this is an internal plugin workflow change

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Prompt reinforcement + lead gate ✓

**What it is:** Strengthen executor prompt to make commit mandatory before bd close. Add commit hashes to task completion message. Lead validates hashes exist before approving.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed executor.md — commit is Step 6 but positioned as part of TDD cycle, easy to skip
- Reviewed executing-plans SKILL.md — lead has no commit verification whatsoever
- Identified two enforcement points: executor self-enforcement + lead gate

**Pros:**
- Catches failure at two points (executor + lead)
- Minimal changes (two files)
- No new infrastructure (hooks, state tracking)

**Cons:**
- Still relies on prompt compliance at the executor level

**Chosen because:** Addresses the problem at both enforcement points with targeted, minimal changes

#### 2. Full restructure of executor flow ❌

**What it is:** Completely reorganize executor steps to separate TDD from commit/close gate.

**Why we looked at this:** Cleaner separation of concerns

**Investigation:**
- Would require rewriting most of executor.md
- Risk of introducing new issues in a well-established flow

**Pros:**
- Cleaner conceptual separation

**Cons:**
- More invasive than needed
- Risk of breaking working parts of the flow

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Scope creep — the commit enforcement can be achieved with targeted changes, not a full rewrite.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Executor.md needs a full rewrite for other reasons.

#### 3. Prompt + PostToolUse hook ❌

**What it is:** Add a hook that detects bd close without preceding git commit.

**Why we looked at this:** Mechanical enforcement can't be rationalized away

**Investigation:**
- Would need session state tracking (when was last commit?)
- Hook detection is tricky — timing, false positives
- Adds infrastructure complexity for a prompt compliance issue

**Pros:**
- Can't be skipped by the executor

**Cons:**
- Hard to implement correctly (session state)
- False positives possible
- Adds maintenance burden

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-engineering. Prompt + lead gate is sufficient enforcement without new infrastructure.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Prompt + lead gate proves insufficient after deployment.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Executor prompt changes (commit timing, anti-skip language, message format)
- Executing-plans lead validation (commit hash check, redirect instruction)

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Hook-based enforcement — deferred unless prompt+gate insufficient
- Changes to TDD cycle itself — never, TDD stays as-is
- Changes to test-runner agent — not needed
- Changes to finish-branch skill — not affected

### Open Questions
- None — design is straightforward

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

> Detailed context from brainstorming for task creation and obstacle handling.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What gap is the executor exhibiting? | Executor skips commits in practice | Need enforcement, not new capability |
| What enforcement mechanism? | Strengthen prompt + lead checks | Two-point enforcement, no hooks |
| Commit timing relative to bd close? | Before bd close (prerequisite) | Commit gates closure |
| How should lead verify? | Executor reports commit hashes | Message format change, not git log |
| Commit granularity? | One per task | Remove per-deliverable commits, consolidate |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Current Executor Commit Flow
**Question explored:** How does the executor currently handle commits?
**Sources consulted:**
- agents/executor.md:83-91 — Step 6 Commit in TDD cycle
- agents/executor.md:93-104 — Verify completeness and Close steps

**Findings:**
- Commit is Step 6 after Refactor, per deliverable
- Position makes it feel optional — it's inside the TDD loop, not a gate before closure
- No enforcement or verification that commit actually happened

**Conclusion:** Reposition commit as a gate before bd close, not part of TDD cycle

#### Lead Validation Gap
**Question explored:** Does the lead check for commits?
**Sources consulted:**
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md:137-187 — Task completion format and lead validation

**Findings:**
- Task completion message has no Commits section
- Lead validation checks requirements, anti-patterns, Design Discovery — but not commits
- Zero enforcement on the lead side

**Conclusion:** Add Commits to message format and Step 0 validation to lead

### Dead-End Paths
None — straightforward design with clear approach.

### Open Concerns Raised
None — user confirmed all design decisions.
close_reason: All success criteria met. Reviewer approved. Two files changed: agents/executor.md (commit gate before bd close, anti-skip language, Rule #10, Commits in message format) and skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md (Step 0 commit validation, redirect template, updated example and checklist).

title: Task 1: Update executing-plans dispatch and review logic
design:
## Goal
Remove per-task model classification from executing-plans and hardcode all dispatches to Sonnet.

## Why
This is the core logic change. executing-plans is the skill that actually dispatches executors and code reviewers — all other files reference its behavior. Changing this first establishes the new truth that other files will align to.

## Context
- executing-plans/SKILL.md is ~350 lines
- Line 2 (frontmatter description): references "classifies tasks as simple/medium" and "dynamic model selection"
- Lines 6-8 (skill_overview): references "classifies each task"
- Step 2 (lines 57-71) is "Task Classification" — currently maps simple→Haiku, medium→Sonnet
- Step 3 (line 80) dispatches with `model: "haiku" OR "sonnet" (based on classification)`
- Step 4 (line 112) dispatches Stage 2 with `model: "haiku"`
- Line 183: NEEDS_HELP reclassification dance (Haiku→Sonnet)
- Critical Rule #8 (line 302): "Dynamic model selection is load-bearing"
- Quick reference (line 22): "Simple (Haiku) vs Medium (Sonnet)"
- Rationalization (line 308): "This task is almost simple, I'll use Sonnet to be safe"

## Implementation
1. **Frontmatter description (line 2):** Update to remove "classifies tasks as simple/medium" and "dynamic model selection". New description should say something like: "Lead reads pre-planned task list, dispatches fresh blocking executor subagent (Sonnet) per task, runs two-stage per-task review, and escalates to user when plan proves invalid."

2. **skill_overview (lines 6-8):** Remove "classifies each task" — update to describe the simplified flow without classification.

3. **Step 2 — rename to "Pre-dispatch Verification":** Keep the step number to avoid renumbering all downstream references. Replace the Simple/Medium classification criteria (lines 59-71) with a brief check: "Before dispatching each task, verify: (1) task has a non-empty spec (design field), (2) blocking dependencies are met. If either fails, skip the task and note why." This is 2-3 lines replacing ~13 lines.

4. **Step 3 — hardcode Sonnet:** Change `model: "haiku" OR "sonnet" (based on classification)` to just `model: "sonnet"`. Remove the comment about classification.

5. **Step 4 — Stage 2 upgrade:** Change `model: "haiku"` to `model: "sonnet"`. Update the surrounding comment from "Haiku code quality review" to "Code quality review".

6. **Step 4 — remove NEEDS_HELP reclassification:** Line 183 says "If a Haiku executor returns NEEDS_HELP on a simple task: reclassify as medium and re-dispatch on Sonnet before escalating." Remove this entirely — all dispatches are already Sonnet.

7. **Critical Rule #8 — remove:** "Dynamic model selection is load-bearing" is no longer true. Remove it. Renumber remaining rules (current #9 becomes #8).

8. **Quick reference table (line 22):** Update from "Simple (Haiku) vs Medium (Sonnet)" to "Verify spec exists and dependencies met" (matching new Step 2).

9. **Rationalization (line 308):** Remove "This task is almost simple, I'll use Sonnet to be safe" — no longer applicable.

10. **Examples (lines 242-262):** Update Example 1 to not reference "Haiku" in classification — just show a normal dispatch with Sonnet. Rewrite Example 2 (NEEDS_HELP reclassification) to show a NEEDS_HELP scenario where the lead answers the question and re-dispatches (without model switching).

11. **Verification checklist (lines 316-320):** Remove "Task classified (simple/medium) with model selected" from the pre-dispatch checklist. Replace with "Task has non-empty spec and dependencies met".

## Tests
No automated tests (this is a markdown documentation project). Verify by reading the final file for internal consistency and grepping for stale terms.

## Verification
- [ ] `grep -i "haiku" skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` returns 0 results
- [ ] `grep -i "dynamic model" skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` returns 0 results
- [ ] `grep "classif" skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` returns 0 results in model-selection context
- [ ] Stage 2 dispatch shows model: "sonnet"
- [ ] Executor dispatch shows model: "sonnet" with no conditional
- [ ] No "reclassify" language remains
- [ ] Frontmatter description updated (no "classifies" or "dynamic model")
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Boundaries
- Do NOT change the two-stage review requirement (both stages mandatory)
- Do NOT change the escalation protocol
- Do NOT change the blocking subagent dispatch pattern
- Do NOT change executor return value parsing (DONE/BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP still valid)
- Do NOT renumber Steps 3-6 (keep numbering stable to avoid cascading reference changes)
- Do NOT touch other files — those are separate tasks
close_reason: All changes implemented and verified. Grep confirms no stale haiku/dynamic-model/classif terms remain.

title: Rework: Mode-1 loop interfaces
description: Gate-state blocks persisted to bd (R2), plan-impact notice format and emission points (R3), bd-not-only-bus pass (R4). Decides Q5 (pushed vs pulled signals) and Q6 (governor prosthetics). Explicitly NOT the base-view review skill.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- R2 gate-state: every gate (escalation, decomposition approval, expansion approval, completion, handoff) emits a standard block — where we are / what is decided / what needs the user — AND persists it to the epic's bd notes, so a fresh session reconstructs gate state from bd alone.
- R3 plan-impact notices: standard format (assumption affected / what was learned / which siblings likely care / proposed planning-repo edit, diff-ready) plus wired emission points: brainstorm discovering a plan assumption is wrong; executor BLOCKED on a contract mismatch; review finding sibling-relevant divergence. Notices persist in bd (findable) and appear in the gate-state block. Sessions never write the shared planning docs; the user carries notices upward.
- R4: no skill asserts bd is the only source of truth; plan-docs-above-bd layering is legitimate; Mode 2 with no docs is never nagged about them.
- Q5 decided here: which signals are pushed to the user vs available on demand (candidates: notice traffic, provenance drift flags, executor churn, disagreement counter-signals).
- Q6 decided here: governor prosthetics — what the system catches for the user beyond version bumps and session-close (candidates: stale ADRs, epics gone quiet, decisions made in chat that never landed in bd).
- plugin.json version bumped before final push.

## Boundaries
In scope: executing-plans gate and escalation text; brainstorming gate points; notice format doc in common-patterns; hooks if prosthetics need them.
Out of scope: the base-view review skill (deferred per D1); the unit schema itself (RW5 epic — this epic uses its vocabulary; if RW5 is unfinished at design time, stub the vocabulary and reconcile).

## Dependencies
Depends on: RW1 epic (same gate and escalation text). Soft dependency on RW5 epic for notice vocabulary — deliberately not a blocking edge.

## Context for Brainstorming
REWORK_PRINCIPLES.md R2, R3, R4. P1: the user is a rotating governor across parallel session lanes. P2: stalled lanes are cheap only when the stall is visible on return — invisible stalls convert to attention burn. The planning repo is git-versioned, so proposed edits can be concrete diffs the user applies at the base view.
notes:
GATE STATE (macro lead): RW4 STARTING, lead-direct per D2-AMENDED.
Decomposition (async veto open):
T1 common-patterns/loop-interfaces.md — single-source formats: the gate-state
   block (where we are / decided / needs-you) and the plan-impact notice
   (assumption affected / learned / siblings / proposed planning-repo edit).
T2 executing-plans — escalation, completion, and handoff points emit the
   gate-state block AND persist it to the epic's bd notes.
T3 brainstorming — decomposition-strategy and Step 8 gates persist gate-state
   to bd notes.
T4 notice emission wired at the three named sites: brainstorming (design
   reveals a plan assumption wrong), executing-plans (executor BLOCKED on a
   contract mismatch), review-implementation (divergence siblings care about).
T5 R4 tolerance pass — no skill asserts bd is the sole truth source;
   plan-docs-above-bd named as legitimate in using-hyper; Mode 2 never nagged.
T6 record Q5/Q6 decisions; sweep; version 3.15.0.

PROPOSED DECISIONS (async veto — grounded in P1/P2 elicitation):
D-RW4-Q5 PUSHED to the user: plan-impact notices, provenance drift flags,
  escalations, gate-state on lane completion (all drift-tier or blocking).
  PULLED on demand: executor churn stats, token burn, progress rollups
  (noise-tier; bd holds them).
D-RW4-Q6 prosthetics (doc-level this epic, hook implementation deferred to
  RW6a breadcrumb): epics-gone-quiet surfaced at session start via bd ready
  guidance; stale ADRs already covered by intuition's age check; version-bump
  enforcement proposed as a pre-push hook — BREADCRUMBED to bd-die, not
  implemented here (hooks change system state; user visibility first).
close_reason: 6 tasks + gap fixes; 2 critical + 3 important reviewer gaps all fixed; merged at v3.15.0.

Fix wording per reviewer recommendation.

title: Task 5: Add team vs solo routing to using-hyper skill
design:
## Goal
Update skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md to include a decision point that routes to the team execution path (wave-planning → team-executing-plans) when 3+ independent streams exist, and the solo path (writing-plans → executing-plans) otherwise.

## Context
Epic bd-6t7 criterion #6: "using-hyper routes to team path when 3+ independent streams exist, solo path otherwise."

Currently using-hyper has NO mention of team, wave, solo, or parallel execution. The brainstorming skill's Section 6 already includes the team vs. solo decision, but using-hyper's mandatory workflows section doesn't reference the team path at all.

## Implementation

### Step 1: Read current using-hyper skill
File: skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md
Understand the structure and find the mandatory workflows section (Section 4).

### Step 2: Update Section 4 (Follow Mandatory Workflows)
In the section that establishes mandatory workflows, add the team execution path alongside the existing solo path.

Current text references:
- hyperpowers:brainstorming (before writing code)
- hyperpowers:test-driven-development (during implementation)
- hyperpowers:verification-before-completion (before claiming done)

Add after brainstorming reference:
- After brainstorming, if epic has 3+ independent streams → use hyperpowers:wave-planning then hyperpowers:team-executing-plans
- After brainstorming, if <3 independent streams → use hyperpowers:writing-plans then hyperpowers:executing-plans (existing solo path)

### Step 3: Update the workflow description
Add a brief decision point explanation showing both paths:
```
brainstorming → sre-task-refinement
  → 3+ independent streams? → wave-planning → team-executing-plans
  → <3 streams? → writing-plans → executing-plans (solo)
```

### Step 4: Add team skills to skill references
Ensure wave-planning, team-executing-plans, and execute-wave command are mentioned where appropriate.

### Step 5: Verify no regressions
- Existing solo workflow references unchanged
- using-hyper still works for all task types
- Team path is ADDITIVE, not replacing anything

## Success Criteria
- [ ] using-hyper mentions team execution path (wave-planning, team-executing-plans)
- [ ] Decision point documented: 3+ streams → team, <3 → solo
- [ ] Existing solo workflow references preserved (no regressions)
- [ ] New skills referenced in appropriate sections

## Anti-Patterns
- DO NOT remove or modify existing solo path references
- DO NOT make team path the default — it's conditional on 3+ streams
- DO NOT add excessive detail — using-hyper is a meta-skill, keep it concise
close_reason: Added team vs solo routing decision point to using-hyper Section 4, updated rigidity/integration/workflow sections. All success criteria met.

title: Review fixes: managing-bd-tasks satellite rewrite
description: Drag the last pre-rework subsystem into the two-tier/30-min world: split/merge spec bodies, SRE handoff for splits, working bd 0.50.3 recipes, task-naming-guide + metrics-guide repair
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- R1: Every task spec authored by managing-bd-tasks operations (split, merge, re-dep) conforms to the two-tier templates in spec-templates.md — zero remaining "## Success Criteria" / "## Acceptance Criteria" / "Definition of Done" spec bodies (currently at SKILL.md 62, 72, 83, 118, 173, 445, 598).
- R2: Mid-flight splits route their new specs through SRE review (mechanism decided in brainstorm) so split-created specs are never unreviewed.
- R3: Every bash recipe in SKILL.md and resources/metrics-guide.md works against live bd 0.50.3: no grep of fields absent from text output (closed_at :264, deployed_at), no "^bd-" greps against glyph-prefixed output (:275, :290, :293; metrics-guide :97, :160), no hyphenated statuses (metrics-guide :17-22, :32-37), bulk-close extracts real IDs (:343-351), grep "Blocking" recipes replaced with real field names or --json (:223, :321).
- R4: Task-tree enumeration uses bd list --parent / bd children, not bd dep tree (:102, :222, :287) — matching the canonical recipe bd-commands.md will carry.
- R5: resources/task-naming-guide.md teaches the pipeline-constants sizing model (simple 2-5 min, medium 5-30 min, 30-min hard ceiling) and two-tier spec shapes; the 2-4h "good size" guidance (:128-142) and AC/DoD framework (:144-265) are replaced. Split examples produce subtasks under the ceiling (fix :469-527 hour-scale splits).
- R6: The dangling resources/dependency-patterns.md link (:703) is resolved (create the file or remove the link).
- R7: Preserved unchanged: Ops 3/4/6/7/8 structure (208-401), red_flags (661-669), task-naming-guide naming (6-116) + priorities (118-126), metrics-guide concepts (non-recipe prose).

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] `grep -rnE "## (Success|Acceptance) Criteria|Definition of Done" skills/managing-bd-tasks/` returns zero hits
- [ ] `grep -rn "dep tree" skills/managing-bd-tasks/` returns zero hits
- [ ] `grep -rn '^bd-' recipes gone: `grep -rnF '"^bd-"' skills/managing-bd-tasks/` and `grep -rn "grep \"\^bd-\"" skills/managing-bd-tasks/` return zero hits
- [ ] No hyphenated status values in any command: `grep -rn "status in-progress\|status=in-progress" skills/managing-bd-tasks/` returns zero hits
- [ ] SKILL.md Op 1 ends with a MANDATORY single-task-mode SRE dispatch step covering all split-created subtasks
- [ ] Every counting recipe in the three files carries `-n 0`
- [ ] Every bash recipe in the three files was executed live against this repo's bd 0.50.3 during implementation (executor captures command + output as evidence)
- [ ] task-naming-guide teaches pipeline-constants sizing; `grep -n "2-4 hours" skills/managing-bd-tasks/resources/task-naming-guide.md` returns zero hits
- [ ] resources section of SKILL.md has no dependency-patterns.md link and no other dangling links
- [ ] R7 preserve-list intact (structure of Ops 3/4/6/7/8, red_flags, naming+priorities sections, metrics concept prose)
- [ ] Version bumped (minor) in .claude-plugin/plugin.json
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO grep-parsing of bd human-readable output in any recipe (glyph-prefixed, unstable; --json is the machine interface — verified live 2026-07-09/10)
- ❌ NO counting recipe without `-n 0` (bd list defaults to limit 50 and silently truncates; verified live: 50 vs 248 actual)
- ❌ NO cycle-time recipe built on updated_at (moves on any design/notes edit; produces noise, not a metric)
- ❌ NO optional/opt-in wording for the split SRE step (R2 is immutable: split-created specs are never unreviewed)
- ❌ NO restating spec-template bodies or sizing bands inline (cite spec-templates.md / pipeline-constants.md — restating recreates the duplication drift this epic cures)
- ❌ NO blank-page rewrite of preserved regions (R7 preserve-list is binding; targeted surgery only)
- ❌ NO recipe shipped untested (every command line runs against live bd 0.50.3 before the task closes)

## Approach
Surgical per-file rewrite. SKILL.md gets the deepest cut in Op 1 (split): example specs become two-tier bodies, hour-scale framing becomes 30-min-ceiling framing, and a new mandatory final step dispatches one fresh blocking subagent running sre-task-refinement SINGLE-TASK MODE against all split-created subtask IDs (same dispatch shape as brainstorming Step 7: general-purpose, bypassPermissions, no model override; the prompt spells out inputs explicitly because single-task mode has no dedicated Input section). Op 2 and the three <examples> scenarios convert their spec bodies to two-tier. Ops 3-8 keep structure; recipes are replaced with live-verified bd 0.50.3 shapes.

Recipe canon (all verified live this session): -n 0 on every count; native --closed-after/--closed-before for velocity; bd list --parent <id> --all -n 0 for full enumeration (bd children is an alias inheriting the open-only default filter and limit 50); bd dep list <id> --direction=up [-t blocks] replaces grep "Blocking" and the bottleneck for-loop; bd blocked --json | jq length; bulk ops via --json | jq -r '.[].id' piped to multi-ID bd close; statuses always underscored.

metrics-guide keeps concept prose (R7) but recipes teach only what is measurable: lead time (created_at→closed_at from --json), with an explicit callout that the JSONL backend records no status-transition timestamps (bd history requires the Dolt backend), so cycle time is not derivable and updated_at is not a valid proxy. task-naming-guide keeps naming/priorities/dependency sections; granularity teaches the pipeline-constants bands by citation; AC/DoD framework is replaced with two-tier teaching ("does it work" → Verification section; "is it done" → standing pre-commit line + epic-level Success Criteria).

## Architecture
Components: skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md (operations + examples), resources/task-naming-guide.md (naming/sizing/spec teaching), resources/metrics-guide.md (metrics recipes). Integration points consumed as canon: skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md (two-tier bodies), skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md (sizing bands, ceiling), skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md single-task mode (:30/:40; dispatch precedent writing-plans:185-198). External blast radius verified at 4 lines (README:40, using-hyper:51+85, writing-plans:479) — all remain valid, no edits needed. bd-commands.md dep-tree sites (:20/:119), CLAUDE.md:144, writing-plans:57 are owned by sibling epic bd-48wr.

## Architecture Impact
(Step 4 result — questions per skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md)

- Q1 new component: NO (dangling link removed; no new files)
- Q2 public interface change: YES (Op 1 contract now mandates SRE dispatch; taught command surface changes)
- Q3 cross-component dependency added/removed: YES (adds deps on sre-task-refinement single-task mode + spec-templates.md; removes phantom dep on dependency-patterns.md)
- Q4 new request path (2+ components): YES (split → fresh subagent → sre-task-refinement)
- Q5 responsibility moved: NO

Result: 3 YES. /intuition was offered and declined 2026-07-10 — audit function served same-day by the six-agent fresh-cut review of these exact seams (consistent with the initiative-level deferral recorded at preordain).

## Boundaries
**In scope:** skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md; skills/managing-bd-tasks/resources/task-naming-guide.md; skills/managing-bd-tasks/resources/metrics-guide.md.
**Out of scope:** bd-commands.md canonical recipes (building-hooks+bd-commands epic owns them); spec-templates.md itself (planning-gates epic).

## Dependencies
Depends on: none (the correct bd recipes are already established empirically; no need to wait for bd-commands.md to land).

## Provenance
Source: bd-q2pa notes digest (2026-07-09 fresh-cut critical review of this repo, incl. live bd 0.50.3 conformance testing) + memory project_v3.24.0_freshcut_review.md; direct user instruction "fix it all" 2026-07-09; no external plan document. Brainstormed 2026-07-10 with fresh live bd 0.50.3 verification of all recipe shapes in this session.

## Settled Decisions
- Machine parsing uses bd --json output, not greps of human-readable text (text format is glyph-prefixed and unstable; closed_at exists only in JSON). Verified live 2026-07-09.
- Rewrite is targeted, not blank-page: preserve-list in R7 is binding.
- R2 mechanism (decided 2026-07-10): mandatory single-task-mode SRE dispatch from Op 1, one fresh subagent covering all new subtask IDs. Full-batch re-review rejected (heaviest, re-reviews N-1 approved specs); routing through writing-plans rejected (its review is opt-in and satisfying R2 would require editing writing-plans, out of scope).
- R6 (decided 2026-07-10): remove the dangling link. Nothing else references dependency-patterns.md; a fourth home for dependency guidance invites duplication drift; new bd dep list material lives in Op 3/6 recipes.
- Metrics honesty (decided 2026-07-10): keep cycle-vs-lead concept prose, recipes teach lead time only, explicit callout that cycle time is not derivable from the JSONL backend.
- NEW live findings folded into R3 scope: -n 0 required on all counting recipes (default limit 50 silently truncates); bd children inherits open-only filter; assignee is distinct from owner and absent unless explicitly set (bd update <id> --assignee) — personal-WIP recipe carries that caveat.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
managing-bd-tasks is the last pre-rework subsystem: its operations author task specs in the dead AC/DoD format, its recipes fail silently against live bd 0.50.3, and split-created specs bypass all review. Anyone following the skill today produces non-conforming specs and broken queries.

### Research Findings
**Codebase (investigator, 2026-07-10):**
- sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md:30,:40 — single-task mode exists, built for off-mainline specs ("tasks split, merged, or otherwise mutated outside the brainstorm flow" per writing-plans:479)
- writing-plans/SKILL.md:185-198 — the only single-task-mode dispatch in the repo; opt-in there
- executing-plans/SKILL.md:325,:385 — gap-fix tasks are the sole mid-execution creation exception; they get two-stage review + reviewer re-dispatch but never SRE; split-created tasks had no mandated review at all
- managing-bd-tasks files are the LAST task-level holdouts of "## Success Criteria"/AC/DoD spec bodies; all other occurrences are legitimate epic-level templates
- External blast radius of this rewrite: 4 lines, all remaining valid
- dependency-patterns.md never existed; only SKILL.md:703 links it
**Live bd 0.50.3 testing (this session):**
- bd list default limit 50 silently truncates (50 vs 248); -n 0 fixes
- Native --closed-after/--closed-before/--created-after filters exist
- bd list --json carries closed_at, dependency_count, dependent_count; bd show --json omits null fields (closed_at absent on open issues)
- bd dep list <id> --direction=up [-t blocks] --json works; dependency_count includes parent-child edges (bottleneck recipes must filter -t blocks)
- No status-transition history without Dolt backend; cycle time not derivable
- bd blocked --json, bd ready --json work with jq length
**External:**
- None because the only external system is the bd CLI itself, which was verified live (version 0.50.3) rather than researched from docs.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| R2 split-SRE mechanism? | Mandatory single-task SRE dispatch | Op 1 gains required final step; anti-pattern forbids opt-in wording |
| R6 dependency-patterns.md? | Remove the link | No new file; dep guidance stays in Ops 3/6 + task-naming-guide + bd-commands canon |
| Cycle time given no transition history? | Honest callout + lead-time recipe | Concept prose preserved (R7); recipes teach created_at→closed_at only; updated_at proxy forbidden |
| Design shape confirmation | Yes, proceed | Surgical 3-file rewrite as presented |
| /intuition on 3-YES impact? | Skip — audit served | Recorded; consistent with initiative-level deferral |

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Surgical rewrite + mandatory single-task SRE dispatch (chosen)

**What it is:** Targeted edits to the three files preserving R7 regions; Op 1 mandates one fresh-subagent single-task-mode SRE dispatch over all split-created specs; recipes replaced with live-verified shapes.

**Investigation:**
- sre-task-refinement:30/:40 confirms the mode exists; writing-plans:185-198 provides the dispatch shape
- Every replacement recipe executed live against bd 0.50.3 this session

**Pros:**
- Reuses an existing review mode — no new mechanism, no out-of-scope edits
- One dispatch regardless of subtask count; lightest option satisfying "never unreviewed"

**Cons:**
- Single-task mode lacks a dedicated Input section in sre-task-refinement — mitigated by spelling out inputs in the dispatch prompt

**Chosen because:** satisfies immutable R2 with the least machinery, entirely within the epic's file boundaries, on proven dispatch precedent.

#### 2. Route split-spec authoring through writing-plans (rejected)

**What it is:** Op 1 stops authoring specs; splits defer to writing-plans, which owns off-mainline spec repair.

**Why explored:** writing-plans:479 already names managing-bd-tasks in its integration section; single ownership of off-mainline spec authoring is attractive.

**Investigation:**
- writing-plans review is opt-in (:185 "If the user opts into review"); it has a per-expansion approval gate

**Pros:**
- One spec-authoring path for everything off-mainline

**Cons:**
- Satisfying R2 would require making writing-plans review mandatory for splits — editing a file outside this epic's boundaries

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** violates epic boundaries and R2 cannot be met with writing-plans as-is.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** a future epic makes writing-plans review mandatory for split-origin specs; then Op 1 may delegate.

#### 3. Full batch SRE re-review after any split (rejected)

**What it is:** Re-run batch mode against the whole epic tree whenever a split occurs.

**Why explored:** batch mode catches cross-task fallout (dependency restructuring after a split).

**Investigation:**
- brainstorming:344-362 batch dispatch reviews every child task — N-1 of them already reviewed and unchanged

**Pros:**
- Catches systemic effects of the split on sibling tasks

**Cons:**
- Heaviest option; re-reviews approved specs; epic explicitly asks for the lightest sound mechanism

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** cost is disproportionate; the split's cross-task effect (dependency rewiring) is already covered by Op 1's dependency steps and the single-task review of the new specs.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** evidence accumulates of split-induced sibling-task breakage that single-task review missed.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- The three managing-bd-tasks files, per Boundaries above

**Out of scope:**
- bd-commands.md, CLAUDE.md:144, writing-plans:57 dep-tree sites — owned by sibling epic bd-48wr (PLAN-IMPACT note with the -n 0 finding already delivered to bd-48wr's bd notes 2026-07-10)
- spec-templates.md (planning-gates epic owns it)
- Adding a dedicated single-task-mode Input section to sre-task-refinement — noted as a candidate for the catalogs epic; the dispatch prompt compensates here

### Open Questions
- None because all three Open-for-Design items (split-SRE mechanism, dependency-patterns.md, jq shapes) were resolved this session and recorded in Key Decisions; the jq shapes were settled empirically by live testing.

title: Rewrite executing-plans SKILL.md as declarative validation protocol
description: Core deliverable: rewrite the executing-plans skill from ~870 lines of orchestration state machine to ~200-300 lines of declarative validation protocol. The skill should describe WHAT the lead validates, not HOW to manage agents.
design:
## Goal
Rewrite skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md from an 870-line orchestration state machine to a ~200-300 line declarative validation protocol.

## Effort Estimate
4-8 hours

## Implementation

1. Study the current skill structure
   - Read current: skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md (870 lines)
   - Identify validation logic (~300 lines to KEEP): proposal validation checklist, escalation handling with Design Discovery, reviewer dispatch, epic compliance checks
   - Identify orchestration mechanics (~550 lines to REMOVE): health monitoring (Steps 3.E, 3.F), shutdown protocol (SendMessage type: shutdown_request/response), naming collision recovery (Step 3.B name checking), TeamCreate/TeamDelete lifecycle, timer logic, recovery protocol, resumption state machine (Step 0)

2. Write the new skill with these sections (~200-300 lines total):

   **Frontmatter + Overview + Rigidity (~20 lines)**
   - Name: executing-plans
   - Description: Lead validates task proposals against bd epic requirements. Fresh executor per task. Native TaskCreate for progress. Project memory bridges cross-task learnings.
   - Overview: Declarative validation protocol — describes WHAT the lead checks, not HOW agents are managed
   - Rigidity: MEDIUM FREEDOM — validation protocol is rigid (always check epic), orchestration is flexible (use Claude Code native features)

   **Quick Reference (~15 lines)**
   - Simplified table with columns: Step | Action | How
   - Steps: Load Epic, Spawn Executor, Validate Proposal, Handle Escalation, Completion Gate
   - No health monitoring, no shutdown protocol, no timer references

   **When To Use (~10 lines)**
   - Use after brainstorming/writing-plans creates epic and first task
   - Symptoms: bd epic exists with tasks ready, need iterative TDD implementation, want cross-task learning without context exhaustion

   **The Process — 5 sections:**

   Section 1: Load Epic Context (~40 lines)
   - Commands: bd list --type epic --status open, bd show <epic-id>
   - Extract and hold: Requirements (IMMUTABLE), Success Criteria (validation checklist), Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN), Design Discovery (obstacle context), Approaches Considered (rejected approaches + revisit conditions)
   - Read project memory: files matching epic-<epic-id>-* in memory directory
   - Resumption: if invoked mid-epic, read bd state (bd list --status in_progress) and project memory to determine where work left off. Spawn executor for the current/next task. No complex state machine — bd and memory are the source of truth.

   Section 2: Spawn Executor (~40 lines)
   - Find next ready task: bd ready
   - Spawn with Agent tool:
     subagent_type: hyperpowers:executor
     team_name: epic-<epic-id>
     name: executor
     mode: bypassPermissions
     prompt: (briefing template below)
   - Executor briefing template (EXACT TEXT to use):
     'Execute task <task-id> in epic <epic-id>.
      Start by reading the epic: bd show <epic-id>
      Then read your task: bd show <task-id>
      Read project memories matching epic-<epic-id>-* for cross-task learnings.
      Use TaskCreate to track your TDD sub-steps (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR/COMMIT).
      When complete: write learnings to memory, send completion report via SendMessage to team-lead.'
   - If name collision (executor-2): use the actual assigned name for subsequent SendMessage. No workaround code.

   Section 3: Validate Proposals (~80 lines — THE CORE VALUE)
   
   3a. On Completion Report:
   - FIRST: Verify Commits section contains at least one actual commit hash. If missing, send message to executor requesting commit before proceeding.
   - Re-read epic: bd show <epic-id>
   - Validation checklist (check EACH item explicitly):
     1. Does proposed next task advance toward a specific success criterion? (Which one?)
     2. Does it violate ANY anti-pattern? (Check each anti-pattern from epic individually)
     3. Does it touch a rejected approach from Design Discovery? If yes, check 'DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS' conditions
     4. Is it consistent with learnings reported in the completion message?
     5. Is scope appropriate? (Not too broad, not trivially small)
   - Decisions:
     APPROVE: Spawn fresh executor for next task with briefing template. Old executor idles out naturally.
     MODIFY: Spawn fresh executor with adjusted task description. Note what was modified and why.
     REDIRECT: Spawn fresh executor with entirely different task. Note why proposal was rejected.
   
   3b. On Escalation:
   - Re-read epic: bd show <epic-id>
   - Check Design Discovery 'Approaches Considered' section
   - If executor proposes a previously rejected approach:
     a. Find the 'REJECTED BECAUSE' reasoning
     b. Find the 'DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS' conditions
     c. If conditions NOT met: reject, explain why original rejection still applies
     d. If conditions ARE met: present to USER via AskUserQuestion before approving
   - Send decision to executor via SendMessage with reasoning

   Section 4: Completion Gate (~40 lines)
   - When executor reports all success criteria met:
     a. Verify each criterion in report against epic's actual criteria
     b. Spot-check evidence — does it make sense?
   - Dispatch reviewer as subagent (Agent tool, NO team_name):
     subagent_type: hyperpowers:reviewer
     prompt: 'Review implementation for epic <epic-id>. Follow agents/reviewer.md exactly. Start with: bd show <epic-id>'
   - Handle APPROVED: present final status to user, hand off to /hyperpowers:finish-branch
   - Handle GAPS FOUND: send gap details to executor via SendMessage, wait for fixes, re-dispatch reviewer

   Section 5: Memory Bridge (~30 lines)
   - Executor writes learnings file before completing each task
   - Filename: epic-<epic-id>-task-<task-id>-learnings.md
   - Location: project memory directory
   - Format: frontmatter with type: project + learnings content
   - What to include: unexpected constraints, rejected approaches confirmed/ruled out, design decisions affecting future tasks, gotchas for next executor
   - What NOT to include: code patterns derivable from reading code, git log summaries, ephemeral session state
   - Next executor reads these files on startup — they are the cross-task knowledge bridge

   **Examples (~50 lines)**
   - Example 1: Proper validation flow — executor completes, lead validates against epic, spawns fresh executor
   - Example 2: Escalation handling — executor proposes rejected approach, lead checks Design Discovery, rejects with reasoning
   - Remove: health check example, recovery protocol example

   **Critical Rules (~30 lines)**
   Keep these rules:
   - Lead NEVER implements — only orchestrates via validation
   - Validate EVERY proposal against epic — no auto-approvals
   - Check Design Discovery for ALL escalations
   - Executor spawned per task, not per epic (fresh context)
   - Epic requirements are immutable
   - Reviewer is subagent (no team_name), not teammate
   - One epic at a time
   
   Remove these rules:
   - Health monitoring rules (10 min + 5 min timers)
   - Shutdown protocol rules (shutdown_request/response)
   - Naming collision recovery rules
   - TeamCreate/TeamDelete lifecycle rules

   **Verification Checklist (~15 lines)**
   Keep: proposal validation checks, reviewer dispatch checks, memory bridge checks
   Remove: health monitoring checklist, shutdown checklist, naming collision checklist

   **Integration (~15 lines)**
   - Calls: executor (teammate), reviewer (subagent)
   - Called by: user via /execute-plan, after brainstorming/writing-plans
   - Workflow: load epic → spawn executor → validate → (repeat) → reviewer gate → present

3. Verify the new skill
   - Count lines: wc -l skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md (must be ≤300)
   - Search for forbidden terms: grep -c 'shutdown_request\|health check\|10 min\|5 min\|naming collision\|TeamCreate\|TeamDelete' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md (must be 0)
   - Verify all 5 process sections present
   - Verify executor briefing template uses TaskCreate for sub-steps
   - Verify SendMessage used only for completion reports, escalations, lead corrections

## Success Criteria
- [ ] New SKILL.md is ≤300 lines (verify: wc -l skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md)
- [ ] Contains all 5 process sections (Load Epic, Spawn Executor, Validate Proposals, Completion Gate, Memory Bridge)
- [ ] Zero references to forbidden terms (verify: grep -c 'shutdown_request\|shutdown_response\|health check\|health monitoring\|10 min\|5 min\|naming collision\|TeamCreate\|TeamDelete\|timer' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md returns 0)
- [ ] Executor briefing template instructs executor to use TaskCreate for TDD sub-steps
- [ ] SendMessage used ONLY for: completion reports, escalations, lead corrections (not progress tracking)
- [ ] Validation protocol preserved with 5-item checklist (advances criterion, violates anti-pattern, touches rejected approach, consistent with learnings, appropriate scope)
- [ ] Resumption handling present (read bd state + memory, spawn executor — no complex state machine)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Separation Strategy: Validation vs Orchestration**
The current skill has validation logic intertwined with orchestration in several places:
- Step 3.B mixes proposal validation (keep) with shutdown protocol (remove)
- Step 3.E mixes health monitoring (remove) with the concept of progress awareness (keep in simplified form)
- Step 3.F mixes recovery protocol (remove) with 'respawn for same task' (keep simplified)
Strategy: Extract the validation LOGIC from each step, discard the orchestration MECHANICS. When in doubt, ask: 'Is this telling the lead WHAT to check, or HOW to manage the agent?'

**Resumption Simplification**
The current skill has a complex Step 0 resumption state machine (decision matrix with 7 states). The new skill replaces this with: 'read bd state and project memory, spawn executor for current/next task.' This covers the common cases (fresh start, resume mid-epic) without edge case handling for migration states, multiple in-progress tasks, or old long-running executor patterns — those are no longer relevant.

**Line Budget Flexibility**
The 300-line limit is a hard constraint from the epic. Per-section line estimates (40, 40, 80, 40, 30) are guidelines. If validation logic requires more in Section 3, borrow from other sections. The constraint is total lines, not per-section lines.

**Risk: Losing Validation Checks During Cuts**
When removing orchestration, carefully preserve these specific validation checks that exist in the current skill:
- Step 3.B.0: Verify commit hashes present in completion message
- Step 3.B.3: Validate proposal against each success criterion, each anti-pattern, Design Discovery
- Step 3.C.2-5: Check rejected approaches and revisit conditions for escalations
- Step 3.D.1-2: Verify completion report against actual epic criteria
Do NOT lose any of these while cutting surrounding orchestration code.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO references to health monitoring, shutdown protocol, naming collision recovery, timers (epic-level anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO accidentally removing validation logic while cutting orchestration — verify the 5-item validation checklist survives intact
- ❌ NO reducing the number of validation checks the lead performs — the new skill must check the same things, just without orchestration mechanics around them
- ❌ NO placeholder text like '[detailed above]' or '[as specified]' — every section must have actual content

title: Task 4: reviewer.md re-keyed to two-tier spec vocabulary
design:
## Goal
agents/reviewer.md extracts and verifies the fields two-tier task specs actually have; epic-level criteria/anti-patterns handling unchanged; verdict contract untouched.

## Why
Review finding F8: the agent's per-task extraction (success criteria, implementation checklist, key considerations) names sections that no two-tier spec contains, forcing cold reviewers to record noise or invent criteria. This epic canonizes the agent as the single engine, so it must match spec reality. R4 of bd-iaem.

## Context
agents/reviewer.md 326 lines. Sites (line-verified): L48 extraction list ("Extract: goal, success criteria, implementation checklist, key considerations, anti-patterns"); Step 5 L120 ("Edge cases from the task's Key Considerations"); Step 8 L180 ("For every success criterion in the task"); Step 9 L193 ("every prohibited pattern from the task and epic anti-patterns sections"); Step 10 evidence table keyed "| Criterion |". Epic-level extraction L23-28 already captures Requirements/Success Criteria/Anti-Patterns/Approach at startup. Two-tier fields per skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md: simple = Goal, Why, Changes, Verification; medium = Goal, Why, Context, Implementation, Tests, Verification, Boundaries.

## Implementation
1. L48: re-key extraction to the two-tier fields — Goal, Why, Verification always; Changes (simple) or Context/Implementation/Tests/Boundaries (medium); note tier is detected by which sections are present
2. Step 5 (L120): source edge-case expectations from Medium Context/Boundaries and the epic Requirements instead of "Key Considerations"
3. Step 8 (L180): verify every item in the task's Verification section, plus map epic-level Success Criteria coverage (from the startup extraction); confidence rules (>=0.8) unchanged
4. Step 9 (L193): prohibited patterns come from the EPIC Anti-Patterns section only (two-tier task specs carry none)
5. Step 10 + verdict templates: relabel table keys to Verification-item / epic-criterion vocabulary consistently

## Tests
None because this is an agent prompt; verification is by grep and a read-through for internal consistency.

## Verification
- [ ] grep -n "implementation checklist\|Key Considerations\|key considerations" agents/reviewer.md returns nothing in the per-task path
- [ ] grep -n "APPROVED\|GAPS FOUND" agents/reviewer.md — verdict lines unchanged; loop-interfaces.md untouched (git diff clean on it)
- [ ] Every downstream step references only fields that exist in spec-templates.md or the epic template
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Boundaries
Re-keying only — no protocol steps added or removed, no frontmatter changes (model pin, preloaded skills), no verdict vocabulary changes.

## Addendum (lead, post-bd-q3az Stage 2 review)
Two additions surfaced by the epic-level loss audit of the review-implementation shrink — both belong in this task's file:
1. Port the dead-code detection tooling lost in the shrink into reviewer.md's Step 3 dead-code audit: language-specific detection (cargo build dead-code warnings; eslint no-unused-vars; swiftlint analyzer rules; vulture for Python) and the git-diff-based orphaned-tests check (tests whose production code was removed). Source: git show 5e526d4:skills/review-implementation/SKILL.md lines 154-171. Keep it compact (a short tool list + one orphaned-tests instruction inside the existing Step 3 — not a new protocol step).
2. Partial-tree note: reviewer.md's startup reviews every closed task; add one sentence stating that open/in-progress tasks are out of the verdict's scope and must be listed in the report as not-yet-reviewed (supports review-implementation's mid-epic sanity-check use case without inventing a new verdict word).
Boundaries update: these two additions are authorized exceptions to the 're-keying only' boundary; everything else in that boundary stands (no frontmatter changes, no verdict vocabulary changes).
