title: Sonnet-first model policy
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All executor dispatches use Sonnet — no per-task model classification for model selection
- Stage 2 code review dispatches use Sonnet (matching code-reviewer.md frontmatter)
- Simple/medium classification is retained for spec template selection only (level of spec detail), not model routing
- Utility agents remain on Haiku: test-runner, codebase-investigator, internet-researcher
- SRE batch review remains on Opus (unchanged)
- No new files created — this is a simplification of existing content

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md dispatches all executors with model: "sonnet"
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md Stage 2 review dispatches with model: "sonnet"
- [ ] No references to "Haiku-first philosophy" remain in any skill file
- [ ] No references to "dynamic model selection" remain in any skill or doc file
- [ ] Classification guide in all files describes spec templates only — no model annotations
- [ ] Critical Rule #8 removed or rewritten to not reference model selection
- [ ] brainstorming Step 8 handoff text updated (no "dynamic model" language)
- [ ] writing-plans description and overview decouple spec templates from model names
- [ ] sre-task-refinement classification checks reference spec complexity, not model
- [ ] commands/execute-plan.md updated (no classification-to-model language)
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated to reflect Sonnet-first policy
- [ ] README.md updated (executor description, example workflow)
- [ ] using-hyper/SKILL.md line 180 updated
- [ ] Grep validation passes: no "Haiku" in executor dispatch context, no "dynamic model" outside agent frontmatter

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO removing simple/medium classification entirely (spec template selection is still valuable — simple specs are less detailed)
- ❌ NO changing utility agent model assignments (test-runner, codebase-investigator, internet-researcher stay Haiku — they do mechanical search/execution)
- ❌ NO adding new model selection logic (the whole point is removing classification overhead, not replacing it with new complexity)
- ❌ NO changing SRE batch review model (Opus serves a different purpose — deep systemic analysis)
- ❌ NO leaving "Haiku" in executor-related contexts (references must be fully cleaned)

## Approach
Remove per-task model classification from the executor dispatch pipeline. All executors dispatch on Sonnet regardless of task classification. The simple/medium classification remains purely for spec template selection — determining how detailed the plan needs to be, not which model executes it.

This simplifies the lead's orchestration logic (no classification decision, no NEEDS_HELP reclassification dance), improves executor quality across the board, and aligns Stage 2 code review with the code-reviewer agent's declared model.

## Architecture
**Affected components:**
- executing-plans skill (primary: dispatch logic, review logic, critical rules)
- brainstorming skill (Haiku-first philosophy, classification guide, Step 8 handoff)
- writing-plans skill (spec template descriptions)
- sre-task-refinement skill (classification consistency checks)
- execute-plan command (delegation model description)
- CLAUDE.md (model guidance, summary table)
- README.md (executor description, example workflow)
- using-hyper skill (one line reference)

**Data flow unchanged:** brainstorming → task tree → writing-plans → specs → executing-plans → executor dispatch. Only the model parameter in dispatch calls changes.

## Architecture Impact
- [ ] Creates a new component/module — NO
- [ ] Changes public interface of existing component — YES (executing-plans removes model classification from dispatch interface)
- [ ] Adds/removes cross-component dependency — NO
- [ ] Creates new request path through 2+ components — NO
- [ ] Moves responsibility between components — NO

Result: 1 box checked. /intuition was offered and deferred (simplification change).

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The current "Haiku-first" philosophy adds classification overhead to planning and execution without meaningful cost savings. Sonnet has improved significantly, and the per-task model decision creates friction (reclassification on NEEDS_HELP, rationalization about what constitutes "judgment"), while the cost differential is small for bounded 2-15 min tasks.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- executing-plans/SKILL.md:59-71 — classification logic maps simple→Haiku, medium→Sonnet
- executing-plans/SKILL.md:112 — Stage 2 hardcoded to model: "haiku" despite code-reviewer.md declaring model: sonnet
- brainstorming/SKILL.md:301-307 — "Haiku-first philosophy" section argues for minimizing Sonnet
- writing-plans/SKILL.md:3,7,78,84,130,149 — spec templates annotated with "(Haiku)" and "(Sonnet)"
- sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md:77-78,148-149 — classification consistency validates model-to-spec alignment
- commands/execute-plan.md:7 — describes "classifies each task as simple (Haiku) or medium (Sonnet)"
- README.md:18,67,160-163 — "dynamic model selection" language
- using-hyper/SKILL.md:180 — one reference to dynamic model

**External:**
None because this is a plugin-internal simplification with no external dependencies.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which direction for leveraging Sonnet? | Executor defaults + code review | Focus on dispatch pipeline, not utility agents |
| Drop per-task model classification entirely? | Yes, drop it | Every executor gets Sonnet; classification for spec templates only |
| Remove model discussion from brainstorming? | Yes, remove entirely | Brainstorming no longer concerns itself with model selection |
| Sonnet-first framing | Yes | Default everything to Sonnet; Haiku only for utility/mechanical |

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Sonnet-first with classification removed (chosen)

**What it is:** All executor dispatches use Sonnet. Classification (simple/medium) retained for spec template depth only. Stage 2 review upgraded to Sonnet.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed executing-plans dispatch logic — model classification adds decision overhead per task
- Found NEEDS_HELP reclassification dance (line 183) — adds round-trip cost when Haiku fails
- Found Stage 2 inconsistency (code-reviewer.md says Sonnet, dispatch says Haiku)

**Pros:**
- Simplifies lead orchestration (no model decision per task)
- Eliminates NEEDS_HELP reclassification overhead
- Resolves code-reviewer model inconsistency
- Better executor quality across all tasks

**Cons:**
- Higher cost per simple task (Sonnet vs Haiku) — mitigated by small task size (2-5 min)

**Chosen because:** Token pressure has eased, Sonnet cost is acceptable for bounded tasks, and the classification overhead isn't worth the marginal savings.

#### 2. Shift threshold (more tasks to Sonnet, keep Haiku for trivial) (rejected)

**What it is:** Keep the classification system but make "simple" criteria much stricter — only pure renames and config changes get Haiku.

**Why explored:** Preserves cost optimization for truly mechanical work.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed current simple criteria — already narrow (exact known edits, no judgment)
- Estimated cost savings of Haiku on 2-5 min tasks — marginal

**Pros:**
- Slight cost savings on trivial tasks

**Cons:**
- Still requires per-task classification decision
- Still requires NEEDS_HELP reclassification logic
- Classification debate ("is this truly mechanical?") persists

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** The classification overhead costs more in lead complexity than it saves in token cost for 2-5 min tasks.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Haiku becomes dramatically cheaper or Sonnet becomes dramatically more expensive, making the per-task cost differential significant.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- All model-to-classification coupling in skills, commands, and docs
- Stage 2 code review model upgrade
- "Haiku-first philosophy" removal
- "Dynamic model selection" language removal

**Out of scope:**
- Utility agent model changes (test-runner, codebase-investigator, internet-researcher stay Haiku — separate decision)
- SRE batch review model (Opus serves different purpose)
- executor.md agent file (has no hardcoded model — correct, model set at dispatch time)

### Open Questions
None because the /consider session resolved all design questions before brainstorming began.
close_reason: All 4 tasks complete. Reviewer APPROVED. 14/14 success criteria met. Sonnet-first policy fully implemented across 9 files.

title: Task 1: Rewrite test-runner agent with universal validation
design:
## Goal
Rewrite agents/test-runner.md to remove all pre-commit references and add universal validation with 'Run: validate' auto-detection.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing agent structure
- Read agents/test-runner.md (350 lines currently)
- Identify all pre-commit specific sections to remove:
  - Line ~3: description mentioning pre-commit
  - Line ~7: 'pre-commit hooks' in mission
  - Lines ~23-24: 'Pre-commit hooks: pre-commit run' command type
  - Lines ~138-177: '### pre-commit hooks' section
  - Lines ~186-220: 'git commit triggers pre-commit' logic
  - Line ~257: 'Pre-commit Hook Assumption' section
  - Lines ~296-306: Example 2: Pre-commit Hooks

### 2. Remove pre-commit sections
Remove all sections identified above. Use grep to verify removal:
```bash
grep -n 'pre-commit' agents/test-runner.md  # Should return nothing after
```

### 3. Add Project Detection section (NEW)
Add after 'Execution Process' section:

```markdown
## Project Detection (for 'Run: validate')

When 'Run: validate' is received, detect project type by file presence:

### Detection Order (first match wins)
1. package.json → JavaScript/TypeScript
2. Cargo.toml → Rust
3. pyproject.toml → Python
4. go.mod → Go
5. Makefile → Generic

### Validation Commands by Ecosystem

**JavaScript/TypeScript (package.json)**
Parse scripts object for these keys (in order):
- Format: format:check, fmt:check → run if found
- Lint: lint → run if found
- Typecheck: type-check, typecheck, tsc → run if found, else try 'npx tsc --noEmit'
- Test: test → run if found

Example: `npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run type-check && npm test`

**Rust (Cargo.toml)**
- Format: cargo fmt -- --check
- Lint: cargo clippy -- -D warnings
- Typecheck: (included in clippy)
- Test: cargo test

**Python (pyproject.toml)**
- Format: ruff format --check .
- Lint: ruff check .
- Typecheck: mypy . (skip if mypy not installed)
- Test: pytest

**Go (go.mod)**
- Format: gofmt -l . (fail if output non-empty)
- Lint: go vet ./...
- Typecheck: (built into compiler)
- Test: go test ./...

**Makefile (fallback)**
Check for targets: format-check, lint, typecheck, test
Run each that exists in Makefile
```

### 4. Add 'Run: validate' handling to Execution Process
Update step 2 'Identify Command Type' to add:
```markdown
- Validate all: 'Run: validate' (triggers auto-detection)
```

Add new step after 'Identify Command Type':
```markdown
3. **If 'Run: validate' received**:
   - Detect project type using detection order above
   - Run validations in order: Format → Lint → Typecheck → Test
   - Stop on first failure (don't continue to later categories)
   - Skip any validation category where no command found
   - Report what was skipped and why
```

### 5. Update report formats
Replace pre-commit report formats with these exact templates:

**Validation Passed:**
```
✓ Validation passed
- Format: ✓ (npm run format:check)
- Lint: ✓ (npm run lint)
- Typecheck: ✓ (npx tsc --noEmit)
- Test: ✓ 47 passed (npm test)
- Exit code: 0
```

**Validation Passed with Skips:**
```
✓ Validation passed (2 skipped)
- Format: ⊘ skipped (no format:check script found)
- Lint: ✓ (npm run lint)
- Typecheck: ⊘ skipped (no type-check script found)
- Test: ✓ 47 passed (npm test)
- Exit code: 0
```

**Validation Failed:**
```
✗ Validation failed at lint
- Format: ✓ (npm run format:check)
- Lint: ✗ 3 errors (npm run lint)
- Typecheck: (not run - lint failed)
- Test: (not run - lint failed)
- Exit code: 1

FAILURES:

lint (npm run lint):
  src/utils.ts:15:1 - error: 'foo' is defined but never used
  [COMPLETE output - not truncated]
```

**No Project Detected:**
```
⚠ No project configuration found
- Searched for: package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, go.mod, Makefile
- None found in current directory

Use explicit commands instead:
  Run: npm test
  Run: cargo test
  Run: pytest
```

### 6. Update examples
Replace Example 2 (pre-commit) with:

**Example 2: Run Validate (JS/TS Project)**
```
User request: 'Run: validate'

You do:
1. Detect package.json → JS/TS project
2. Parse scripts: { 'lint': 'eslint .', 'test': 'vitest' }
3. Run npm run lint (pass)
4. Skip format:check (not found)
5. Skip type-check (not found)
6. Run npm test (45 passed)
7. Return validation report

User sees: Category breakdown with 2 skipped
```

**Example 3: Validation with Skipped Categories**
```
User request: 'Run: validate'

You do:
1. Detect pyproject.toml → Python project
2. Run ruff format --check . (pass)
3. Run ruff check . (pass)
4. Run mypy . (command not found - skip)
5. Run pytest (23 passed)
6. Return report showing mypy skipped

User sees: Validation passed with typecheck skipped
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `grep -c 'pre-commit' agents/test-runner.md` returns 0
- [ ] 'Run: validate' magic command documented with detection logic
- [ ] All 5 ecosystems documented with exact commands:
  - JS/TS: npm run format:check, npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm test
  - Rust: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, cargo test
  - Python: ruff format --check, ruff check, mypy, pytest
  - Go: gofmt -l, go vet, go test
  - Makefile: make format-check, make lint, make typecheck, make test
- [ ] Report format templates include all 4 variants (pass, pass+skip, fail, no-project)
- [ ] Explicit command handling preserved (existing 'Run: npm test' examples unchanged)
- [ ] 3 new examples added showing 'Run: validate' flow
- [ ] Agent description updated (line ~3) to remove pre-commit mention

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO breaking explicit command syntax ('Run: npm test' must still work exactly as before)
- ❌ NO hardcoded file paths (use relative paths, let agent discover)
- ❌ NO running format with --fix during validation (validation is read-only, use --check variants)
- ❌ NO continuing after failure category (stop early, report clearly)
- ❌ NO silent skipping (always report what was skipped and why)
- ❌ NO assuming tools are installed (handle 'command not found' gracefully)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Multiple Config Files**
- If both package.json AND Cargo.toml exist, first match wins (package.json)
- Document this behavior explicitly: 'Detection order is strict - first match used'

**Edge Case: No Config Files Found**
- Report error with helpful message listing what was searched
- Suggest explicit commands as alternative
- Do NOT fail silently

**Edge Case: Script/Command Not Found**
- For JS/TS: If package.json exists but no 'lint' script, skip lint category
- For Python: If mypy not installed, skip typecheck category
- Always report skip with reason: '⊘ skipped (no lint script found)'

**Edge Case: Partial Failures**
- If format passes but lint fails, stop there
- Report format as passed, lint as failed, remaining as 'not run'
- Include full lint output in FAILURES section

**Reference: Current Agent Structure**
Study existing report formats at lines 39-109 for consistent styling.
Study existing example format at lines 284-330 for consistent structure.

## Verification Commands
After completing rewrite, verify with:
```bash
# No pre-commit references
grep -c 'pre-commit' agents/test-runner.md  # Must be 0

# Has 'Run: validate' documentation
grep -c 'Run: validate' agents/test-runner.md  # Should be 5+

# Has all ecosystems
grep -c 'package.json' agents/test-runner.md  # Should be 2+
grep -c 'Cargo.toml' agents/test-runner.md    # Should be 2+
grep -c 'pyproject.toml' agents/test-runner.md # Should be 2+
grep -c 'go.mod' agents/test-runner.md        # Should be 2+

# Has new report formats
grep -c 'Validation passed' agents/test-runner.md  # Should be 3+
grep -c 'skipped' agents/test-runner.md            # Should be 4+
```

title: Review fixes: executor contract hardening
description: Make the executor side of the executor<->lead seam truthful and armored: Why in Identify, commit hash in DONE, anti-rationalization tripwires, TDD RED-evidence resolution
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- R1: executor.md's Identify list names every spec section the executor must read, including Why and Context (executing-plans rule 7 calls Why "the executor's only context").
- R2: The DONE one-liner carries the final commit hash; loop-interfaces.md's executor<->lead seam entry records the updated format.
- R3: executor.md carries tripwires for the recorded failure modes: never return DONE without a landed commit; never bypass a failing check (no --no-verify, no editing .git/hooks) — one-line mirror of the canonical vbc rule; existing format-discipline rules retained.
- R4: The TDD RED gate is executable by an executor that uses test-runner: test-driven-development/SKILL.md + executor.md state who runs the failing test and what failure-reason evidence is required (resolves the "read only the summary" collision).
- R5: TDD explicitly handles "no test framework configured" (not an implicit exception a rationalizer can slot into).
- R6: Out-of-boundary validation failures resolved: fix in-boundary failures directly; if the fix requires edits outside the task's Boundaries, return NEEDS_HELP.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] executor.md Identify list names Goal, Why, Changes/Implementation, Verification, and (if present) Context, Tests, Boundaries
- [ ] executor.md and loop-interfaces.md both read `DONE: <commit-hash> — <summary>` and agree verbatim on the executor->lead entry
- [ ] executor.md contains the landed-commit self-check (git log -1 + git status before returning DONE) and the one-line hook-bypass mirror citing verification-before-completion
- [ ] TDD SKILL.md Verify RED states who runs the failing test (implementer, directly, single test) and what evidence is required (failure message showing feature missing); executor.md agrees
- [ ] TDD SKILL.md names the no-framework case explicitly (blocked path, not self-service exception); executor.md returns NEEDS_HELP for Tests-section-without-framework
- [ ] The boundary-conflict sentence appears verbatim in executor.md: "Fix in-boundary failures directly; if the fix requires edits outside your Boundaries, return NEEDS_HELP."
- [ ] Consistency greps pass: old DONE template absent from the three in-scope files; git diff touches only the three files + plugin.json
- [ ] Version bumped in .claude-plugin/plugin.json (3.24.0 -> 3.25.0)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO frontmatter skill loads on executor.md (decision: inline tripwires — loads add hundreds of lines of context per fresh dispatch, worsening the exact context pressure that causes the recorded format drift)
- ❌ NO edits to executing-plans, finishing-branch, vbc, brainstorming, sre skills, or test-runner agent (owned by sibling epics bd-42cc / planning-gates; this epic's stale-looking DONE refs there are expected and theirs to fix)
- ❌ NO restated vbc methodology in executor.md (one-line mirror only — single canonical home is verification-before-completion)
- ❌ NO freeform hash position in DONE (the lead parses the hash positionally; freeform placement recreates the drift-detection gap this epic closes)
- ❌ NO new common-pattern files (out of boundary; all additions land in the three named files only)

## Approach
Surgical amendment at verified anchors: 7 edit sites in agents/executor.md, 2 in skills/test-driven-development/SKILL.md, 1 in skills/common-patterns/loop-interfaces.md. Preserves executor.md's deliberate minimalism (84 lines, cut from 369 in bd-l5m) — every addition is a tripwire or contract line, no restated methodology.

Chosen over (a) restructuring executor.md's Process around a gate model — larger diff, contradicts the settled minimalism decision, higher drift risk; and (b) centralizing the full contract in loop-interfaces.md with executor.md citing it — a citation is not armor for a fresh-per-task agent; the tripwires must live in the context the executor actually receives, and loop-interfaces' own rule is that definition sites carry the definition.

## Architecture
Components: agents/executor.md (contract definition site), skills/test-driven-development/SKILL.md (RED-gate methodology), skills/common-patterns/loop-interfaces.md (Verdict Contracts registry). Flow: executor.md defines the DONE format -> loop-interfaces records it -> bd-42cc (lead-side spine) later updates executing-plans' parser to consume the fixed-position hash. This epic must merge before bd-42cc starts (dependency recorded in bd).

## Architecture Impact
(Step 4 result — questions per skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md)

- Q1 new component: NO
- Q2 public interface change: YES (executor->lead verdict format gains fixed-position commit hash)
- Q3 cross-component dependency added/removed: NO (existing executor-lead edge changes contract shape; no edge added/removed — initiative-level YES reflected the initiative as a whole)
- Q4 new request path (2+ components): NO
- Q5 responsibility moved: NO (RED-run assignment resolves an ambiguity nobody could execute; nothing taken from an owning component)

Result: 1 YES. /intuition was offered and deferred (audit function served same-day by the six-agent fresh-cut review of these exact seams; initiative-level deferral recorded at preordain).

## Provenance
Source: bd-q2pa notes digest (2026-07-09 fresh-cut critical review of this repo) + memory project_v3.24.0_freshcut_review.md; direct user instruction "fix it all" 2026-07-09; no external plan document. Brainstormed 2026-07-10.

## Boundaries
**In scope:** agents/executor.md (sole owner this initiative); skills/test-driven-development/SKILL.md; loop-interfaces.md executor<->lead seam entry ONLY; .claude-plugin/plugin.json (version bump per release convention).
**Out of scope:** executing-plans/finishing (lead-side spine epic); vbc/brainstorming/sre (planning-gates epic — canonical hook-bypass rule lives there); loop-interfaces Stage-2 seam entry (lead-side spine epic); test-runner agent (its summary contract is consumed as-is — RED mechanics chosen so no change is needed there).

## Dependencies
Depends on: none. Blocks: bd-42cc lead-side execution spine epic (consumes the DONE-hash contract).

## Settled Decisions
- DONE carries the final commit hash — enables the lead's HEAD-advance verification. Rejected: lead-side-only detection via git log (lead cannot attribute commits without a declared hash).
- Hook-bypass canonical MUST lives in verification-before-completion (planning-gates epic); executor.md carries only a one-line mirror. Keeps one canonical home.
- Boundary-conflict wording (use verbatim, same text as vbc's): "Fix in-boundary failures directly; if the fix requires edits outside your Boundaries, return NEEDS_HELP."
- executor.md stays minimal (deliberately cut 369->84 lines in bd-l5m); all additions surgical, no restated methodology.

## Open for Design
- Armor mechanism: inline tripwires vs frontmatter skill loads. → RESOLVED 2026-07-10: inline tripwires (user-confirmed; loads contradict the one-line-mirror settled decision and add the context pressure that causes drift).
- Exact DONE format with hash; whether BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP carry a hash when partial commits landed. → RESOLVED 2026-07-10: `DONE: <commit-hash> — <summary>` fixed-position; BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP name landed hashes in prose (those routes get judgment, not mechanical parsing).
- RED-test mechanics: executor runs the single failing test directly vs test-runner failure excerpt. → RESOLVED 2026-07-10: executor runs the single test directly (bounded output); test-runner reserved for full-suite runs — also forced by boundary: test-runner.md is out of scope here.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The executor<->lead seam lies in three recorded ways: executors have returned DONE without committing, the DONE format gives the lead no way to attribute commits, and the TDD RED gate as written is unexecutable by an executor that follows its own test-runner rule ("read only the summary"). The fresh-cut review (bd-q2pa) confirmed all three live.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- agents/executor.md:17 — Identify list omits Why and Context; :52 commands committing but has no landed-commit self-check; :58 DONE template has no hash; :39 "read only the summary" collides with :20 "confirm it fails for the right reason"
- skills/test-driven-development/SKILL.md — grep "framework" returns nothing; exception list :37-41 is closed but lacks the no-framework case; Verify RED :58-68 never says who runs the test
- skills/common-patterns/loop-interfaces.md:72-76 — executor->lead entry says "lead parses the first word only"; no hash provision
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md:276 — example DONE already shows "committed as 3f9a1b2": informal precedent for hash-in-DONE on the consumer side
- agents/reviewer.md:7-9 — the frontmatter skills-load model evaluated and rejected as executor armor

**External:**
- None because this is a plugin-internal contract repair with no external dependencies.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Armor mechanism | Inline tripwires | Surgical MUST lines in executor.md; no frontmatter loads; consistent with one-line-mirror settled decision |
| DONE/hash format | Fixed-position: `DONE: <commit-hash> — <summary>`; BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP name landed hashes in prose | loop-interfaces entry updated to match; bd-42cc parser reads hash positionally |
| RED mechanics | Executor runs the single failing test directly; test-runner full-suite only | Written into both executor.md and TDD SKILL.md; no test-runner contract change (out of boundary) |
| No test framework | Executor returns NEEDS_HELP; TDD skill names the case as a blocked path | Closes the implicit-exception slot R5 targets |

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Surgical amendment at verified anchors (chosen)
**What it is:** Targeted edits at 10 verified sites across the three in-scope files, preserving executor.md's minimalism.
**Investigation:** Every anchor read directly this session; all line references confirmed current.
**Pros:** Smallest reviewable diff; respects settled minimalism decision; each requirement maps 1:1 to edit sites.
**Cons:** Wording must thread carefully through an already-tight file — mitigated by whole-file single-task ownership.
**Chosen because:** All four requirements are contract/tripwire additions, not structural problems; the settled decisions explicitly mandate surgical additions.

#### 2. Restructure executor.md Process around explicit gates (rejected)
**What it is:** Rewrite the Process section as named gates (SPEC GATE, RED GATE, COMMIT GATE, OUTPUT GATE) with per-gate evidence.
**Why explored:** Gate framing matches how the failures were recorded.
**Investigation:** Compared against bd-l5m history — executor.md was deliberately cut 369->84 lines; heavier structure was already tried and removed.
**Pros:** Very explicit; hard to rationalize past.
**Cons:** Large diff; re-grows the file the prior epic deliberately shrank; more context per dispatch.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Contradicts the settled "stays minimal, additions surgical" decision.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Post-fix telemetry shows executors still drifting past inline tripwires.

#### 3. Centralize full contract in loop-interfaces, thin executor citation (rejected)
**What it is:** Move DONE/BLOCKED/NEEDS_HELP definitions + tripwires into loop-interfaces.md; executor.md just cites it.
**Why explored:** Single-sourcing instinct — loop-interfaces is already the Verdict Contracts registry.
**Investigation:** loop-interfaces.md:70 states its own model: "Definition sites cite this section" — the registry records, definition sites define; executor.md IS the definition site.
**Pros:** One home for all verdict text.
**Cons:** A fresh-per-task executor receives only its dispatch prompt + agent file; a citation it never reads is not armor.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Inverts the registry's own definition-site model and removes the tripwires from the only context that needs them.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Executor dispatch mechanics change to inject common-pattern files into executor context.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** the three named files + plugin.json version bump.
**Out of scope:** executing-plans' parser and its stale DONE example (bd-42cc, which depends on this epic); vbc canonical hook-bypass rule (planning-gates epic); test-runner agent contract (RED mechanics chosen to avoid needing it); Stage-2 seam entry in loop-interfaces (bd-42cc).

### Open Questions
- None because all three Open-for-Design items were resolved with the user this session (see Key Decisions Made) and no implementation-time judgment beyond wording coherence remains.

title: Rework: Brainstormable-unit schema + authoring skill
description: Single brainstormable-unit schema (phase-doc slice + bd epic transports) in common-patterns, plus a thin draft/check authoring skill for the planning repo. Fixes observed pain: hand-guiding phase-doc structure every time.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- A single brainstormable-unit schema defined once in skills/common-patterns/, with two transports: phase-doc service slice (Mode 1, shared file) and bd leaf epic (Mode 2); preordain's leaf-epic template references the shared schema instead of duplicating it.
- Slice sections: Deliverable plus Requirements; Contracts (exposes/consumes, owning sibling, aligned to per-service build/release units); Depends-on/blocks; Boundaries (what belongs to siblings); Settled decisions with rationale; Open-for-design; Release framing.
- Phase-doc template: phase overview plus per-service slices. Colleague-facing constraint: reads as a normal engineering planning doc; machine-legibility comes from consistent structure only — no plugin jargon or markup.
- Slice requirements written separably: one slice may spawn several epics; an oversized slice can feed preordain as its initiative input.
- New thin skill plus command, home is a planning-repo session, two verbs: draft (structure roadmap thinking into a conforming phase doc) and check (schema conformance; per-slice cold-session test — could a fresh session brainstorm from this slice plus the overview alone; contract symmetry across slices; release alignment with lockstep-deploy flags). Signals only — flags gaps, never decides, never writes service repos.
- Q8 decided here: schema evolution discipline for colleague-facing docs (default proposal: additive-only).
- plugin.json version bumped before final push.

## Boundaries
In scope: schema file in common-patterns; phase-doc template; new skill and command; preordain template refactor to reference the schema.
Out of scope: brainstorm-side slice ingestion (RW3 epic consumes this schema); plan-impact notices (RW4 epic); the base-view review skill (explicitly deferred per D1 until provenance and notice usage is observed in real work).

## Dependencies
Depends on: none. Can run parallel with the RW1 epic. The RW3 epic is blocked on this one.

## Context for Brainstorming
Input doc: REWORK_PRINCIPLES.md R5 and the Mode 1 section. User facts: plans live in a dedicated git planning repo as phase documents with work breakdowns; one slice spawns one-to-several epics; docs are consumed by colleagues and by per-service Claude sessions. Observed pain being fixed: the user hand-guides doc structure every time so docs meet downstream brainstorm needs and respect per-service build/release structure. Naming: thinking-skill names follow the blue MTG cantrip convention.

title: Delete volatility-decomposition skill and /decompose command
design:
## Goal
Remove the volatility-decomposition skill and /decompose command entirely. Clean up references in skills that are NOT being rewritten in subsequent tasks. Leave architectural-audit references alone — that entire skill is being rewritten and renamed to 'intuition' in the next task.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Delete skill directory
```bash
rm -rf skills/volatility-decomposition/
```

### Step 2: Delete command file
```bash
rm commands/decompose.md
```

### Step 3: Clean up brainstorming cross-references

File: skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md

**Reference 1 (line ~171):** 'since the outer-loop decomposition without stale artifacts'
Replace with: 'catching drift without stale artifacts'

**Reference 2 (line ~1086):** In integration section, the call chain:
```
OUTER LOOP (when architecture node is stable):
  /decompose → /audit-arch → stable node → brainstorming (auto-loads arch context)
```
Replace with:
```
ARCHITECTURE CYCLE:
  /brainstorm → build → update model
              ↕
  /intuition → find tensions → resolve → update model
```

**Reference 3 (line ~1096):** 'Stable architecture node handoff from outer loop (/decompose -> /audit-arch -> stable -> /brainstorm)'
Replace with: 'Architecture tension resolution (via /intuition)'

### Step 4: DO NOT touch these files (rewritten in subsequent tasks)
- skills/architectural-audit/SKILL.md — entire skill being rewritten and renamed to intuition
- skills/common-patterns/adr-template.md — vocabulary changes happen in the intuition rewrite task

### Step 5: Verify no dangling references in final-state files
```bash
# Check brainstorming (should have zero /decompose or volatility-decomposition)
grep -n 'decompos\|/decompose\|volatility.decomposition' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md

# Check using-hyper (should have zero — confirmed clean already)
grep -n 'decompos\|/decompose' skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md

# Check all command files (decompose.md should not exist)
ls commands/decompose.md 2>&1

# Full sweep of non-audit skills for dangling refs
grep -rn '/decompose' skills/ --exclude-dir=architectural-audit --exclude-dir=volatility-decomposition
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] skills/volatility-decomposition/ directory does not exist
- [ ] commands/decompose.md does not exist
- [ ] `grep -rn '/decompose' skills/brainstorming/` returns zero matches
- [ ] `grep -rn 'volatility.decomposition' skills/brainstorming/` returns zero matches
- [ ] `grep -rn '/decompose' skills/ --exclude-dir=architectural-audit` returns zero matches (audit excluded — rewritten in next task)
- [ ] Brainstorming integration section shows revised call chain without /decompose
- [ ] All remaining skills parse correctly (no broken markdown)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ DO NOT update skills/architectural-audit/SKILL.md (entire skill rewritten in next task — wasted work)
- ❌ DO NOT reference 'Mode 2' or other future capabilities in replacement text (doesn't exist yet)
- ❌ DO NOT update common-patterns/adr-template.md vocabulary (part of larger vocabulary shift in intuition task)
- ❌ DO NOT delete files with git rm — just rm, let git track the deletion normally
- ❌ DO NOT rewrite brainstorming sections beyond the specific /decompose references — minimize blast radius

## Key Considerations

**Scoping discipline:** The audit skill has 12+ /decompose references, but updating them now is pure waste — the entire file is being rewritten. Only update files that are in their final state after this task.

**The /intuition command doesn't exist yet:** When updating brainstorming's integration section, reference /intuition in the call chain (it will exist after the next task). This is acceptable because the integration section is documentation of the intended workflow, not executable.

**common-patterns/adr-template.md:** Contains 'volatility axis' in example ADRs. This is Lowy vocabulary that will be updated when the intuition skill is written (vocabulary shift is part of that task's scope). Do not touch it here.

title: Task 7: preordain — naming fix and constants citations
description: Remove the two Grand planner leftovers and cite pipeline-constants for the sizing gate and leaf-epic target.
design:
## Goal
skills/preordain/SKILL.md uses its own name consistently and sources its thresholds from pipeline-constants.md.

## Why
Two "Grand planner" leftovers survive from the pre-rename skill (finding F7), and preordain embeds its own copies of the gate/target numbers — part of the four-variant drift this epic kills.

## Changes
- skills/preordain/SKILL.md rule 4 (~line 243): "Grand planner creates epics, not tasks" becomes "Preordain creates epics, not tasks" (keep the rule's meaning and strength).
- Integration section "Relationship to brainstorming" (~lines 296-299): replace "Grand planner" with "Preordain" in both lines.
- when_to_use (~line 30), Step 3 edge case (~line 136), and rule 5 (~line 244): keep the 5-10 leaf target and gate language but cite skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md as the source of the numbers (e.g. "below the sizing gate — see common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md").

## Additional Changes and Site Corrections (ADDED BY SRE BATCH REVIEW)
- Site correction: rg finds exactly two "Grand planner" occurrences — line ~243 (rule 4) and line ~297 (ONE line inside "Relationship to brainstorming"). The "both lines" wording above overcounts; the rg verification below is the authority.
- "Does NOT produce" list ~line 293: "Implementation plans (writing-plans creates those)" is a stale mainline claim (epic requirement: no claim that writing-plans creates the plan/tree). Keep the bullet's meaning (preordain does not produce implementation plans) but re-attribute, e.g. "Implementation plans (task specs are created by brainstorming; writing-plans only repairs specs off-mainline)". Note the adjacent line ~292 already correctly says brainstorming creates tasks.

## Verification
- [ ] rg -i 'grand planner' skills/ returns zero
- [ ] rg -c 'pipeline-constants' skills/preordain/SKILL.md returns 1 or more
- [ ] rg 'writing-plans creates' skills/preordain/SKILL.md returns zero (ADDED BY SRE BATCH REVIEW)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

title: RW5 T1: Create skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md (unit schema)
description:
Tier: medium. Executor: lead-direct (D2-AMENDED).

## Goal
Create skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md — the single definition of the brainstormable unit, rendered through two transports (phase-doc service slice, bd leaf epic).

## Why
R5: one schema, single-sourced in common-patterns, kills the observed pain of hand-guiding phase-doc structure. RW3 (bd-uyc) ingestion and the portent skill both consume this file.

## Context
Modeled on skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md (single-source pattern: 'skills reference these values instead of embedding them'). The bd transport must match what skills/preordain/SKILL.md Step 5 creates and skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md consumes (Requirements IMMUTABLE / Boundaries / Dependencies / Context for Brainstorming — verified 2026-07-06).

## Implementation
Sections, in order:
1. Header: what this file is; definition rendered via two transports; skills cite, never restate.
2. Definition: smallest self-contained package of intent letting a fresh design session produce a full task tree without the author. Cold-session acceptance test stated explicitly: fresh session + unit + surrounding overview => knows what to build, what is decided, what is open, where the edges are. Any of the four requiring the author = fail.
3. Sections table: seven sections, each with what-it-holds and downstream-need-served columns: Deliverable & Requirements; Contracts (exposes/consumes, owning sibling named, aligned to per-service build/release units); Dependencies (depends-on/blocks); Boundaries (siblings' jobs); Settled Decisions (with rationale — the don't-re-ask rule); Open for Design (scopes the downstream question round); Release Framing (flags, ordering, lockstep constraints).
4. Required-means-addressed semantics: every unit addresses all seven; 'None' is a statement, omission is an accident.
5. Separability rule: requirements written so subsets pull apart cleanly; one unit may spawn several epics; an oversized unit becomes preordain's initiative input (sizing thresholds: cite skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md — NO inline numbers).
6. Transport 1 — phase-doc slice: colleague-facing constraint (reads as normal engineering planning doc; consistent structure only, no tool vocabulary); template at skills/portent/phase-doc-template.md.
7. Transport 2 — bd leaf epic: mapping table unit-section -> epic design section (Deliverable & Requirements -> Requirements (IMMUTABLE); Boundaries -> Boundaries; Dependencies -> Dependencies; Settled Decisions -> Settled Decisions; Open for Design -> Open for Design; Contracts + Release Framing -> fold into Context for Brainstorming when single-service, own headings when cross-service). Creation scaffold: cite skills/preordain/SKILL.md Step 5.
8. Schema evolution (Q8 decision, additive-only): v1 seven sections are the only permanently required ones; later additions always optional; renames/removals only via deprecation (old name stays valid, marked here, checks report informational); conformance checks never fail docs missing later-added sections; breaking change = successor file proposed to user, never in-place edit. Changelog: v1 (2026-07-06).

## Verification (gates — abort on failure, never proceed)
- rg -q 'skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md' skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md || ABORT
- rg -q 'skills/portent/phase-doc-template.md' skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md || ABORT
- rg -q 'skills/preordain/SKILL.md' skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md || ABORT
- rg -qE '[0-9]+ tasks|[0-9]+ min|exceed ~?[0-9]' skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md && ABORT (threshold numbers live only in pipeline-constants)
- All seven section names appear verbatim (fixed-string rg per name) || ABORT

## Boundaries
No edits to preordain (T5), no template content (T2), no skill process text (T3). Schema defines WHAT a unit is, not HOW to author one.
close_reason: Schema created; all gates green (citation x3, no inline thresholds, seven sections verbatim)

title: Task 1: executing-plans — branch establishment + working-branch verification (R1, R9)
description: Add Branch Establishment to Startup, per-dispatch/per-commit branch re-verification, Branch: <working branch> in all 5 templates, and the Announce line
design:
## Goal
executing-plans establishes a working branch at startup and re-verifies it before every dispatch and every lead-side commit, so executors never run on the default branch.

## Why
This is the epic's P0: the mainline never creates a feature branch — executors commit straight to main, and finishing/reviewer flows assume a feature branch that doesn't exist. A live incident (bd-go7m, 2026-07-10) also proved review subagents can switch the repo to main mid-epic, so a startup-only check is insufficient. Tasks 2 and 3 edit later regions of this same file and assume this task's text is in place; Task 5's finishing gate assumes epic work lands on a feature branch.

## Context
File: skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md (read it whole first; ~396 lines at v3.25.0 — line hints below are from that version, re-locate by quoted text).
- <the_process> opens at "## 1. Startup" (~39) with a bash block (bd show / bd list), then "**Extract and hold**", then the gate-state paragraph, then "**Verify:** Every task has a non-empty spec".
- "## 2. Pre-dispatch Verification" (~57) has a 2-item numbered list.
- Five templates carry the line "Branch: <current branch>": executor dispatch (~90), Stage 1 feedback template (~158), BLOCKED re-dispatch (~181), NEEDS_HELP re-dispatch (~204), timeout re-dispatch in Integration/When stuck (~389).
- Stage-2 PASS closure sentence (~138): "Run `bd close <task-id>`, then proceed to the next task."
- quick_reference table (~16-23); verification_checklist (~347-365); Integration "**Flow:**" line (~374).
- This repo's branch convention: epic/<bd-id> (13 consecutive epic merges). Default branch here is main.
This task is the single owner of ALL branch-related text file-wide — including the small insertions in Stage-2/Completion regions listed below. Tasks 2/3 will not touch branch text.

## Implementation
1. Announce line (R9): immediately after the <the_process> tag, before "## 1. Startup", add:
   **Announce:** "I'm using the executing-plans skill."
2. Branch Establishment (R1): in Startup, after the opening bash block and before "**Extract and hold for the entire session:**", add a subsection:
   ### Branch Establishment
   ```bash
   git branch --show-current
   ```
   - On the default branch (main/master) → create the epic branch: `git checkout -b epic/<epic-id>`. If `epic/<epic-id>` already exists (e.g. resuming after a subagent left the repo on main — the exact bd-go7m incident), check it out instead: `git checkout epic/<epic-id>`.
   - On any other branch → accept it as-is (resumes and user-created branches are both valid).
   - Empty output (detached HEAD) → not on any branch; create the epic branch from here (`git checkout -b epic/<epic-id>`), which preserves the current state.
   Record the result as the **working branch** for the session. Executors are NEVER dispatched on the default branch.
   Then state the re-verification rule (evidence: observed live — a review subagent checked out main mid-epic and the lead committed onto it):
   Subagents can and do switch branches. Re-verify `git branch --show-current` equals the working branch (a) before every executor dispatch and (b) before every lead-side git commit or bd mutation that triggers a sync commit (bd close, bd update, gate-state persist). On mismatch: `git checkout <working-branch>` before proceeding.
3. Pre-dispatch Verification: add item 3: "Current branch equals the working branch (`git branch --show-current`) — restore it if a subagent switched branches."
4. Replace all five "Branch: <current branch>" template lines with "Branch: <working branch>".
5. Stage-2 PASS closure sentence: before "Run `bd close <task-id>`", insert the clause "verify the working branch (Branch Establishment rule), then". Touch nothing else in the Stage-2 region.
6. Completion step 3a (gate-state persist): prepend "Verify the working branch, then" to the persist instruction. Touch nothing else in the Completion region.
7. quick_reference: add a row directly after the Startup row:
   | **Branch** | Establish working branch; never dispatch on the default | `git branch --show-current` (+ `git checkout -b epic/<epic-id>` when on main) |
8. verification_checklist: under "Before dispatching each task" add "- [ ] Current branch equals the working branch"; under "After each DONE return" add "- [ ] Working branch verified before task closure"; under "Before completion" add "- [ ] Working branch verified before gate-state persist and any final commits".
9. Integration "**Flow:**" line: insert "Establish branch → " after "Startup → ".
Commit the change when done.

## Verification
- `grep -n 'Announce' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` → 1 hit at the top of the_process
- `grep -c 'Branch: <working branch>' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` → 5
- `grep -n 'Branch: <current branch>' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` → no matches
- `grep -n 'epic/<epic-id>' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` → hits in Branch Establishment + quick_reference
- `grep -c 'working branch' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` → ≥ 10 (startup rule, pre-dispatch, 5 templates, closure, persist, checklist ×3)

## Boundaries
Out of scope: Stage-1 diff mechanics and spec-truncation checks (Task 2); Stage-2 CONCERNS handling, reviewer MUST framing, naming sweep (Task 3); any file other than skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md; version bump (Task 6). In the Stage-2 and Completion regions, make ONLY the two branch-clause insertions named in steps 5-6.

title: Review seam reconciliation: single end-of-epic gate in executing-plans
description: Resolve review-findings cluster 2: executing-plans owns the end-of-epic reviewer gate + STOP + architecture checklist; review-implementation becomes an on-demand re-verification utility; reviewer.md re-keyed to two-tier specs; five doc surfaces reconciled.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- R1: executing-plans' completion section, APPROVED branch: persist gate-state, run the post-build architecture checklist (cited from skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md — dispatch ponder UPDATE when any answer is YES and docs/arch/*.c4 exists; note-and-suggest when no model), present final status with explicit manual-validation STOP (do not auto-call finish-branch; user triggers closure), and carry the plan-impact-notice emission line. GAPS FOUND gap-fix loop unchanged.
- R2: skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md is the single source for BOTH variants of the 5 structural questions (pre-build design check and post-build implementation check, explicitly marked as the same questions in different tense) plus the ponder UPDATE dispatch input template. No consumer restates the questions.
- R3: skills/review-implementation/SKILL.md is an on-demand re-verification utility of at most ~150 lines: dispatch the reviewer agent, apply the same STOP semantics, cite the same checklist. No stale caller claims (the "called by executing-plans (Step 5)" lie dies), no dead resources/ links, command description updated to the utility role.
- R4: agents/reviewer.md per-task extraction matches spec-templates.md two-tier fields (Changes/Implementation, Verification, Context/Boundaries); epic-level Success Criteria and Anti-Patterns remain extracted at startup; Steps 5/8/9/10 re-keyed accordingly. Verdict contract (APPROVED/GAPS FOUND per loop-interfaces.md) unchanged.
- R5: finishing-a-development-branch (4 sites + call-chain diagram) and commands/finish-branch.md reference the reviewer gate inside executing-plans' completion (or /review-implementation re-verification) plus manual validation — no reference to review-implementation as a mandatory mainline step.
- R6: ponder skill (7 reference lines) and agents/ponder.md description name the new dispatchers; ponder's dispatch-is-approval rule (critical rule 4) updated to the new authorized callers.
- R7: CLAUDE.md (lines 198, 210 area), README.md (lines 20, 59 area), and using-hyper routing table row describe the reconciled chain: brainstorming -> executing-plans (reviewer gate + STOP in completion) -> finishing-a-development-branch, with review-implementation listed as on-demand re-verification.
- R8: Consistency sweep clean (no file claims the old chain; analyzing-test-effectiveness's 3 references still read correctly with minimal wording fixes only); plugin.json bumped to 3.21.0.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] grep -rn "called by.*executing-plans" skills/review-implementation/ returns nothing; no repo file (outside .beads/.git/.claude) claims executing-plans calls review-implementation
- [ ] The 5 structural questions appear in exactly one file: skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md (both tenses); brainstorming and executing-plans and review-implementation cite, not restate
- [ ] wc -l skills/review-implementation/SKILL.md <= 150; no resources/ links in it
- [ ] grep -n "implementation checklist\|key considerations" agents/reviewer.md returns nothing in the per-task extraction path; grep -n "APPROVED\|GAPS FOUND" agents/reviewer.md unchanged in contract terms; loop-interfaces.md untouched by this epic
- [ ] finishing-a-development-branch + commands/finish-branch.md contain no mandatory-prerequisite reference to /hyperpowers:review-implementation; their stated gate is reviewer APPROVED + manual validation
- [ ] executing-plans completion contains the STOP instruction (explicit: do not auto-call finish-branch) and the architecture checklist citation
- [ ] using-hyper routing table row for build tasks shows the reconciled chain; a row or note covers on-demand re-verification
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing; ./hooks/test/contract-test.sh exits 0 (no hooks touched, run as regression evidence)
- [ ] plugin.json version = 3.21.0

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO changes to verdict vocabularies or loop-interfaces.md (the three stage-distinct contracts are the one verified-airtight seam — do not touch)
- ❌ NO changes to the per-task two-stage review loop (ADR-003 guard cluster; guard removal requires the A/B re-baselining method — entirely out of scope)
- ❌ NO restating the 5 structural questions outside architecture-impact-check.md (restatement drift is the defect class this epic fixes)
- ❌ NO weakening the manual-validation STOP while porting it (relocation, not disarmament — the "do not auto-call finishing" instruction survives verbatim in substance)
- ❌ NO opportunistic fixes of other review clusters (satellite skills, README general staleness, context-economics dedup, brainstorming Step 8 "spec check + code quality" shorthand — separate epics)

## Approach
Pattern-file-first surgical reconciliation: (1) extend architecture-impact-check.md into the single source for both question variants + the ponder dispatch template; (2) rewrite the two consumers — executing-plans completion gains the STOP and checklist citations, review-implementation shrinks to a re-verify utility; (3) realign reviewer.md's per-task vocabulary with two-tier specs; (4) reconcile the five naming surfaces (finishing, ponder, CLAUDE.md, README, using-hyper); (5) gated sweep + minor bump.

Rejected: deleting review-implementation outright (loses on-demand re-verification; 10+ external references need semantic rewrites; ponder's authorized-dispatcher list would dangle) and copy-don't-centralize (duplicating STOP/checklist into both consumers recreates the drift rot). Rejected: review-implementation staying the mainline owner of the reviewer dispatch (splits the GAPS FOUND gap-fix loop away from the executor machinery in executing-plans and adds a 1,175-line skill load at the fullest-context moment).

## Architecture
Reconciled chain: brainstorming -> executing-plans (per-task loop; completion = reviewer gate -> checklist -> STOP) -> user manual validation -> finishing-a-development-branch. review-implementation = off-mainline utility (like writing-plans). Affected: skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md, skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md, skills/review-implementation/SKILL.md, commands/review-implementation.md, agents/reviewer.md, skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md, commands/finish-branch.md, skills/ponder/SKILL.md, agents/ponder.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md, .claude-plugin/plugin.json.

## Architecture Impact
- [ ] Creates a new component/module — NO
- [x] Changes public interface of existing component — YES (mainline chain visibly changes; review-implementation's role changes)
- [x] Adds/removes cross-component dependency — YES (executing-plans -> ponder edge added; phantom executing-plans -> review-implementation edge removed; ponder authorized-dispatcher list changes)
- [ ] Creates new request path through 2+ components — NO (ponder UPDATE path relocated, not created)
- [x] Moves responsibility between components — YES (end-of-epic gate ownership -> executing-plans)

Result: 3 boxes checked. /intuition was offered and deferred (architect: six-agent review + same-day investigator inventory with full line-level reference map constitute the evidence an audit would gather; ADRs checked, no contradiction).

## Provenance
None because idea-first entry: scope originates from the 2026-07-07 critical review (cluster 2, findings F1/F8/F10 of the mainline and architecture reviews) plus the same-day codebase-investigator inventory; no upstream plan document governs this area.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Four files disagree about what happens after the last task of an epic: executing-plans dispatches agents/reviewer.md and hands straight to finish-branch; review-implementation claims to be the mainline step (citing a nonexistent executing-plans Step 5); finishing-a-development-branch gates on review-implementation by name five times and never mentions the reviewer agent; CLAUDE.md/using-hyper document one chain while README's example shows the other. Consequences: the architecture checklist -> ponder UPDATE loop silently never runs on the mainline; sessions run double review or single review by accident of which file they read; finish-branch's prerequisites reference a step the upstream skill no longer produces. Observed live in the bd-854 session (2026-07-07).

### Research Findings
**Codebase (investigator inventory, line-verified):**
- review-implementation/SKILL.md 1,175 lines: unique load-bearing content is ONLY Step 4 STOP + plan-impact (L579-619) and Step 5 architecture checklist -> ponder (L623-679); ~380 lines of examples; three dead resources/ links (L1164-1169, directory does not exist); stale caller claim at L1136
- agents/reviewer.md 326 lines: verdict contract single-sourced in loop-interfaces.md L67-82; per-task extraction (L48: goal, success criteria, implementation checklist, key considerations, anti-patterns) predates two-tier specs; epic-level extraction (L23-28) already carries Success Criteria + Anti-Patterns
- executing-plans completion L224-247: reviewer dispatch + gap-fix loop present; no STOP, no checklist, hands to finish-branch at L245
- finishing-a-development-branch L28/34/43/464 + commands/finish-branch.md:7 gate on review-implementation; zero references to the reviewer agent
- ponder/SKILL.md: UPDATE input contract L67-79 (review-implementation's L645-662 template already conforms); dispatch-is-approval rule L336 names review-implementation; 7 reference lines total + agents/ponder.md:3
- ADR check: ADR-001/002/003 read in full; no contradiction (ADR-003's guard cluster is the per-task loop, not the end-of-epic gate; ADR-002 requires only that PLAN-IMPACT prefix conventions survive relocation)
**External:**
- None because this is a plugin-internal workflow reconciliation with no external dependencies.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which skill owns the end-of-epic gate? | executing-plans (its completion section) | review-implementation demoted to utility; gap-fix loop stays with executor machinery; one fewer mandatory load at fullest-context moment |
| Fix reviewer.md two-tier vocabulary here? | Yes, in scope | The canonized engine ships spec-accurate; F8 resolved in this epic |
| Where do the 5 questions live? | Single-sourced in architecture-impact-check.md, both tenses | F10 resolved; both call sites cite; no restatement anywhere |
| Delete review-implementation entirely? | No (implicit in seam-owner choice) | Retained as on-demand re-verification utility; external references get wording updates, not semantic rewrites |
| /intuition after 3-YES impact check? | Proceed without | Recorded offered-and-deferred |

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Pattern-file-first surgical reconciliation (chosen)
**What it is:** Single-source the checklist, rewrite the two consumers, realign the engine, sweep the naming surfaces.
**Investigation:** Investigator inventory classified all 1,175 lines of review-implementation; only ~95 lines are unique and load-bearing; ponder's input contract already matches the template being relocated.
**Pros:** Fixes F1+F8+F10 in one epic; no duplication created; matches lived pipeline behavior; ADR-clean.
**Cons:** Touches 13 files — mitigated by task-per-surface decomposition and a gated sweep.
**Chosen because:** The unique content is small and portable; the duplication is the disease, so the cure must not copy.

#### 2. Delete review-implementation outright (rejected)
**What it is:** Remove the skill + command; reviewer agent is the only review anywhere.
**Why explored:** Maximal simplification, consistent with the teardown epic's spirit.
**Investigation:** Reference map shows 10+ external naming sites incl. ponder's authorized-dispatcher rule and analyzing-test-effectiveness's flow.
**Pros:** Fewest files at rest.
**Cons:** Loses on-demand re-verification (post-gap-fix re-check, auditing externally-executed epics); forces semantic rewrites at every reference site.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** The utility role is real and cheap to keep at ~150 lines.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** The utility goes unused for several months of epics and its references rot again.

#### 3. review-implementation stays mainline owner (rejected)
**What it is:** executing-plans hands to review-implementation, which owns reviewer dispatch + STOP + checklist.
**Why explored:** Preserves the four-step chain narrative in routing/CLAUDE.md as written.
**Investigation:** GAPS FOUND handling requires creating fix tasks and dispatching executors — machinery defined in executing-plans; the wrapper would split or duplicate that loop.
**Pros:** Named review step stays visible in the pipeline.
**Cons:** Splits the gap-fix loop across two skills; adds a mandatory skill load at end-of-epic (fullest-context moment); contradicts how the pipeline actually ran on bd-854.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Cohesion of the gap-fix loop and context economics both favor executing-plans ownership.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** executing-plans' completion section grows past ~80 lines and needs extraction anyway.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** R1-R8 files only.
**Out of scope:**
- Per-task two-stage review loop and ADR-003 guard cluster (untouchable without A/B re-baselining)
- brainstorming Step 8 / commands/execute-plan.md "spec check + code quality" shorthand mislabel (F7 — belongs to a doc-sync epic)
- analyzing-test-effectiveness's half-migrated SRE model and orphaned agent (satellites cluster epic); only its 3 naming references get minimal wording fixes here
- Context-economics dedup of the test-quality doctrine copies (economics cluster epic)
- loop-interfaces.md (verified airtight; frozen)

### Open Questions
- Exact routing-table wording for the utility row (mainline chain row shrinks; decide in-task whether re-verification gets its own row or a note on the existing row)
- Whether executing-plans' completion cites ponder's skill or dispatches the ponder agent directly (resolve in-task; ponder/SKILL.md L106-112 suggests skill-mediated dispatch — follow ponder's own contract)
notes:
GATE STATE (2026-07-07, epic bd-iaem completion — reviewer APPROVED):
Where we are: All 7 tasks closed through two-stage review (10 commits, b9aed2d..7e473a8 on epic/bd-iaem); end-of-epic reviewer APPROVED with all 9 success criteria at confidence 1.0, run under the re-keyed reviewer.md this epic produced. Version 3.21.0. Branch local-only, not pushed.

Reviewer's non-blocking note for a future hygiene pass: bd-tz73's spec mixed Changes (simple) with Boundaries (medium), which straddles reviewer.md's tier-detection heuristic.

title: RW5 T3: Create skills/portent/SKILL.md (draft/check authoring skill)
description:
Tier: medium. Executor: lead-direct (D2-AMENDED).

## Goal
Create skills/portent/SKILL.md — thin authoring skill, home is a planning-repo session, two verbs: draft (structure roadmap thinking into a conforming phase doc) and check (conformance + cold-session + contract symmetry + release alignment). Signals only.

## Why
R5: observed pain is hand-guiding doc structure every session. The skill encodes the structure once. Named portent per the blue-cantrip convention (looks at the top of the library and arranges what is coming).

## Context
Follow the house skill shape (model: skills/consider/SKILL.md): frontmatter (name, description), skill_overview, rigidity_level, quick_reference, when_to_use, the_process, critical_rules, verification_checklist, integration. Uses exact Claude Code tool names (AskUserQuestion; Read/Write/Edit). Cites schema skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md and template skills/portent/phase-doc-template.md root-relative.

## Implementation
- Frontmatter description: trigger phrasing 'Use when drafting or checking phase planning documents in a planning repo...'.
- rigidity: draft = high freedom on content, fixed on structure; check = rigid checklist.
- quick_reference: verb table + invocation patterns (/hyperpowers:portent draft [topic], /hyperpowers:portent check <path>). No verb -> AskUserQuestion.
- when_to_use: planning-repo sessions structuring multi-service phase docs; NOT for single-repo work (bd epic transport, preordain/brainstorm own it), NOT for service implementation.
- DRAFT process: (1) gather — phase goal, services, source material from conversation/notes; AskUserQuestion only for genuine unknowns; (2) structure into the template — every slice addresses all seven sections; unknowns land in Open for Design, NEVER invented as settled decisions; write in the team's voice, zero plugin vocabulary in output; (3) self-check — run the check protocol on own draft before handing over; (4) hand off with remaining gaps flagged.
- CHECK process, per slice: (a) conformance — all seven sections addressed; sections added to the schema after the doc was written are informational only (cite schema evolution rules); (b) cold-session test — could a fresh design session run from this slice + overview alone: requirements testable? settled decisions carry rationale? contracts name owners? open questions answerable downstream?; (c) contract symmetry across slices — every Consumes has an exposing owner and vice versa; owners match; contracts aligned to deployable units; (d) release alignment — framing consistent with dependency ordering; lockstep deploys named on BOTH sides. Output: per-slice findings report, severities GAP / ASYMMETRY / INFO, each finding cites doc section and proposes a concrete edit. Signals only.
- critical_rules: (1) signals only — flag and propose, never decide; (2) never writes service repos; (3) no task-tracker artifacts — output is the document; (4) never invent settled decisions — unknown goes to Open for Design; (5) generated docs carry zero plugin vocabulary; (6) schema evolution is additive-only — never edit section meanings while drafting (cite schema); (7) AskUserQuestion for questions. Common-excuses block mapping each rationalization to its rule.
- integration: schema + template cited; a slice is the input a service-repo design session brainstorms from; oversized slice -> initiative input to /hyperpowers:preordain; check verb front-loads contract coherence while the base-view review skill is deferred.

## Verification (gates — abort on failure)
- rg -q 'skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md' skills/portent/SKILL.md || ABORT
- rg -q 'skills/portent/phase-doc-template.md' skills/portent/SKILL.md || ABORT
- rg -q 'AskUserQuestion' skills/portent/SKILL.md || ABORT
- rg -qE '\bTask tool\b|TodoWrite|SendMessage|team_name' skills/portent/SKILL.md && ABORT (exact-tool-name rule; no orchestration surface)
- rg -qE '[0-9]+ tasks|[0-9]+ min|exceed ~?[0-9]' skills/portent/SKILL.md && ABORT
- Frontmatter has name: portent and a description line || ABORT

## Boundaries
No brainstorm-side ingestion text (RW3/bd-uyc owns that). No plan-impact notices (RW4/bd-erq). No base-view review verb (deferred per D1). Skill signals; user decides.
close_reason: Skill created; citation, tool-name, threshold, and frontmatter gates all green

title: Task 4: Rewrite brainstorming Steps 6-8 for upfront task model + sizing gate
description: Replace brainstorming's single-task creation with full upfront task tree, add sizing gate that escalates to grand planner, batch SRE review, fix contradicting rationalizations.
design:
## Goal
Rewrite brainstorming Steps 6-8 and related sections to support the new execution model: all tasks created upfront with simple/medium classification, batch SRE review, sizing gate that escalates to grand planner.

## Why
Brainstorming currently creates only the first task and defers rest to execution. The new model plans all tasks upfront. Also, brainstorming needs a sizing gate to detect when scope is too large and escalate to the grand planner. The contradicting rationalizations ("Can plan all tasks upfront — Plans become brittle") must be fixed.

## Effort Estimate
20-25 minutes (modify Steps 6-8, quick_reference, critical_rules, key_principles)

## Context
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md — current file, Steps 1-5 stay unchanged, Steps 6-8 need rewriting
- Step 8 handoff was already updated in bd-bjr to describe the new execution model — but Step 6 still says "Create ONLY first task" and Step 7 still says per-task SRE
- The sizing gate is a new addition: if scope exceeds ~10 tasks or 3-4 components, escalate to grand planner
- Task specs follow two formats: simple (Goal/Why/Changes/Verification) and medium (Goal/Why/Context/Implementation/Tests/Verification/Boundaries)
- Intuition routing was moved to grand planner level — brainstorming Step 3 friction detection and Step 4 Architecture Impact Check should remain (they're useful for leaf epics too), but the routing can note that grand planner handles initiative-level architecture analysis

## Implementation guidance
1. Read current skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md fully
2. Modify Step 6 — "Creating the Task Tree":
   - Create ALL tasks for the epic, not just the first
   - Each task must have simple/medium classification (effort estimate or explicit tag)
   - Each task follows the two-tier spec format
   - Task dependencies set in bd
   - Upper bound heuristic: if planning produces >10 tasks, this is likely too large for a single leaf epic — trigger sizing gate
3. Add sizing gate (new, between Step 5 and Step 6 or as part of Step 6):
   - Check: >10 tasks estimated? >3-4 distinct components? Multiple independent deliverables?
   - If yes: escalate to grand planner. Brainstorming work done so far becomes input.
   - If no: proceed with task creation
   - This is a gate, not a hard block — user can override
4. Modify Step 7 — "Batch SRE Review":
   - SRE refinement runs ONCE against the full task tree
   - Not per-task, not per-first-task
   - Can suggest: splitting tasks, adding tasks, reordering, strengthening criteria
5. Step 8 handoff already updated in bd-bjr — verify it's correct, adjust if needed
6. Fix quick_reference table rows 6-8
7. Fix critical_rules: change "Create ONLY first task" to "Create complete task tree"
8. Fix key_principles: change "One task only" to "Complete task tree upfront"
9. Fix common rationalizations: remove/update "Can plan all tasks upfront" contradiction
10. Keep Steps 1-5 completely intact (Socratic questioning, research, design, architecture impact)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO modifying Steps 1-5 (Socratic questioning, research, design, architecture impact are unchanged)
- ❌ NO removing the Architecture Impact Check (it's useful for leaf epics too)
- ❌ NO removing friction detection from Step 3 (still useful for leaf epics)
- ❌ NO hard-blocking the sizing gate (it's a gate the user can override, not a wall)

## Edge Cases

**Brainstorming called directly by user (not from grand planner):**
- Full Socratic flow, then task creation, then sizing gate check
- If sizing gate triggers: offer to escalate to grand planner with work done so far

**Brainstorming called from grand planner with near-complete requirements:**
- Brainstorming assesses requirement confidence and adapts (skips questions where confident)
- This is NOT a rigid mode — brainstorming uses judgment

**Sizing gate triggers but user overrides:**
- User says "proceed anyway" — brainstorming creates the full task tree
- Note in epic that sizing gate was triggered but overridden

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Step 6 creates complete task tree (not "ONLY first task")
- [ ] Task specs use two-tier format (simple/medium) with effort estimates
- [ ] Sizing gate present: checks task count and component count, offers grand planner escalation
- [ ] Step 7 describes batch SRE review (once against full plan, not per-task)
- [ ] quick_reference table rows 6-8 updated
- [ ] critical_rules updated: no "Create ONLY first task" rule
- [ ] key_principles updated: no "One task only" principle
- [ ] "Can plan all tasks upfront" rationalization removed or reversed
- [ ] Steps 1-5 unchanged (verified: diff shows no changes in lines before Step 6)

title: Task 6: version bump 3.28.0 + epic-wide consistency sweep
description: Bump plugin.json to 3.28.0; run the epic's full grep sweep across the three rewritten files and fix residue
design:
## Goal
Bump the plugin version to 3.28.0 and prove the three rewritten managing-bd-tasks files satisfy the epic's Success Criteria via the full grep sweep.

## Why
Version bump is mandatory before the final push of any skills/ change (CLAUDE.md; minor bump for a teaching-content rewrite). The sweep is the epic's Success Criteria made mechanical — it catches cross-task residue the per-task greps missed. This task runs last, after Tasks 3, 4, and 5.

## Changes
- `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`: "version": "3.27.0" -> "3.28.0" (current value verified 2026-07-10)
- Run the sweep below; fix any residual hits, but only within skills/managing-bd-tasks/ (three files). Hits outside those files are out of scope — report them in the final message instead of fixing.

## Verification
Each sweep grep must return zero hits:
- `grep -rnE "## (Success|Acceptance) Criteria|Definition of Done" skills/managing-bd-tasks/`
- `grep -rn "dep tree" skills/managing-bd-tasks/`
- `grep -rn '\^bd-' skills/managing-bd-tasks/`
- `grep -rn "status in-progress\|status=in-progress" skills/managing-bd-tasks/` (the epic-SC shape — commands only. Prose "in-progress" legitimately remains in SKILL.md Op 1/Op 4 prose and the R7-preserved red_flags block; do NOT edit red_flags to chase a broader grep)
- `grep -n "in-progress" skills/managing-bd-tasks/resources/metrics-guide.md` (stricter, file-scoped: Task 5 normalizes that file fully, prose included)
- `grep -rn "dependency-patterns" skills/managing-bd-tasks/`
- `grep -rnE "2-4 hours|Acceptance Criteria|Definition of Done" skills/managing-bd-tasks/resources/task-naming-guide.md`
- `grep -rn "wc -l" skills/managing-bd-tasks/`
- `grep -rnE "deployed_at|started_at|assignee:me" skills/managing-bd-tasks/`

title: Task 5: Rewrite HOOKS.md around the two survivors
design:
## Goal
HOOKS.md accurately documents exactly two hooks with their real I/O contracts, the keep-criterion, and how to run the contract tests.

## Why
Current HOOKS.md (491 lines, 9 sections, verified) documents nine hooks including wrong I/O formats and "(blocking)" labels on hooks that never blocked; it is the primary false-confidence document. R6 of bd-854.

## Context
Current section heads at lines 3/7/243/258/326/415/429/467/487 (Overview, Hook Types, Installation, Troubleshooting, Customization, Performance, Architecture, FAQ, See Also). Target ~150 lines.

## Implementation

Rewrite with sections: Overview (two-hook plane, enforcement-vs-injection distinction); The Hooks (session-start.sh: SessionStart event, hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext output; version-bump-guard: PreToolUse Bash event, tool_name/tool_input input, hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision deny output, fail-open contract, what it guards); The Keep-Criterion (verbatim from epic bd-854: mechanical at a tool boundary + recurrent despite prompt-level instruction + cheap to verify against the real contract); Testing (run ./hooks/test/contract-test.sh; fixtures encode Claude Code's schemas, never hook assumptions); History (one paragraph: 2026-07-07 teardown, what was removed and why, reference bd-854)

## Tests
