title: Task 5: sre-task-refinement — tier-aware categories, constants citations, promotion recommendation
description: SRE stops auto-rejecting well-formed simple-tier tasks: Categories 1/3/5/7 become tier-aware, bands cite pipeline-constants, and SRE gains the power to recommend Executor: opus promotion.
design:
## Goal
skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md applies tier-appropriate criteria: simple tasks pass with a concise spec; medium tasks keep full rigor; numbers cite pipeline-constants; SRE can recommend executor promotion.

## Why
Categories 3/5/7 encode the pre-tier heavyweight format — run literally, batch mode must reject every simple task that brainstorming's own template mandates (3+ criteria, 3+ checklist items, anti-patterns section). This contradiction was a headline finding (F2) and fires on every batch review.

## Context
File: skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md (~983 lines). Sites to read before editing:
- quick_reference table (~14-31): Category rows with auto-reject conditions.
- Category 1 Granularity (~137-156): 2-5/5-15 bands and the 15-min ceiling.
- Category 3 Success Criteria (~176-193): "3+ specific, measurable criteria per task".
- Category 5 Safety (~211-225): mandatory anti-patterns checks.
- Category 7 Red Flags (~245-257): "fewer than 3 items", "No anti-patterns section", "No effort estimates".
- Batch Phase 2a/2d (~75-99): granularity and classification-consistency wording.
Reference: skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md (Task 1) for bands, ceiling, and the promotion flag.

## Implementation
1. Category 1: bands and ceiling cite pipeline-constants.md (simple 2-5, medium 5-30, ceiling 30). Update the ">15 min" breakdown triggers to ">30 min".
2. Category 3: tier-aware — simple tasks require a Verification section with at least one specific measurable outcome plus the hooks line; medium tasks keep the 3+ criteria requirement.
3. Category 5: anti-patterns are checked at the EPIC level; task-level anti-patterns required only when the task carries task-specific risk (state this explicitly); simple tasks are exempt from a mandatory task-level anti-patterns section.
4. Category 7: scope "fewer than 3 checklist items" to medium tasks; replace "No anti-patterns section" with "Epic missing anti-patterns section, or risky task missing task-level anti-patterns"; "No effort estimates" is satisfied by a simple/medium classification being present.
5. Batch Phase 2a/2d and the quick_reference table: align wording with the tier rules and cited bands.
6. Add to both batch mode scope and the single-task review process: SRE may recommend adding the "Executor: opus" promotion flag to a task per pipeline-constants.md, as a suggestion to the lead.
7. Sweep the file for remaining 5-15/15-min mentions and replace with citations.

## Additional Sites and Clarifications (ADDED BY SRE BATCH REVIEW)
8. Mainline references (epic requirement: pipeline stated identically in all six files — this file is one of the six). Recast writing-plans as the off-mainline spec repair/expansion utility everywhere it appears:
   - when_to_use ~line 39: "After hyperpowers:writing-plans creates initial plan" becomes after-brainstorming-Step-6c wording for batch mode, with single-task mode serving repaired/expanded specs.
   - when_to_use ~line 47: "use brainstorming → writing-plans" becomes "use brainstorming" (brainstorming creates the task tree).
   - Integration ~line 926: "hyperpowers:writing-plans (creates initial plan)" becomes the spec repair/expansion utility framing.
   - Integration call chain ~lines 942-944: relabel the "Plan refinement (single-task mode)" chain as the spec-repair path (writing-plans repairs a spec → single-task SRE → executing-plans); keep the batch chain as the mainline.
9. Item-7 sweep scope clarification: the sweep INCLUDES the example blocks (e.g. ~line 522 "15 min (medium)") and the Output Format template (~line 458 "5-15 min"). In examples, update numeric values/bands only — do not rewrite example substance. This is a numbers sweep, not the example rewrite the Boundaries forbid.

## Verification
- [ ] rg '5-15|>15 min|15 min' skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md returns zero
- [ ] rg 'Executor: opus' skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md returns 1 or more
- [ ] Category 3 and Category 7 text distinguishes simple from medium explicitly
- [ ] rg -c 'pipeline-constants' skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md returns 2 or more
- [ ] rg 'creates initial plan' skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md returns zero (ADDED BY SRE BATCH REVIEW)
- [ ] rg 'brainstorming → writing-plans' skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md returns zero (ADDED BY SRE BATCH REVIEW)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Boundaries
- Keep the 8-category structure and all medium-tier rigor intact
- Do NOT convert SRE to subagent dispatch in this file (bd-ope owns dispatch mechanics)
- Do NOT rewrite the Rust-flavored examples (cosmetic; out of scope)

title: Task 3: Harden version-bump-guard against verified bypasses
design:
## Goal
The three empirically verified bypasses of hooks/pre-tool-use/02-version-bump-guard.py are closed: multi-line continuation pushes, wrapper-prefixed commands, non-main/master default branches.

## Why
This is the one hook with demonstrated recurring value (forgotten version bumps documented repeatedly); the epic keeps it on condition it actually holds. R4 of bd-854.

## Context
parse_push_invocations at lines 40-77: splits on newlines at line 49, so "git push \" + continuation line leaves a trailing-backslash segment that raises ValueError in shlex.split (line 54) and hits the per-segment fail-open — push never detected. Env-assignment skip exists at lines 57-59, but wrapper tokens (time/command/env/nice/nohup) fall through the basename check at line 60. push_targets_default_branch hardcodes ("main","master") at lines 87 and 95; base fallback hardcodes origin/main at line 139. The fail-open contract (docstring lines 9-11) is inviolable: every new code path must exit 0 on error.

## Implementation
1. Top of parse_push_invocations: collapse line continuations before the segment split — normalize backslash-newline sequences to a single space
2. Before the env-assignment loop: skip leading wrapper tokens from the allowlist {time, command, env, nice, nohup}, then re-run the env-assignment skip (env VAR=x git push). Make the skip loop-until-fixpoint, not single-pass: wrappers and env assignments interleave and stack ("time env VAR=1 git push", "command nice git push"), so repeatedly strip leading wrapper tokens and env assignments until the head token is neither. Decide in-task whether wrapper flags (nice -n 10) are covered; record the decision in a code comment either way
3. Add a resolve_default_branch(repo_root) helper: git symbolic-ref --short refs/remotes/origin/HEAD, strip the "origin/" prefix; on failure return None. In push_targets_default_branch use {resolved} when available, else the {"main","master"} literal set (fail-open bias preserved)
4. Line 139 base fallback: try origin/{resolved} first, then origin/main, else exit 0 as today

## Tests
Manual repro before/after (formal fixtures land in Task 4):
- printf-built PreToolUse JSON with command "git push \\\n  origin main" in an unbumped scratch plugin repo — must DENY after fix
- same with "time git push origin main" — must DENY
- scratch repo whose origin/HEAD points at a "trunk" default branch — bare "git push" from trunk must be evaluated (denied when unbumped)

## Verification
- [ ] All three repro commands show the new behavior; single-line matrix from the review (deny unbumped main, allow feature-branch, allow bumped) unchanged
- [ ] Every new code path fails open (exception or git failure exits 0)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Boundaries
No regex-based command parsing — extend the existing shlex token flow only. No change to the deny-message wording. No new watched paths. Formal test fixtures are Task 4, not this task.

title: T1: ponder-agent path + vocab; ADR-002 amendment
description: R1 view-path fix + R4 Mode1/2 vocab reword in agents/ponder.md; R4 ADR-002 terminology amendment
design:
## Goal
Fix agents/ponder.md's bootstrap dynamic-view path (R1) and its two "Mode 1/2" vocabulary sites (R4), and append a terminology-clarifying amendment to ADR-002 (R4).

## Why
R1: the bootstrap writer must use the same canonical path (`docs/arch/views/data-flows/`) that both drift-check readers already use (agents/ponder.md L300 REVIEW, skills/intuition/SKILL.md L97/L866) — a writer that omits `data-flows/` produces views the readers never find, silently breaking drift detection. R4: the "Mode 1/2" audit vocabulary is undefined in current docs and must be retired from the ponder agent; ADR-002 uses the same phrase for a *different* concept (the governor's operating model) and must be clarified, not deleted (ADR discipline: amend, never rewrite). If skipped: bootstrap writes orphaned views, and future readers of ponder.md/ADR-002 hit undefined terms.

## Changes
- `agents/ponder.md` line 234: change `For each discovered flow, create a dynamic view in `docs/arch/views/`:` to `For each discovered flow, create a dynamic view in `docs/arch/views/data-flows/`:` (append the `data-flows/` segment so the writer matches the readers). Do NOT change L190/L268 — those are the landscape view (`docs/arch/views/landscape.c4`), legitimately not under `data-flows/`.
- `agents/ponder.md` line 3 (frontmatter `description`, inside the 2nd `<example>`): change `Context: First Intuition resolve on a Mode 2 audit. No architecture model exists yet.` to `Context: First Intuition resolve on an audit with no existing architecture model.` (removes "Mode 2"; preserves meaning).
- `agents/ponder.md` line 274 (BOOTSTRAP return template): change `Architecture model bootstrapped. Future audits will run in Mode 1.` to `Architecture model bootstrapped. Future audits will load the model.` (matches ponder SKILL's own correct wording at skills/ponder/SKILL.md L256/L390).
- `docs/arch/adr/adr-002.md`: APPEND (do not modify the existing Context/Decision/Consequences, L1-42) a new section at end of file:

  ```
  ## Amendment (2026-07-09): "Mode 1/2 operating model" terminology

  "Mode 1/2 operating model" in the Context above names the rotating
  governor's two work modes — work-mode (multiservice plans authored in
  shared planning-repo files, reviewed from a base view) and personal-mode
  (single repo, all task tracking via bd). It is unrelated to the retired
  ponder audit-mode vocabulary, which once used "Mode 1"/"Mode 2" for
  "model exists → update" versus "no model → bootstrap". That ponder
  vocabulary has been removed elsewhere in the plugin; this ADR preserves
  its own usage because it names a different concept, and ADR history is
  amended, never rewritten.
  ```

## Verification
- `grep -n "Mode 1\|Mode 2\|run in Mode" agents/ponder.md` → returns nothing.
- `grep -n "views/data-flows/" agents/ponder.md` → L234 now matches alongside L300.
- `grep -n "views/landscape.c4" agents/ponder.md` → L190/L268 still the landscape path (unchanged).
- `grep -n "Future audits will load the model" agents/ponder.md` → L274 matches.
- `grep -n "Amendment" docs/arch/adr/adr-002.md` → the appended section exists; original Context (L6-22, including the "Mode 1/2 operating model" clause) is unchanged.

title: Task 1: SKILL.md Op 1 split rewrite + mandatory single-task SRE step
description: Op 1 + payment example: two-tier split specs, 30-min-ceiling framing, parent-epic linking, mandatory single-task SRE dispatch, dep-tree fix
design:
## Goal
Rewrite SKILL.md Operation 1 (splitting tasks mid-flight) and the payment-split example so splits produce two-tier, under-ceiling specs that are mandatorily SRE-reviewed before work resumes.

## Why
Op 1 is where split specs are born. Today it teaches the dead "## Success Criteria" format (epic R1), hour-scale subtasks violating the 30-minute ceiling (R5), a `bd dep tree` recipe that is childless on bd 0.50.3 (R4), and no review of the new specs (R2 — immutable: split-created specs are never unreviewed). Tasks 2 and 3 edit other regions of this same file and are sequenced after this task.

## Context
Read before starting:
- skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md — simple tier (Goal, Why, Changes, Verification with the standing "Pre-commit hooks passing" final line).
- skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md — bands (simple 2-5 min, medium 5-30 min) and the 30-minute hard ceiling. Cite, never restate.
- skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md:30 and :40 — single-task mode exists for off-mainline specs; :53 — the skill is loaded BY a dispatched review subagent.
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:344-364 — the dispatch shape to mirror (fresh blocking subagent, general-purpose, bypassPermissions, no model override).
- Regions owned by this task in skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md: quick_reference split row (:17), Operation 1 (:44-136), example 2 payment split (:465-541). Line numbers verified 2026-07-10 — anchor edits on content, not line numbers.

## Implementation
1. Reframe Op 1's trigger prose: "Task in-progress but turns out too large" stays, but the example framing changes from "8+ hours of work" to ceiling terms (work is clearly beyond the 30-minute hard ceiling in skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md).
2. Rewrite the three subtask bd create commands in the auth example with simple-tier bodies (Goal, Why, Changes, Verification ending with "Pre-commit hooks passing"). Keep the auth scenario content (endpoints, session, hashing) but each subtask is a bounded 5-30-minute unit. Keep --description one-liners.
3. Replace the dep-tree view (anchor: `bd dep tree bd-5`): after blocking deps are added, link each subtask to the ORIGINAL TASK'S PARENT EPIC (`bd dep add bd-12 <epic-id> --type parent-child`; the epic comes from bd show bd-5's PARENT field), then enumerate with `bd list --parent <epic-id> --all -n 0`.
4. Step 3 close-out: drop the "## Success Criteria" checkbox block from the parent's closing design update; keep the Status summary listing completed work + subtask IDs; keep `bd close bd-5 --reason "Split into bd-12, bd-13, bd-14"`.
5. Add a NEW required step between dependency setup and "work on subtasks": mandatory SRE review. Insert this block (adapt surrounding prose minimally):

   **SRE review of the new specs (REQUIRED — split-created specs are never unreviewed)**

   Dispatch one fresh blocking subagent covering ALL new subtasks:

   ```
   Agent tool:
     subagent_type: "general-purpose"
     mode: "bypassPermissions"
     prompt: |
       Load the skill hyperpowers:sre-task-refinement with the Skill tool and
       run its SINGLE-TASK MODE against <new-task-ids>.
       Inputs: bd show <each new task id>; bd show <parent epic id> for epic
       requirements context.
       You may strengthen the specs directly via bd update (preserve existing
       sections; never insert placeholders). Do not create, close, or
       re-classify tasks.
       Return: per-task verdict (APPROVE / NEEDS REVISION) with one-line
       reasons and an exact list of bd updates applied.
   ```

   Do not pass a model override. Apply NEEDS REVISION fixes before starting any subtask. This step is not optional — an unreviewed split spec is the anti-pattern this operation exists to prevent.
6. Renumber Op 1's steps after the insertion; keep the ready/in_progress closing flow (statuses already underscored here — keep them so).
7. Example 2 (payment split, anchor "Implement payment processing"): reframe hour-scale estimates into ceiling terms; in the correction, show the first subtask bd create with a full simple-tier body (the remaining creates may stay abbreviated with ...), add the parent-epic parent-child linking and the mandatory SRE dispatch step (one summary line each — the full block lives in Op 1), and update the "What you gain" list (bounded reviewable tasks, clean resume points, no marathon sessions — remove multi-day/hour framing).
8. quick_reference split row (anchor "| Split task |"): key-command cell becomes "Create subtasks (two-tier specs), add deps, SRE-review, close parent".

## Verification
- `grep -n "dep tree" skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md` → zero hits in Op 1 / example 2 regions (hits in Ops 3-8 remain; Task 3 owns them)
- `grep -nE "## (Success|Acceptance) Criteria" skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md` → zero hits within Op 1 and example 2 regions (Op 2 / examples 1&3 hits remain; Task 2 owns them)
- `grep -n "hour" skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md` → zero hits FILE-WIDE (deterministic: verified 2026-07-10 that every current "hour" hit — :48 and :471-:526 — lies inside the Op 1 / example 2 regions this task owns; no other task introduces the word)
- `grep -n "SINGLE-TASK MODE" skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md` → at least 1 hit inside Op 1
- Live-check the new recipes read-only against this repo's bd: `bd list --parent bd-x7z3 --all -n 0` returns 5 children; `bd show bd-fnd8` shows a PARENT field
- Pre-commit hooks passing

## Boundaries
Only skills/managing-bd-tasks/SKILL.md, and only the quick-ref split row, Operation 1, and example 2 regions. Do not touch Op 2 or examples 1/3 (Task 2), Ops 3-8 recipes, quick-ref metrics row, or resources block (Task 3). Do not edit sre-task-refinement, writing-plans, spec-templates, or pipeline-constants.

title: Satellite repairs: execution contract fixes
description: Make every documented pipeline contract match the hybrid model: SRE vocabulary+authority, task-closure ownership, dispatch task-ID, promotion authority, finish-branch ordering, writing-plans claims, Stage-1 mislabel, bd dep-type fix.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- R1: sre-task-refinement's Step 4 update templates and checklist items reference only two-tier task sections (skills/common-patterns/spec-templates.md) and epic-level sections — no Effort Estimate / per-task Success Criteria / Implementation Checklist / Key Considerations references remain.
- R2: One batch-SRE authority statement governs all three sites (brainstorming Step 7 dispatch text, sre-task-refinement batch section, its Step 4 + Category 1): may strengthen specs via bd update; may NOT create, close, or re-classify tasks; structural suggestions go in the report.
- R3: Task closure has a single owner, stated identically in agents/executor.md and executing-plans (owner choice is Open for Design).
- R4: All four executing-plans dispatch templates (initial + three re-dispatch) guarantee the executor receives the task ID.
- R5: pipeline-constants' promotion entry defers to executing-plans' capability/cosmetic classification — one authority, no contradiction.
- R6: finishing-a-development-branch verifies tests BEFORE closing the epic, and the discard option leaves honest bd state (mechanism Open for Design).
- R7: writing-plans' integration claims match executing-plans' actual startup-only routing, and the approval-gate relationship is stated consistently on both sides of that seam.
- R8: brainstorming's Step 8 handoff text and commands/execute-plan.md describe the two-stage review accurately (Stage 1 = lead epic-coherence; Stage 2 = spec-match + code quality).
- R9: Documented bd dep commands produce gating dependencies: brainstorming SKILL.md:338, brainstorming examples.md:110-111, preordain SKILL.md:194 switch off the non-gating "--type blocking" syntax.
- R10: plugin.json minor bump before final push.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] `grep -rn "Effort Estimate\|Implementation Checklist\|Key Considerations" skills/sre-task-refinement/` returns zero (including negation-list mentions; per-task "Success Criteria" phrasing replaced by Verification vocabulary)
- [ ] `grep -rn -- "--type blocking" skills/ commands/ agents/` returns zero
- [ ] All four executing-plans dispatch templates contain a `Task: <bd-task-id>` line
- [ ] `grep -n "bd close" agents/executor.md` returns zero; executing-plans states exactly one closure owner (lead, after Stage 2 PASS)
- [ ] pipeline-constants promotion item 3 defers to executing-plans' CONCERNS classification (no unconditional CONCERNS promotion)
- [ ] finishing-a-development-branch: test verification precedes epic close in step order; discard path records a bd note and performs no close
- [ ] writing-plans integration section claims startup-only routing by executing-plans; executing-plans' inline mid-flight gap-fix authoring (L253) unchanged
- [ ] brainstorming Step 8 handoff and commands/execute-plan.md describe Stage 1 = lead epic-coherence check, Stage 2 = reviewer spec-match + code quality
- [ ] plugin.json bumped to 3.22.0
- [ ] ./hooks/test/contract-test.sh passes (regression check; hooks untouched)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing on all commits

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO new common-patterns files or sections for the reconciled contracts (scope: Approach 2 rejected — exceeds epic Boundaries; the SRE subagent loads its own skill file and needs in-skill authority text)
- ❌ NO edits to per-task two-stage loop semantics, verdict vocabularies, or loop-interfaces.md (frozen: ADR-003 guard cluster — changes require A/B re-baselining)
- ❌ NO deleting a contradiction without stating the canonical rule at that site (R2 requires one governing statement; delete-only re-accumulates drift)
- ❌ NO retaining `bd close` in agents/executor.md as a fallback (dual closure owners IS the resume-hole bug being fixed — review F3)
- ❌ NO `--type blocking` in any documented bd dep command (verified live 2026-07-07: stored but does not gate readiness)
- ❌ NO touching agents/reviewer.md or completion-gate content (epic bd-iaem's scope, already done)

## Approach
Conform-to-canonical surgical edits. Each contested contract already has a settled canonical site: brainstorming's Step 7 dispatch text for batch-SRE authority, executing-plans for the Stage-1 definition, the CONCERNS capability/cosmetic classification, and closure ownership, and positional `bd dep add` for gating dependencies. Non-canonical sites are edited to match, citing the authority where repo convention supports it (pipeline-constants L61 pattern: cite, never restate). No new files; blast radius stays inside the epic's In-scope list.

The one substantive rewrite this forces: sre-task-refinement's "Breaking Down Large Tasks" section (L406-445) currently instructs direct `bd create` splits, which the settled authority forbids — it becomes a report-recommendation section. Alternatives rejected: extracting shared contracts to common-patterns (drift-proof but exceeds Boundaries), delete-only contradiction removal (fails R2's one-authority requirement).

## Architecture
Seven seams across ten files (current line refs verified 2026-07-08):
- A. SRE vocabulary+authority: skills/sre-task-refinement/SKILL.md (Step 4 L354-390; refs L189, L250, L266-267, L475, L500, L920-924, L981; examples L591-L833; batch scope L138; L55; Category 1 L158-163; Breaking Down L406-445)
- B. Closure+task-ID: agents/executor.md (L26 close removed; L18, L45-48 consume dispatched ID) + skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md (templates L77-89, L143-155, L167-176, L189-197 gain Task: line; L91 adjusted; L136 sole closure owner; checklist L349)
- C1. Promotion: skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md L49-51 defers to executing-plans L138-140 classification
- C2. Finish-branch: skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md restructure — verify tasks closed → verify tests → integration choice → merge/MR/keep close epic; discard notes without closing (Step 1 L47-74, Step 2 L77-100, Option 4 L198-220)
- D1. writing-plans seam: skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md L39, L478 + executing-plans startup-routing lines (L33, L53)
- D2. Stage-1 relabel: skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md L403-404 + commands/execute-plan.md L7
- D3. Dep-type: skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md L338, examples.md L110-111, skills/preordain/SKILL.md L194
- E. Version: .claude-plugin/plugin.json 3.21.0 → 3.22.0

## Architecture Impact
(Step 4 result — questions per skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md)

- Q1 new component: NO
- Q2 public interface change: YES (lead↔executor dispatch contract: Task-ID line added; executor lifecycle loses close step)
- Q3 cross-component dependency added/removed: NO (executing-plans→writing-plans edge remains; only its documented trigger corrected to startup-only)
- Q4 new request path (2+ components): NO
- Q5 responsibility moved: YES (task closure executor→lead; task-splitting SRE-direct→report-recommendation)

Result: 2 YES. /intuition was offered and deferred — the 2026-07-07 six-agent review audited exactly these seams and produced the findings (F2-F10) this epic implements; initiative-level check deferred on the same grounds.

## Provenance
None because direct in-session input: the 2026-07-07 critical review (mainline-pipeline reviewer findings F2-F7, F9, F10) plus the live bd dep-type discovery during epic bd-854; no governing plan document.

## Boundaries
**In scope:** skills/sre-task-refinement, skills/brainstorming (SKILL + examples: Step 7 dispatch, Step 8 handoff, L338 dep line), skills/executing-plans (dispatch templates + closure line only), agents/executor.md, skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md, skills/finishing-a-development-branch, skills/writing-plans, commands/execute-plan.md, skills/preordain (L194 dep line only), CLAUDE.md + README.md (Stage-1 gloss lines only — folded in at the Step 8 gate per user decision 2026-07-08), commands/finish-branch.md (ordering list only — folded in during task verification).
**Out of scope:** agents/reviewer.md and the completion-gate content (epic bd-iaem's, done); SRE category CONTENT (only vocabulary/authority change); per-task two-stage loop semantics (ADR-003 guard cluster — changes require A/B re-baselining); verdict vocabularies / loop-interfaces.md (frozen); everything in the quality-line and architecture-line epics.

## Dependencies
Depends on: none — first epic of the satellite-repair initiative.

## Settled Decisions
- Batch-SRE authority direction: strengthen-in-place via bd update is ALLOWED, create/close/re-classify is NOT (matches brainstorming's dispatch text; the other two sites conform to it). Rejected: suggestions-only — the strengthen-in-place value was demonstrated live in the bd-854 and bd-iaem SRE runs.
- Dep-type fix uses bd's default gating type (positional `bd dep add A B`); verified live 2026-07-07 that "--type blocking" is stored but does not gate readiness.
- Stage-1 relabel direction: executing-plans' definition is canonical (Stage 1 = epic coherence only); handoff texts conform to it, not vice versa.
- /intuition offered at initiative level and deferred (3-YES impact check; the six-agent review served the audit function).

## Open for Design
- Task-closure owner → RESOLVED (2026-07-08 brainstorm): lead closes after Stage 2 PASS; executor.md close step removed. Protects bd-status-driven resume (executing-plans L34, L226-229) from the closed-but-unfixed skip (review F3).
- finish-branch discard mechanism → RESOLVED (2026-07-08 brainstorm): epic close moves after the integration choice; merge/MR/keep close it, discard never closes and records a bd note. No reopen dance; state always matches reality.
- Task-ID guarantee location → RESOLVED (2026-07-08 brainstorm): `Task: <bd-task-id>` line in all four dispatch templates; spec templates stay ID-free (no stale-ID risk on bd update). Matches the convention the lead carried manually all session.

## Context for Brainstorming
Line-verified sites from the 2026-07-07 review, RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-08 during this brainstorm (see Architecture section for current refs). Every epic claim confirmed still live; drift at most a few lines. The lead has ported the task-ID practice manually all session (dispatch prompts carried "Task: bd-X" lines) — that convention worked and is the R4 shape adopted.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The 2026-07-07 six-agent critical review found the documented pipeline contracts contradict the hybrid execution model at seven seams. These are not cosmetic: dual closure ownership creates a resume hole that skips failed tasks; missing task-ID input breaks three executor obligations; non-gating dep syntax silently voids execution ordering.

### Research Findings
**Codebase (investigator sweep, 2026-07-08):**
- All ten cited sites confirmed live; nothing fixed by intervening epics bd-854/bd-iaem; line drift ≤ a few lines per site
- sre-task-refinement contradicts itself: L138 says batch SRE "does not directly modify the plan" while L55/L158-163/L349-403/L406-445 instruct direct bd update/create
- executing-plans L136 already assigns closure to the lead — executor.md L26 is the conflicting second owner
- Neither spec template carries an ID field; no dispatch template carries a Task: line
- pipeline-constants L50-51 authorizes promotion on any CONCERNS; executing-plans L140 forbids it for cosmetic-class
- finishing-branch Option 4 contains zero bd commands; epic closed at L72 stays closed after discard
- writing-plans L478/L39 claim mid-execution invocation; executing-plans routes to it only at startup (L33, L53) and authors gap-fixes inline (L253)

**External:**
- None because this is a documentation-contract repair inside the plugin with no external dependencies.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Task-closure owner (R3)? | Lead closes after Stage 2 PASS | executor.md loses bd close; executing-plans L136 sole owner; resume hole closed |
| Discard honest-state mechanism (R6)? | Close epic after integration choice | finishing-branch restructure: tests → choice → close on merge/MR/keep; discard notes, never closes |
| Task-ID guarantee location (R4)? | Dispatch-template line | `Task: <bd-task-id>` in all four templates; spec templates stay ID-free |
| Approach? | Conform-to-canonical surgical edits | No new files; canonical site per seam; cite-don't-restate where convention allows |
| Run /intuition on 2-YES impact? | Defer | Six-agent review already audited these exact seams |

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Conform-to-canonical surgical edits (chosen)

**What it is:** Edit non-canonical sites to match the settled canonical site per seam; cite authority where repo convention supports it. No new files.

**Investigation:** Investigator confirmed every seam has exactly one plausible canonical site already settled by preordain or this session; pipeline-constants L61 shows the existing cite-don't-restate convention.

**Pros:** Blast radius stays inside epic Boundaries; each edit verifiable by grep; no new drift surfaces.

**Cons:** Contracts remain stated in-skill (a future fourth site could restate one) — mitigated by citation lines pointing at the canonical site.

**Chosen because:** Matches R1-R9's shape exactly; every requirement names sites to conform, not structures to invent.

#### 2. Extract shared contracts to common-patterns (rejected)

**What it is:** New common-patterns sections (authority, closure, task-ID) with all sites citing them.

**Why explored:** Best long-term drift prevention; matches pipeline-constants precedent.

**Investigation:** SRE subagent loads its own skill file — needs readable in-skill authority text regardless; nearby files (loop-interfaces, verdict vocabularies) are frozen.

**Pros:** Single source of truth per contract.

**Cons:** Exceeds epic Boundaries; adds files mid-repair; partial extraction (SRE text stays in-skill anyway) yields two-place statements — the drift shape being fixed.

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Scope creep past the epic's In-scope list for marginal gain over citation lines.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** A fourth site restates one of these contracts, or a future epic opens common-patterns for structural work.

#### 3. Delete-only contradiction removal (rejected)

**What it is:** Remove the contradicting lines without designating canonical statements.

**Why explored:** Smallest possible diff.

**Investigation:** R2 explicitly requires one governing statement across three sites.

**Pros:** Fast.

**Cons:** Ambiguity where authority text once stood; re-drift guaranteed.

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Fails R2 on its face.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Never — requirement-level conflict.

### Scope Boundaries
See ## Boundaries above (unchanged from preordain; verified still accurate against the 2026-07-08 investigation).

### Open Questions
- None because all three Open-for-Design items were resolved this session (see Key Decisions Made) and remaining choices are wording-level, bounded by each task spec's verified Changes list.

title: Task 7: Stage-1 relabel in handoff texts + gating dep syntax sweep
description: Fix two-stage review descriptions in brainstorming Step 8 and execute-plan.md; switch all documented bd dep commands off non-gating --type blocking (R8, R9)
design:
## Goal
brainstorming's Step 8 handoff, commands/execute-plan.md, CLAUDE.md, and README.md describe the two-stage review accurately (Stage 1 = lead epic-coherence check; Stage 2 = reviewer spec-match + code quality). All documented sequencing bd dep commands use bd's default gating dependency type — zero `--type blocking` remains in skills/, commands/, agents/.

## Why
Epic bd-k9aa R8, R9. Both handoff texts gloss the two stages as "spec check + code quality" — that describes only Stage 2; executing-plans' canonical definition makes Stage 1 a lead epic-coherence check. And `--type blocking` was verified live (2026-07-07) to be stored by bd but NOT gate readiness — every documented use silently voids execution ordering.

## Changes
All sites verified 2026-07-08 by grep sweep (items 1-5 cover ALL occurrences of `--type blocking` in skills/, commands/, agents/; items 6-9 cover ALL occurrences of the Stage-1 gloss in CLAUDE.md and README.md, folded in at the Step 8 gate per user decision):

1. skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md L403: replace "4. After each task: lead runs a two-stage review (spec check + code quality)," with "4. After each task: lead runs a two-stage review (Stage 1: lead epic-coherence check; Stage 2: reviewer spec-match + code quality),".
2. commands/execute-plan.md L7: replace "The lead runs two-stage per-task review (spec check + code quality review), then proceeds to the next task." with "The lead runs two-stage per-task review (Stage 1: epic-coherence check by the lead; Stage 2: spec-match + code quality by a fresh reviewer), then proceeds to the next task."
3. skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md L338: replace "`bd dep add bd-[task-B] bd-[task-A] --type blocking` for tasks that must run in sequence." with "`bd dep add bd-[task-B] bd-[task-A]` (bd's default dependency type — it gates readiness) for tasks that must run in sequence."
4. skills/brainstorming/examples.md L110-111: replace both lines "bd dep add bd-[t2] bd-[t1] --type blocking" / "bd dep add bd-[t3] bd-[t2] --type blocking" with "bd dep add bd-[t2] bd-[t1]" / "bd dep add bd-[t3] bd-[t2]" (add trailing comment "# default type gates readiness" to the first).
5. skills/preordain/SKILL.md L194: replace "bd dep add bd-[epic-B] bd-[epic-A] --type blocking" with "bd dep add bd-[epic-B] bd-[epic-A]" (the L193 comment "# Epic B cannot start until Epic A is complete" stays).
6. README.md L19: replace "runs two-stage review (spec + code quality) after each task" with "runs two-stage review (Stage 1: epic coherence; Stage 2: spec-match + code quality) after each task".
7. README.md L156: replace "[Lead runs two-stage review (spec check + code quality) after each task]" with "[Lead runs two-stage review (Stage 1: epic coherence; Stage 2: spec-match + code quality) after each task]".
8. CLAUDE.md L162: replace "the lead runs a two-stage review (spec check + code quality review) before moving to the next task" with "the lead runs a two-stage review (Stage 1: epic-coherence check by the lead; Stage 2: spec-match + code quality by a fresh reviewer) before moving to the next task".
9. CLAUDE.md L197: replace "runs two-stage review (spec + code quality) after each task" with "runs two-stage review (Stage 1: epic coherence; Stage 2: spec-match + code quality) after each task".

## Verification
- `grep -rn -- "--type blocking" skills/ commands/ agents/` → zero matches (`--type parent-child` occurrences are correct and stay)
- `grep -rn "spec check + code quality\|spec + code quality" skills/ commands/ agents/ CLAUDE.md README.md` → zero matches
- `grep -n "epic-coherence" skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md commands/execute-plan.md` → 1 match in each
- Pre-commit hooks pass on the task's commit

title: Brainstorming: LikeC4 MCP context loading + architecture update checklist
description: Replace bd-based architecture context loading in brainstorming building mode with LikeC4 MCP queries. Add architecture update checklist (5 questions) and dynamic view proposal to building mode flow. Builds on bd-2ub (mode detection gate) and bd-07n (LikeC4 model format).
design:
## Goal
Two changes to the brainstorming skill's building mode path:
1. Replace bd-based architecture context loading with LikeC4 MCP tool calls
2. Add architecture update checklist and dynamic view proposal to building mode flow

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Replace architecture context loading (building mode path)

The building mode path currently uses bd commands to detect and load architecture context (lines added by bd-2ub under BUILDING MODE section). Replace with LikeC4 MCP:

CURRENT (bd-based):
```bash
bd list --label arch --type epic --status open
bd list --label arch,component --parent <epic-id>
bd show <node-id>
bd dep tree <node-id>
```

NEW (LikeC4 MCP):
```
# Check for architecture model
ls arch/*.c4 2>/dev/null

# If found, use LikeC4 MCP to load context:
# 1. read-project-summary — get overview of element kinds, tags, elements
# 2. search-element — find component matching user's reference (by title, kind, tags, or metadata)
# 3. read-element — get full details: metadata (volatility_axis, layer, stability_state), relationships, source location
# 4. find-relationships — get adjacent components (direct and indirect)

# Still load ADRs the same way:
ls doc/arch/adr-*.md 2>/dev/null
# Read ADRs that mention the component or its volatility axis
```

Update the context presentation format:
```
Architecture context loaded for [component name]:
- Volatility axis: [from element metadata.volatility_axis]
- Layer: [from element metadata.layer]
- Stability state: [from element metadata.stability_state]
- Interface contract: [from linked doc/arch/components/<name>.md]
- Adjacent components: [from find-relationships — which components this one connects to]
- Relevant ADRs:
  - ADR-NNN: [title] — [key decision and consequences]
- Data flow context: [from dynamic views involving this component, via read-view]
  - Inbound: [from upstream in dynamic views]
  - Outbound: [to downstream in dynamic views]
  - Request paths: [dynamic views this component appears in]
```

If stability_state is 'pre-fit': note in presentation that volatility axis is a hypothesis.

### 2. Update codebase-investigator dispatch

The component-scoped investigator dispatch references bd deps for boundaries. Update to reference LikeC4 relationships:

CURRENT: 'For component [name] with these boundaries: [adjacent nodes from bd deps]'
NEW: 'For component [name] with these boundaries: [adjacent components from find-relationships]'

Reference the interface contract from doc/arch/components/<name>.md instead of bd design field.

### 3. Update edge cases section

Current edge cases reference bd (no architecture graph = no bd list results). Update:
- No architecture model exists (no arch/*.c4): skip detection, proceed with normal brainstorming
- No codebase but model exists: skip flow dispatch, present architectural context only
- Multiple components referenced: load context for all matched (search-element for each)
- Component is #target (aspirational): still load context, note it's aspirational
- No ADRs found: note 'No ADRs found for this component'
- User doesn't reference any component: skip detection, proceed normally
- LikeC4 MCP not running: error with 'LikeC4 MCP server required. Run: likec4 mcp --stdio'

### 4. Add architecture update checklist (end of building mode)

After Step 6 (SRE refinement + handoff), add a new note in the building mode flow:

```
## Architecture Update Note

After inner-loop work completes, the review-implementation skill runs this checklist:

1. Did this work change a component's public interface?
2. Did this work add or remove a dependency between components?
3. Did this work create a new component or remove an existing one?
4. Did this work move responsibility from one component to another?
5. Did this work create a new request path through 2+ components?

If any answer is 'yes':
- Update the relevant .c4 file(s)
- Remove #target tag if component now matches intent
- Update linked markdown doc if interface contract changed
- If Q5 yes, propose new dynamic view
- Confirm with architect before committing

This checklist is informational in the brainstorming epic design.
The review-implementation skill enforces it after execution.
```

### 5. Add dynamic view proposal during design

In Step 3 (Presenting the Design), add a question when the design involves data flowing through 2+ existing components:

'Does this feature create a new request path through 2+ existing components? If so, it may warrant a new dynamic view in arch/views/data-flows/.'

If yes, the epic's Architecture Context section should note: 'Proposed dynamic view: [flow name]'

### 6. Update epic design template

In Step 4 (Creating the bd Epic), add Architecture Context section to the epic template:

```
## Architecture Context
- Component: [LikeC4 element name]
- Volatility axis: [from metadata]
- Layer: [from metadata]
- Stability state: [from metadata]
- Adjacent: [from relationships]
- Relevant ADRs: [linked from element]
- Key data flows: [from dynamic views involving this component]
- Proposed dynamic view: [if new request path identified]
```

### 7. Test with subagents

Test cases:
- Building mode with arch/*.c4 present → loads via LikeC4 MCP (not bd)
- Building mode with no arch/ directory → skips context loading gracefully
- Building mode references specific component → search-element finds it, read-element loads details
- MCP not running → hard fail with helpful error message
- #target component referenced → context loaded, noted as aspirational
- Pre-fit component → volatility axis noted as hypothesis
- Feature creates new request path → dynamic view proposed
- Feature doesn't create new path → no dynamic view proposal
- Architecture update checklist present in epic template
- No bd architecture commands remain in building mode path (no bd list --label arch)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Building mode loads architecture context via LikeC4 MCP (search-element, read-element, find-relationships)
- [ ] No bd architecture commands remain (no bd list --label arch, no bd show for architecture nodes)
- [ ] MCP not running → hard fail with 'Run: likec4 mcp --stdio'
- [ ] ADR loading unchanged (still reads doc/arch/adr-*.md)
- [ ] Codebase-investigator dispatch references LikeC4 relationships (not bd deps)
- [ ] Architecture update checklist (5 questions) documented in building mode flow
- [ ] Dynamic view proposal question added to design presentation (Step 3)
- [ ] Epic template includes Architecture Context section
- [ ] Edge cases updated for LikeC4 (no arch/ dir, #target components, pre-fit, MCP down)
- [ ] Tested with subagents per writing-skills skill
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ Do NOT add .c4 file parsing as MCP fallback (epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ Do NOT change thinking mode behavior (this task only touches building mode)
- ❌ Do NOT change Socratic questioning logic (only change context loading and epic template)
- ❌ Do NOT change mode detection gate from bd-2ub (additive only)
- ❌ Do NOT auto-insert ADR tradeoffs as anti-patterns (architect decides during brainstorm)

title: Gap-fix B: align refactoring-safely example-session.md with bd-issue transport
description: Reviewer gap: the linked walkthrough still creates its own bd issue with bd-456 literals, contradicting the Step 0/2/7 transport contract
design:
Classification: simple

## Goal
skills/refactoring-safely/resources/example-session.md walks through working an EXISTING refactor bd issue (gate → in_progress → commits → re-emit design + close), consistent with SKILL.md Steps 0/2/7.

A cold reader following it violates the R6 transport contract.

All verified in the working tree (branch epic/bd-sv5d):

  ```bash
  bd show <refactor-id>
  # Gate (Step 0): design field must contain both "## Diagnosis Report"
  ```

title: Rename architectural-audit to intuition — core rewrite with Brand framing
design:
## Goal
Rename skills/architectural-audit/ to skills/intuition/, rewrite SKILL.md with Brand's empirical framing, delete commands/audit-arch.md, create commands/intuition.md. This is the core rewrite — Pass 8, resolution protocol, forward audit, and model bootstrapping come in subsequent subtasks.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Rename skill directory
- mv skills/architectural-audit/ skills/intuition/

### Step 2: Rewrite SKILL.md section by section

**Frontmatter:**
- name: intuition
- description: Updated with Brand framing (empirical observation, shearing layers, git history as evidence)

**Overview:**
- Reframe from Hickey-only to Brand+Hickey: empirical observation of architecture via structured analysis
- Model describes what IS, not what should be

**Rigidity:**
- Update pass count from 7 to 7 (Pass 8 added in subtask B)
- Keep RIGID level

**Quick reference:**
- Step 0: Mode gate (Mode 1: model exists, Mode 2: no model)
- Steps 1-3: Updated pass descriptions without Löwy vocabulary

**When to use:**
- Remove '/decompose created or revised an architecture graph' — decompose is dead
- Add: 'When friction suggests structural mismatch', 'Periodic health check', 'When brainstorming suggests architectural investigation'
- Intuition is intermittent diagnostic, not part of sequential chain

**Don't use for:**
- Remove 'Creating a new decomposition (use /decompose)' — decompose is dead
- Remove 'When no architecture graph exists (run /decompose first)' — Mode 2 handles this now
- Keep: inner-loop feature work, bug fixes

**Step 0 (Mode gate):**
- Mode 1: Model exists (arch/*.c4 found) — load via LikeC4 MCP (existing behavior, updated vocabulary)
- Mode 2: No model exists — dispatch codebase-investigator for structure discovery, run passes against codebase evidence only
- Remove all #target handling (model describes what IS)
- Remove pre-fit component handling (Löwy concept)
- Remove 'run /decompose first' fallback
- Remove Löwy search terms (manager, engine, resourceAccessor, utility) from MCP queries
- Remove volatility_axis from metadata references
- Remove landscape view check referencing /decompose

**Step 1 (Gather evidence):**
- Keep codebase-investigator dispatch structure
- Remove Löwy layer references from investigation prompts
- Keep module structure, imports, co-change, call patterns, interfaces, shared state categories
- Keep Step 1b (data flow investigation)

**Passes 1-4, 6:**
- Remove Löwy vocabulary from all pass descriptions
- Remove #target handling from all passes
- Remove pre-fit component handling from all passes
- Keep Hickey simplicity framework (complection, interfaces, temporal coupling, hidden deps, state/identity)

**Pass 5 (reframed):**
- Was: 'Layer Violations' with Löwy layer rules (Manager/Engine/Resource Accessor/Utility)
- Now: 'Structural Dependency Direction' — dependencies flow from orchestration toward data/infrastructure
- Rule: upward calls from lower-abstraction to higher-abstraction modules are tensions
- Remove all Löwy layer names (Manager, Engine, Resource Accessor, Utility)
- Keep principle: one-way data flow, no upward dependencies

**Pass 7 (Dynamic View Drift):**
- Remove #target component skip
- Remove pre-fit component skip
- Keep dynamic view comparison logic

**Step 3 (Self-check + present):**
- Update report format: remove 'Target components (skipped)', remove 'Pre-fit components' sections
- Remove #target and pre-fit from model summary table
- Update resolution options: remove '/decompose to restructure' — replaced with 'ADR + bd ticket' (resolution protocol comes in subtask C, but remove /decompose now)
- Keep self-check procedure unchanged

**Examples:**
- Rewrite all examples to use Brand vocabulary
- Remove Löwy layer names from example components
- Remove #target and pre-fit from examples
- Remove /decompose references from examples
- Keep tension format and self-check demonstration

**Critical rules:**
- Remove rules about #target and pre-fit
- Remove references to /decompose
- Update pass count references
- Keep core rules: no recommendations, no severity, run all passes, self-check, etc.

**Verification checklist:**
- Remove #target and pre-fit checklist items
- Remove Löwy-specific items
- Add Mode 1/Mode 2 gate verification
- Keep core verification items

**Integration:**
- Update call chain to: /brainstorm → build → update model ↕ /intuition → find tensions → resolve → update model
- Remove /decompose from 'called by' and 'call chain'
- Update command reference from /audit-arch to /intuition
- Remove /decompose from artifacts consumed
- Intuition is intermittent diagnostic, not part of sequential chain

**Resources:**
- Remove Löwy layer rules section
- Keep Hickey framework references
- Update 'when stuck' section

### Step 3: Delete commands/audit-arch.md

### Step 4: Create commands/intuition.md
- description: Brand-framing description of empirical architecture observation
- Body: 'Use the hyperpowers:intuition skill exactly as written'

### Step 5: Verify
- grep -rn '#target' skills/intuition/SKILL.md returns zero
- grep -rn 'volatility.axis\|Manager.*Engine\|Resource.Accessor\|Utility' skills/intuition/SKILL.md returns zero (Löwy vocabulary)

- commands/audit-arch.md does not exist

## Success Criteria
- [ ] skills/architectural-audit/ does not exist
- [ ] skills/intuition/SKILL.md exists with Brand framing
- [ ] Mode 1 (model exists) and Mode 2 (no model) paths in Step 0
- [ ] Pass 5 reframed as structural dependency direction (no Löwy layer names)

- [ ] Zero Löwy vocabulary in skills/intuition/SKILL.md

- [ ] Zero pre-fit references in skills/intuition/SKILL.md

- [ ] Integration section shows revised call chain without /decompose
- [ ] When-to-use reflects intermittent diagnostic role
- [ ] All examples rewritten with Brand vocabulary
- [ ] Brand terms present: shearing layers, empirical observation, git history, rate of change

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO #target tag anywhere
- NO Löwy vocabulary as primary framing (volatility axis, Manager/Engine/Resource Accessor/Utility)
- NO /decompose references
- NO pre-fit component handling
- NO upfront force prediction language
