title: Feature: Add Pass 9 (Workaround Cascade) and Pass 10 (Mechanism Bypass) to intuition skill
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Pass 9 detects workaround cascades: code that exists to compensate rather than build value
- Pass 9 signals: special-case filtering, description/label branching, noise suppression, exception lists, adapter layers between things that should speak the same interface, git history fix clusters
- Pass 9 groups multiple workarounds in the same area into cascade clusters (single workaround is not a cascade)
- Pass 10 takes Pass 9 cascade clusters as input and probes for root cause via targeted codebase-investigator dispatch
- Pass 10 identifies mechanism bypass: a subsystem achieving the same outcome through a parallel path instead of the standard mechanism
- Pass 10 traces both standard path and bypass path, identifying the fork point
- Pass 10 skipped when Pass 9 finds zero cascades
- Pass 10 reports 'cascade detected, root cause unclear' when no bypass found
- Both passes work in Mode 1 and Mode 2 (purely structural, no model required)
- Both passes use same tension format as existing passes (Pull A / Pull B, neutral observation)
- Both passes subject to same self-check rules (no recommendation language, no severity)
- Resolution protocol presents linked Pass 9+10 findings together
- Step 1 evidence gathering updated with additional questions to feed Pass 9
- No convention file loading (CLAUDE.md, design docs) — detection is purely structural

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Pass 9 added to SKILL.md with complete detection logic, signals list, grouping rules, and output format
- [ ] Pass 10 added to SKILL.md with linked-pass input from Pass 9, codebase-investigator dispatch template, and output format including standard path, bypass path, and fork point
- [ ] Step 1 evidence gathering updated with additional codebase-investigator questions for compensating code patterns
- [ ] Step 2 updated to run Pass 9 after Pass 8, Pass 10 after Pass 9
- [ ] Step 3 self-check updated to include Pass 9/10 in keyword scanning
- [ ] Step 3 presentation template updated with Pass 9/10 counts in Audit Outcome
- [ ] Step 4 resolution protocol handles linked Pass 9+10 findings (present together, resolving bypass resolves cascade)
- [ ] Pass 9 skip condition documented (no codebase = skip)
- [ ] Pass 10 skip condition documented (zero cascades = skip)
- [ ] Mode 1 and Mode 2 behavior documented for both passes
- [ ] All existing passes unchanged (no regressions in pass 1-8 content)
- [ ] Skill follows existing pass structure conventions exactly

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO convention file loading in Pass 9 or 10 (design principle: detection is structural, not convention-aware — the workaround cascade IS the evidence)
- ❌ NO recommendation language in pass output (skill philosophy: neutral structural observation with both pulls stated equally)
- ❌ NO severity ratings on findings (skill philosophy: evidence speaks for itself)
- ❌ NO independent Pass 10 execution (Pass 10 is always guided by Pass 9 findings — blind parallel-mechanism scanning produces false positives)
- ❌ NO single-workaround cascades (one special-case filter is not a cascade — require clustering of multiple compensating patterns in same area)
- ❌ NO changes to existing passes 1-8 content (additive only — update Step 1 evidence gathering, Step 2 ordering, Step 3 presentation, Step 4 resolution)
- ❌ NO new resolution protocol options (existing 5 options sufficient — Accept, Resolve, Brainstorm, Investigate, Skip)

## Approach
Add two new analysis passes to the intuition skill following a two-phase linked detection model. Pass 9 scans Step 1 evidence for workaround cascade patterns — code that exists to compensate rather than build value. When cascades are found, Pass 10 takes them as input and dispatches targeted codebase-investigator to trace each cascade back to its root cause, typically a mechanism bypass where one subsystem achieves the same outcome through a parallel path instead of the standard mechanism.

This mirrors the actual diagnostic experience: you notice the cascade first (bugs accumulating, digging a hole), then probe to discover the bypass. The detection is purely structural — same evidence model as the existing 8 passes, no convention file loading.

Step 1 evidence gathering gets additional codebase-investigator questions to surface compensating code patterns. Step 3 presentation and Step 4 resolution protocol updated to handle linked Pass 9+10 findings.

## Architecture
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md — sole file modified
- Pass 9 section added after Pass 8 in Step 2
- Pass 10 section added after Pass 9 in Step 2
- Step 1 evidence gathering dispatch template updated
- Step 3 presentation template updated
- Step 4 resolution protocol updated for linked findings

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The intuition skill's 8 analysis passes are all structural: import graphs, model edges, co-change rates, dependency direction. They detect where code lives wrong but miss when code behaves wrong — specifically, when a subsystem builds a parallel execution path instead of using the standard mechanism, causing workaround cascades (special-case filtering, description branching, noise suppression).

In practice, this manifested as a bot system that used direct db transformations instead of the project's standard event system, causing 4+ bugs and requiring multiple workaround layers. The existing passes framed the workarounds as independent structural tensions instead of recognizing them as symptoms of a single bypass.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md — 8 existing passes, all independent, all structural
- Pass 1 (Complection) detects concerns braided together — Pass 10 detects the inverse (a concern that should be unified but is split into parallel paths)
- Existing ADR drift check catches structural disagreements, not behavioral/principle violations
- Step 1 dispatches codebase-investigator for structural + data flow evidence
- Passes 7 and 8 dispatch their own codebase-investigator (precedent for per-pass evidence gathering)
- No convention file loading anywhere in current skill

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Two-phase linked passes (Pass 9 finds cascades, Pass 10 traces to bypass) ✓

**What it is:** Pass 9 detects workaround cascades from Step 1 evidence. Pass 10 takes Pass 9 findings as input and dispatches targeted codebase-investigator to find root cause. Mirrors actual diagnostic flow: notice cascade, probe for cause.

**Investigation:**
- Discussed with user — their real diagnostic signal was cascading bugs and 'digging a hole' feeling, not reading CLAUDE.md
- Confirmed cascade-first detection matches experience across multiple cases

**Pros:**
- Mirrors actual diagnostic experience
- Pass 10 is focused by Pass 9 findings (no blind scanning)
- Produces strongest output: cascade + cause together

**Cons:**
- New precedent: first pass depending on another pass

**Chosen because:** The causal link between cascade and bypass is the key insight. Independent detection loses this.

#### 2. Two independent passes, cross-referenced in presentation ❌

**What it is:** Both run independently on Step 1 evidence. Cross-reference when both flag the same area.

**Why we looked at this:** Consistent with existing pass independence pattern.

**Investigation:**
- Evaluated whether Pass 10 could effectively detect parallel mechanisms without knowing where to look
- Concluded blind scanning for parallel mechanisms produces too many false positives

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Loses the causal link. Pass 10 without Pass 9 guidance doesn't know where to look, producing false positives.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Pass 9 proves insufficient to guide Pass 10 (i.e., cascade detection misses cases where bypass exists without visible cascade).

#### 3. Single combined pass ❌

**What it is:** One pass detecting both cascades and bypasses in one sweep.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler, captures causal relationship naturally.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Too large for a single pass. Doesn't match existing pass granularity. Harder to describe, harder to skip (Pass 10 skip condition is 'no cascades' which requires separate pass).

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** The two-pass approach creates excessive overhead in the skill file.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Pass 9 and Pass 10 detection logic, output format, skip conditions
- Step 1 evidence gathering updates
- Step 3 presentation updates
- Step 4 resolution protocol updates for linked findings

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Convention file loading (rejected by design — structural detection only)
- New resolution protocol options (existing 5 sufficient)
- Changes to passes 1-8 (additive only)

### Open Questions
- Exact threshold for cascade clustering (how many workarounds in same area = cascade?) — resolve during implementation by studying existing pass thresholds
- Whether Mode 1 model edges provide additional signal for Pass 10 fork point detection — implement Mode 2 first, add Mode 1 enhancements if obvious

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Should passes load convention files (CLAUDE.md, ADRs)? | No — the structural signal (workaround cascade) IS the evidence | Anti-pattern: NO convention file loading |
| Are these one pass or two? | Two — cascade is symptom, bypass is cause | Two-phase linked detection |
| Which is detected first? | Cascade first, then probe for bypass | Pass 9 runs first, feeds Pass 10 |
| Does cascade always come from bypass? | No — cascade stands alone if no bypass found | Pass 10 reports 'root cause unclear' when applicable |
| Should output format change for violations vs tensions? | No — keep neutral format, evidence distinguishes | Same Pull A / Pull B format |
| New resolution options needed? | No — existing 5 sufficient | No changes to resolution option list |

### Dead-End Paths

#### Convention Compliance (original Pass 9 framing)
**Why explored:** User's initial description framed this as 'load CLAUDE.md conventions and check compliance'
**Investigation:**
- Discussed what the actual diagnostic signal was
- User confirmed signal was cascading bugs and 'digging a hole' feeling, not reading CLAUDE.md
- CLAUDE.md violation was the explanation found after the fact, not the detection mechanism

**Why abandoned:** Convention loading is brittle (depends on conventions being written and current), and the real signal is structural (workaround accumulation). Structural detection works without any convention files.

#### Mechanism Duplication (original Pass 10 framing)
**Why explored:** User's initial description framed this as 'detect when a subsystem reimplements an existing mechanism'
**Investigation:**
- Discussed whether blind parallel-mechanism scanning would work
- Concluded that without knowing where to look (Pass 9 guidance), too many false positives

**Why abandoned:** Reframed as Bypass Detection guided by Pass 9 cascade findings. More focused, fewer false positives.

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'The skill rigid neutrality can obscure cases where one pull is the project own convention' → Addressed by making evidence strong enough that the right choice is obvious without the skill recommending. The cascade + bypass evidence is much stronger than a typical structural tension.
close_reason: All 12 success criteria met, reviewer approved. Pass 9 (Workaround Cascade) and Pass 10 (Mechanism Bypass) added to intuition skill.

title: Add Pass 10 (Mechanism Bypass Detection) and update Step 4 resolution protocol for linked findings
design:
## Goal
Complete all remaining epic bd-sao success criteria. Add Pass 10 (Mechanism Bypass Detection) to skills/intuition/SKILL.md with linked-pass input from Pass 9, codebase-investigator dispatch template, standard/bypass/fork-point output format. Update Step 3 self-check and Audit Outcome for Pass 10. Update Step 4 resolution protocol to present linked Pass 9+10 findings together.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Add Pass 10 section after Pass 9 in Step 2

Insert after the Pass 9 section (after the --- divider before Step 3). Follow EXACT format conventions of existing passes. Pass 10 is unique: it takes Pass 9 cascade clusters as input rather than Step 1 evidence directly.

Key elements:
- **What to look for:** Mechanism bypass — a subsystem achieving the same outcome through a parallel path instead of the standard mechanism
- **Skip if:** Pass 9 found zero cascades (no cascades to trace)
- **Linked input:** For each cascade from Pass 9, dispatch targeted codebase-investigator
- **Codebase-investigator dispatch template:** Include subsystem name and area from Pass 9, instructions to trace standard mechanism path and alternative (bypass) path, identify fork point
- **Model evidence (Mode 1 only):** Components with parallel edges achieving same outcome through different paths
- **Codebase evidence:** Parallel code paths achieving same outcome, one using standard mechanism and one using alternative
- **Root cause unclear variant:** When Pass 10 finds no bypass for a cascade, report 'cascade detected, root cause unclear' — still uses tension format with both pulls
- **Tension format:** Includes standard path, bypass path, fork point, linked Pass 9 cascade reference, cascade members from Pass 9

### 2. Update Step 3 Audit Outcome template

Add after the Pass 9 line:
```
**Mechanism bypass (Pass 10):** [count, or "skipped — no cascades" or "none — cascades without identified bypass"]
```

### 3. Update Step 4 resolution protocol for linked Pass 9+10 findings

Add a section before Model Bootstrapping explaining linked finding presentation:
- When Pass 10 identifies a bypass for a Pass 9 cascade, present them together as a linked finding
- The architect resolves the bypass (Pass 10); resolving the bypass resolves the cascade (Pass 9) automatically
- Present the cascade evidence alongside the bypass evidence so architect sees full picture
- For cascades where Pass 10 found no bypass ("root cause unclear"), present the Pass 9 cascade standalone for resolution

### 4. Verify cross-references

Check and update any remaining references that mention specific pass names or behaviors:
- Examples section: verify examples still make sense with 10 passes
- Critical rules: already updated in bd-sf8, verify Pass 10 skip condition is mentioned
- Verification checklist: already updated in bd-sf8, verify complete
- Integration section: verify agents used mentions Pass 10

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Pass 10 codebase-investigator dispatch specificity**
The dispatch template must be targeted: include the specific subsystem identified by Pass 9, the area where compensating code was found, and instructions to trace both the standard mechanism path and the alternative path. Too vague and the investigator returns generic results.

**Edge Case: Root cause unclear variant**
When no bypass found, the tension format must still use both pulls stated equally. Pull A: cascade has an unknown root cause that warrants investigation. Pull B: cascade members are independent issues, not symptoms of a single bypass.

**Edge Case: Multiple cascades from Pass 9**
Each cascade gets its own Pass 10 investigation. Dispatch one targeted codebase-investigator per cascade. Some may find bypass, others may not.

**Edge Case: Step 4 linked resolution semantics**
Resolving the bypass (Pass 10) resolves the cascade (Pass 9) because the cascade is a symptom of the bypass. But Accepting the cascade (Pass 9) does NOT skip Pass 10 investigation — the bypass may still need resolution even if the workarounds are accepted.

**Consistency: Self-check keywords**
Pass 10 uses terms like "bypass" and "architectural shortcut" in Pull B. Verify these read as structural observations, not recommendations, by checking both pulls are stated equally.

## Anti-Patterns
- Do NOT use different heading conventions than existing passes
- Do NOT modify any content in Passes 1-9 (additive only)
- Do NOT use recommendation language in Pass 10 output format
- Do NOT add convention file loading
- Do NOT allow independent Pass 10 execution (always guided by Pass 9)
- Do NOT add new resolution protocol options (existing 5 sufficient)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Pass 10 section uses exact heading conventions matching passes 1-9
- [ ] Pass 10 includes linked-pass input from Pass 9 (not independent)
- [ ] Pass 10 includes codebase-investigator dispatch template with subsystem/area from Pass 9
- [ ] Pass 10 tension format includes standard path, bypass path, and fork point
- [ ] Pass 10 has 'root cause unclear' variant documented
- [ ] Pass 10 skip condition documented (zero cascades = skip)
- [ ] Mode 1 and Mode 2 behavior documented in Pass 10
- [ ] Step 3 Audit Outcome includes Pass 10 count line
- [ ] Step 4 resolution protocol handles linked Pass 9+10 findings
- [ ] Step 4 documents that resolving bypass resolves cascade
- [ ] Existing passes 1-9 content unchanged (verify with diff)
- [ ] No recommendation language in Pass 10 output (run self-check mentally)

title: Add Pass 9 (Workaround Cascade Detection) to intuition skill
description: Add Pass 9 to the intuition skill including Step 1 evidence gathering updates. Pass 9 detects workaround cascades — code that exists to compensate rather than build value. This is the foundation that Pass 10 (Bypass Detection) will build on.
design:
## Goal
Add Pass 9: Workaround Cascade Detection to skills/intuition/SKILL.md, including updating Step 1 evidence gathering, skill metadata sections, and Step 3 presentation.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Read the full skill file
Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md to confirm current structure. Verify pass heading conventions, tension format, and metadata sections before making changes.

### 2. Update skill metadata sections

**Frontmatter description (line 3):** Add workaround cascade and mechanism bypass to the description string.

**rigidity_level (line 13):** Change '8 analysis passes' to '10 analysis passes' and 'Passes 1-7 + Pass 8' to 'Passes 1-10'.

**quick_reference table (line 21):** Change 'Run 8 analysis passes' to 'Run 10 analysis passes' and 'Passes 1-7 + Pass 8: rate-of-change mismatch' to 'Passes 1-10'.

**skill_overview (lines 7-9):** Add 'workaround cascades, mechanism bypass' to the list of things the skill examines.

**Step 2 heading (line 238):** Change 'Run 8 Analysis Passes' to 'Run 10 Analysis Passes'.

### 3. Update Step 1a evidence gathering dispatch template

Add three new items to the codebase-investigator prompt (after item 6 SHARED STATE, before the closing backticks at ~line 199):

```
7. COMPENSATING CODE PATTERNS
   - Special-case filters, exception lists, or conditional branches that handle
     one subsystem differently from all others
   - Adapter or translation layers between components that should speak the
     same interface
   - Code whose purpose is to suppress, filter, or work around effects from
     a specific code path rather than to deliver end-user value

8. FIX CLUSTERING (from git history)
   - Clusters of related fixes in the same area within short time windows
     (e.g., 3+ related commits in the same module within 2 weeks)
   - Areas where commit messages reference workarounds, fixes for side effects,
     or handling special cases
```

### 4. Add Pass 9 section after Pass 8 in Step 2

Insert after the Pass 8 section (after line ~448, before the Step 3 divider). Follow EXACT format conventions of existing passes:

```markdown
### Pass 9: Workaround Cascade Detection

**What to look for:** Code that exists to compensate rather than to build value — the structural fingerprint of accumulated workarounds. A single workaround is not a cascade; look for clusters of 2+ compensating patterns in the same area, all handling special cases for the same subsystem or code path.

**Note:** Pass 9 may flag modules also flagged by Pass 1 (Complection). This is expected — a module can braid concerns (Pass 1) AND contain compensating code (Pass 9). Pass 9 adds the cascade framing: these are not independent concerns but symptoms of accumulated workarounds.

**Model evidence (Mode 1 only):**
- Components with adapter relationships that exist solely to bridge one subsystem to a standard interface used by all other subsystems
- Components appearing in edges that duplicate an existing interaction pattern through a different mechanism

**Codebase evidence:**
- Special-case filtering: code that filters or handles data differently based on source/origin rather than content (e.g., 'if this came from subsystem X, exclude from Y')
- Description/label branching: different messages, labels, or descriptions depending on which code path produced the data
- Noise suppression: code that hides or suppresses effects that would not exist if the standard mechanism were used
- Exception lists: hardcoded lists of things to exclude or handle specially for one subsystem
- Adapter layers: translation between components that should already speak the same interface
- Fix clustering (from Step 1a item 8): 3+ related commits in the same area within a short timeframe, especially with commit messages referencing workarounds or side effects

**Flow evidence (from Step 1b, when available):**
- Request paths that route through extra adapter or filter modules that other paths do not need
- Data flows where one path requires transformation/translation layers that parallel paths skip

**Grouping rule:** Cluster workarounds by area and subsystem. A cascade requires 2+ compensating patterns in the same neighborhood, all related to the same subsystem or code path. Report each cluster as one cascade, not individual workarounds.

**Tension format:**
\`\`\`
Name: Workaround cascade in [area] (compensating for [subsystem])
Components: [list of files/modules containing compensating code]
Analysis Pass: 9 — Workaround Cascade
Pull A: Accommodations are necessary — [subsystem] has legitimately different needs than other subsystems. Gain: handles real differences. Cost: maintenance burden of N workarounds.
Pull B: Accommodations compensate for an architectural shortcut in [subsystem] — address at the source. Gain: eliminates N workarounds. Cost: requires reworking [subsystem]'s approach.
If you assume: [subsystem]'s differences are inherent and permanent, the workarounds are justified
If you assume: [subsystem] could use the same mechanism as other subsystems, the workarounds are unnecessary
Structural observation: [N] compensating patterns in [area], all handling special cases for [subsystem]
Evidence: [specific files with workaround code, git history fix clusters]
Cascade members:
  1. [file:line] — [what this workaround compensates for]
  2. [file:line] — [what this workaround compensates for]
  ...
\`\`\`
```

### 5. Update Step 3 Audit Outcome template

Add after the Pass 8 line (line ~554):
```
**Workaround cascades (Pass 9):** [count, or 'none']
```

### 6. Update Step 3 Module Summary table

Add 'Cascades' note to the Notes column guidance — no new column needed since cascades are a type of tension counted in the Tensions column.

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Overlap with Pass 1 (Complection)**
Pass 1 detects concerns braided together. Pass 9 detects compensating code. The same module can be flagged by both — e.g., a module that braids filtering logic (Pass 1) where that filtering exists to work around a bypass (Pass 9). This is expected and documented in the Pass 9 note. Do NOT deduplicate — both framings add value.

**Edge Case: Cascades spanning module boundaries**
Workaround code may be distributed across multiple modules (filter in module A, adapter in module B, noise suppression in module C — all compensating for the same subsystem). The grouping rule clusters by area AND subsystem, not just module. A cascade can span modules.

**Edge Case: Legitimate multi-subsystem handling**
An API gateway or adapter layer that handles different backends differently is NOT a cascade — it's intentional multi-system integration. Pass 9's Pull A handles this ('legitimately different needs'). The key signal is whether the code COMPENSATES (workaround) vs INTEGRATES (by design). Compensating code often has fix-cluster git history; integration code is written once.

**Edge Case: Fix clustering false positives**
Rapid iteration during initial development looks like fix clustering. The 3+ related commits threshold combined with 'within short timeframe' and 'referencing workarounds' filters for genuine cascades vs normal development churn. The git history signal is supplementary, not primary — it strengthens structural evidence, not replaces it.

**Consistency check: Self-check keywords**
The self-check in Step 3 scans for recommendation/severity/ordering language. Pass 9's tension format uses 'compensating' and 'architectural shortcut' in Pull B — verify these read as structural observations, not recommendations. Both pulls must be stated equally.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ Do NOT use different heading conventions than existing passes (must match: 'What to look for', 'Model evidence (Mode 1 only)', 'Codebase evidence', 'Flow evidence', 'Tension format')
- ❌ Do NOT modify any content in Passes 1-8 (additive only — update Step 1, add Pass 9, update Step 3)
- ❌ Do NOT use recommendation language in Pass 9 output format
- ❌ Do NOT add convention file loading (CLAUDE.md, design docs) — detection is purely structural
- ❌ Do NOT count single workarounds as cascades (minimum 2+ in same area)
- ❌ Do NOT forget to update metadata sections (rigidity_level, quick_reference, skill_overview, Step 2 heading)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Frontmatter description updated to include workaround cascade and mechanism bypass
- [ ] rigidity_level updated: '10 analysis passes' and 'Passes 1-10'
- [ ] quick_reference updated: '10 analysis passes' and 'Passes 1-10'
- [ ] skill_overview updated to include workaround cascades
- [ ] Step 2 heading updated: 'Run 10 Analysis Passes'
- [ ] Step 1a codebase-investigator dispatch includes items 7 (COMPENSATING CODE PATTERNS) and 8 (FIX CLUSTERING)
- [ ] Pass 9 section uses exact heading conventions matching passes 1-8
- [ ] Pass 9 includes all required sections: What to look for, Model evidence, Codebase evidence, Flow evidence, Grouping rule, Tension format
- [ ] Pass 9 has skip condition documented (no codebase = skip)
- [ ] Pass 9 grouping rule specifies minimum 2+ compensating patterns
- [ ] Pass 9 note documents overlap relationship with Pass 1
- [ ] Pass 9 tension format includes Gain/Cost structure matching existing passes
- [ ] Pass 9 tension format includes Cascade members list
- [ ] Step 3 Audit Outcome includes Pass 9 count line
- [ ] Existing passes 1-8 content unchanged (verify with diff)
- [ ] No recommendation language in Pass 9 output (run self-check mentally)
- [ ] Mode 1 and Mode 2 behavior documented in Pass 9

title: Task 7: Update writing-plans for upfront task tree with two-tier spec format
description: Update writing-plans skill to produce complete upfront task trees with simple/medium classification and two-tier task spec format.
design:
## Goal
Update writing-plans to produce complete upfront task trees where each task is classified as simple or medium and follows the two-tier spec format.

## Why
Two epic success criteria depend on this: "Writing-plans produces complete upfront task tree with simple/medium classification" and "Task specs follow the two-tier format." The current writing-plans expands individual tasks but doesn't produce classified task trees or use the two-tier format.

## Effort Estimate
15-20 minutes (modify existing skill, add spec format templates)

## Context
- skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md — current skill (478 lines)
- Two-tier task spec format:
  - Simple (2-10 min, Haiku): Goal/Why/Changes/Verification
  - Medium (10-30 min, Sonnet): Goal/Why/Context/Implementation guidance/Tests/Verification/Boundaries
- Brainstorming Step 6b already creates tasks with classification — writing-plans expands them with detail
- The flow is: brainstorming creates task tree → writing-plans expands specs → SRE batch reviews

## Implementation guidance
1. Read current skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md
2. Key changes needed:
   a. Add the two-tier task spec templates (simple and medium formats)
   b. Update the process to classify each task as simple or medium based on complexity
   c. Ensure each expanded task spec includes the Why section (explains how task fits into epic)
   d. Ensure each expanded task spec includes Boundaries section for medium tasks
   e. Update effort estimate guidance from hours to minutes (2-10 min simple, 10-30 min medium)
   f. Reference that brainstorming creates the task tree; writing-plans expands the specs
3. Keep the core value of writing-plans: codebase-investigator dispatch to verify assumptions, no placeholders, exact file paths
4. Update any references to old execution model (iterative task creation, old granularity)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO tasks exceeding 30 min estimate
- ❌ NO task specs without a Why section
- ❌ NO medium task specs without a Boundaries section
- ❌ NO placeholder text in expanded specs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Two-tier task spec templates present (simple: Goal/Why/Changes/Verification; medium: Goal/Why/Context/Implementation/Tests/Verification/Boundaries)
- [ ] Task classification guidance: simple (2-10 min, single-file, exact changes) vs medium (10-30 min, multi-file, judgment required)
- [ ] Every task spec template includes Why section
- [ ] Medium task spec template includes Boundaries section
- [ ] Effort estimates in minutes (not hours)
- [ ] No references to old execution model (iterative creation, 4-8 hour tasks)
close_reason: Writing-plans updated with two-tier spec format (simple/medium). All 6 criteria verified. Commit abe76c4.

title: Satellite repairs: quality-line fixes
description: Fix the debugging/testing satellites: fixing-bugs RED gate, analyzing-test-effectiveness half-migration + orphaned agent, dispatching-parallel-agents monitoring story, refactoring artifact transport, root-cause cross-refs, agent model pins.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- R1: fixing-bugs' RED-phase instructions are internally consistent — the regression test FAILS before the fix and PASSES after — with the authoring-erratum text removed (the "test was written backwards by mistake earlier" heading and inverted comments).
- R2: fixing-bugs and refactoring-safely bd commands use valid priority syntax (numeric 0-4 per skills/common-patterns/bd-commands.md).
- R3: analyzing-test-effectiveness consistently reflects the batch SRE model everywhere (frontmatter description, checklist items, integration, flow diagram), its generated task templates conform to the two-tier spec format (Why and Boundaries present), and it uses ONE phase numbering across rigidity declaration, quick reference, body headers, and worked example.
- R4: agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md is either actually dispatched by the skill (keeping verbose analysis out of the lead) or deleted — no orphaned duplicate methodology remains (choice is Open for Design).
- R5: dispatching-parallel-agents tells one coherent dispatch-and-monitoring story — no instruction that is impossible under its own dispatch mechanics.
- R6: The refactoring trio's artifacts (diagnosis report, design spec) have a durable transport that survives session boundaries, making refactoring-safely's Step 0 gate satisfiable cold; refactoring-diagnosis's "consider rewrite" verdict routes to an explicit destination.
- R7: root-cause-tracing and debugging-with-tools cross-reference each other bidirectionally, pointing at sections that exist.
- R8: Model-pin decisions applied for test-effectiveness-analyst, codebase-investigator, internet-researcher (pin values are Open for Design).
- R9: plugin.json minor bump before final push.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] fixing-bugs: no inverted RED comments remain; grep -n "written backwards" skills/fixing-bugs/SKILL.md returns nothing; the regression-test steps read FAILS-before-fix / PASSES-after-fix, matching the file's own critical rule 3.
- [ ] Priority syntax: grep -rEn -- "--priority P[0-9]" skills/ commands/ agents/ returns zero hits (fixing-bugs, refactoring-safely, managing-bd-tasks all fixed).
- [ ] analyzing-test-effectiveness: grep -in "on each task\|on all tasks" skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md returns zero hits; exactly one numbering scheme across rigidity declaration, quick-reference, body headers, and worked example.
- [ ] analyzing-test-effectiveness dispatches hyperpowers:test-effectiveness-analyst via a blocking-subagent dispatch block; RED/YELLOW/GREEN methodology detail lives only in the agent file (skill cites, never restates).
- [ ] The four generated task templates in analyzing-test-effectiveness conform to the two-tier spec format: Why present in all; Boundaries present where medium; simple/medium classification stated.
- [ ] The skill's batch SRE dispatch block carries the canonical elements from brainstorming Step 7: Inputs line, preserve-existing-sections clause, APPROVE/NEEDS REVISION/REJECT verdict enum, epic-coverage table in the Return clause, and the once-against-the-full-tree sentence.
- [ ] dispatching-parallel-agents: grep -n "TaskOutput" skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md returns zero hits; Step 4 describes post-return triage; no mid-flight polling or cancel-stuck-agent instructions remain.
- [ ] Refactoring trio: refactoring-diagnosis ends by creating the refactor bd issue with the diagnosis report under "## Diagnosis Report" in --design; refactoring-design reads that issue and appends "## Refactor Design Spec" via bd update --design; refactoring-safely Step 0 gates on bd show showing both sections and works the existing issue instead of creating a new task; the rewrite verdict routes explicitly to hyperpowers:brainstorming.
- [ ] Cross-refs: root-cause-tracing's "called by" entry names a section that exists in debugging-with-tools; debugging-with-tools lists root-cause-tracing under "calls" with a real call site in its body; the circular called-by/called-by pair is gone.
- [ ] agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md frontmatter has model: sonnet; codebase-investigator and internet-researcher carry a deliberate-inheritance note (unpinned by design); no other agent frontmatter changed.
- [ ] plugin.json version is 3.23.0.
- [ ] ./hooks/test/contract-test.sh passes (regression only — no hook changes expected).
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO deleting agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md (settled: dispatch model chosen; deletion resolves duplication the wrong way and forfeits context isolation)
- ❌ NO background-dispatch or TaskOutput-monitoring language in dispatching-parallel-agents (settled: pure blocking story; reintroducing polling recreates the exact contradiction this epic removes)
- ❌ NO new in-repo report directories (docs/refactors/ or similar) — bd design field is the transport; only the architecture domain (docs/arch/) writes repo files
- ❌ NO frontmatter model pins on codebase-investigator or internet-researcher (design-tier research inherits the session model per brainstorming Step 2 — a pin silently downgrades it)
- ❌ NO restating common-patterns content in rewritten text — cite spec-templates.md / pipeline-constants.md / bd-commands.md instead (duplication is the defect class this epic fixes)
- ❌ NO placeholder text in rewritten templates or specs ("[TBD]", "[Remaining steps truncated]") — every template ships complete
- ❌ NO editing requirements R1-R9 during execution (epic contract is immutable; tasks adapt, requirements do not)

## Approach
Three repair lanes. (1) analyzing-test-effectiveness is restructured as an orchestrator: the agent file becomes the single owner of the analysis methodology (gains model: sonnet and a tightened return contract), and the skill sheds its ~400 duplicated lines to become a 5-step flow — scope, dispatch analyst as blocking subagent, present findings, create bd epic+tasks with two-tier templates, batch SRE review aligned to brainstorming's canonical block. This resolves the per-task SRE references, the three-scheme numbering chaos, and the orphaned agent in one construction. (2) The refactoring trio gets a single bd issue per refactor as artifact transport: diagnosis creates it with the report in --design, design appends the spec to the same field, safely's Step 0 gates on bd show — satisfiable cold, matching the repo canon that --design carries the full detailed spec. The rewrite verdict routes to /brainstorm. (3) Mechanical alignment for the rest: fixing-bugs RED semantics corrected against its own critical rule 3 and the erratum deleted; priority syntax swept to numeric (including the approved scope add in managing-bd-tasks); dispatching-parallel-agents rewritten to the pure blocking story with post-return triage; cross-refs wired bidirectionally at real sections; deliberate-inheritance notes on the two research agents.

Chosen over deleting the analyst agent (loses context isolation for verbose test-suite analysis, against the plugin's agent pattern) and over background+TaskOutput monitoring (larger rewrite, diverges from the repo-wide blocking-subagent doctrine).

## Architecture
Components affected: skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness (major rewrite), agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md (contract + pin), skills/fixing-bugs, skills/managing-bd-tasks (priority lines only), skills/dispatching-parallel-agents, skills/refactoring-diagnosis, skills/refactoring-design, skills/refactoring-safely, skills/root-cause-tracing, skills/debugging-with-tools, agents/codebase-investigator.md, agents/internet-researcher.md (notes only), commands/analyze-tests.md (wording check), .claude-plugin/plugin.json.
New edges: analyzing-test-effectiveness skill → test-effectiveness-analyst agent (blocking dispatch); debugging-with-tools → root-cause-tracing (forward call, replacing a phantom reverse edge). New artifact contract: refactor bd issue design field accumulates Diagnosis Report then Refactor Design Spec.

## Architecture Impact
(Step 4 result — questions per skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md)

- Q1 new component: NO
- Q2 public interface change: YES (analyst return contract; refactoring artifact contract)
- Q3 cross-component dependency added/removed: YES (skill→agent dispatch edge added; phantom reverse cross-ref edge removed)
- Q4 new request path (2+ components): YES (analysis flow now traverses skill → agent)
- Q5 responsibility moved: YES (methodology ownership skill → agent)

Result: 4 YES. /intuition was offered and deferred — the 2026-07-07 critical review that sourced this epic already served the audit function over this exact ground, and the initiative-level offer was also deferred.

## Provenance
None because direct in-session input: the 2026-07-07 critical review (quality-line reviewer findings F1-F10); no governing plan document. Re-verified against HEAD e4b39ee on 2026-07-08 by codebase-investigator: zero line drift.

## Boundaries
**In scope:** skills/fixing-bugs, skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness (+ commands/analyze-tests.md if wording shifts), agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md, skills/dispatching-parallel-agents, skills/refactoring-diagnosis, skills/refactoring-design, skills/refactoring-safely, skills/root-cause-tracing, skills/debugging-with-tools, agents/codebase-investigator.md + agents/internet-researcher.md (model documentation only), skills/managing-bd-tasks (priority-syntax lines L59/L69/L80 ONLY — scope add approved 2026-07-08, same defect class as R2).
**Out of scope:** test-driven-development, testing-anti-patterns, verification-before-completion (verified clean in the review); dedup of test-quality doctrine into common-patterns (context-economics cluster); sre-task-refinement itself (execution-contracts epic, done); any managing-bd-tasks change beyond the three priority lines.

## Dependencies
Depends on: bd-k9aa Satellite repairs: execution contract fixes — CLOSED 2026-07-08, satisfied.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The 2026-07-07 critical review found the quality-line satellites (debugging/testing/refactoring skills) carrying ten defects: self-contradicting RED-phase instructions, invalid bd syntax, a half-migrated skill with three numbering schemes and an orphaned 377-line duplicate agent, an internally impossible dispatch-and-monitor story, artifacts with no durable transport, and phantom cross-references. Each defect misleads a future session that follows the skill as written.

### Research Findings
**Codebase (codebase-investigator re-verification, 2026-07-08, HEAD e4b39ee):**
- Zero line drift from the review's citations; bd-k9aa touched none of these files.
- fixing-bugs states the CORRECT RED semantics at L361-374 and L493-497 — the fix is alignment to the file's own rule, not new design.
- analyzing-test-effectiveness Step 5.3 (L657-677) is already batch-correct and near-canonical; 8 surrounding sites still say per-task (L3, L7, L39, L817, L985, L1046, L1068, L1081). Numbering: THREE schemes (legacy 5-phase at L13 + Step 5.x labels + worked example; 8-phase quick-ref; 7-phase body headers).
- test-effectiveness-analyst agent: 377 lines, ~90% duplicates skill Phases 2-6 verbatim (same worked examples); sole normative mention is skill L1070; no dispatch instructions exist anywhere; no model pin.
- dispatching-parallel-agents: blocking single-message dispatch mandated at L154/L617 while L177-190/L501-515/L512/L618/L658 instruct mid-flight TaskOutput polling and cancel-of-stuck — impossible for a blocked lead.
- Refactoring trio: diagnosis and design skills contain ZERO bd commands and no storage instruction; safely Step 0 demands both artifacts with no location — unsatisfiable cold. refactoring-safely L74 uses invalid P2.
- Transport precedent is unambiguous: bd --design carries specs (bd-commands.md L69, brainstorming, writing-plans), bd --notes carries gate state (loop-interfaces.md), in-repo files exist only for architecture (docs/arch/). No docs/refactors/ precedent.
- Model pins: reviewer/code-reviewer/ponder=sonnet, test-runner=haiku, executor deliberately unpinned; the three in question have no pin. Brainstorming Step 2 mandates research dispatches inherit the session model.
- NEW: managing-bd-tasks L59/L69/L80 carry the same invalid --priority P1 syntax (scope add approved).
- Cross-refs: root-cause-tracing L541 cites phantom "Phase 2: Trace Backward"; debugging-with-tools' real Phase 2 is "Form Hypothesis" (L114); each file lists the other only under "called by" — circular as written.

**External:**
- None because this is a plugin-internal documentation repair epic with no external APIs or libraries involved.

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Analyst agent: dispatch or delete? | Dispatch it from the skill | Skill becomes orchestrator; agent owns methodology; R4 resolved via dispatch; enables sonnet pin |
| dispatching-parallel-agents story? | Pure blocking dispatch | Monitoring sections rewritten to post-return triage; all TaskOutput refs removed |
| Refactoring artifact transport? | bd design fields | One bd issue per refactor, created at diagnosis; design field accumulates both artifacts; Step 0 gates on bd show |
| Model pins for the three agents? | Analyst=sonnet; researchers stay unpinned, documented | Pin added to analyst only; deliberate-inheritance notes on both researchers; brainstorming design-tier doctrine preserved |
| managing-bd-tasks P1 syntax (new finding, out of declared scope)? | Fold into R2's task | Boundaries updated; three lines fixed in this epic; no separate epic for 3 lines |
| Design sections 1-3 validation | All approved as presented | Design frozen as recorded in Approach/Architecture |
| Impact check: run /intuition (4 YES)? | Proceed — audit already served | Deferral recorded; 2026-07-07 review covered this ground |

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Orchestrator restructure + bd-issue transport + mechanical alignment (chosen)

**What it is:** Restructure analyzing-test-effectiveness around dispatching the existing analyst agent; carry refactoring artifacts in one bd issue per refactor; align everything else mechanically to already-correct in-repo canon.

**Investigation:**
- Verified the agent duplicates ~90% of skill Phases 2-6 — the split is a extraction, not new writing
- Verified bd --design is the repo-wide spec transport; verified brainstorming Step 7 block as the canonical SRE dispatch to copy
- Verified fixing-bugs and ATE both already contain their own correct versions — fixes are alignment

**Pros:**
- Context isolation for verbose test-suite analysis (matches test-runner/reviewer/ponder pattern)
- Single methodology owner kills the duplication; skill shrinks ~1114→~500 lines
- Transport uses existing canon; no new precedent

**Cons:**
- The ATE rewrite is the largest single change in the epic — mitigated by splitting into two sequential tasks with the target outline in the specs

**Chosen because:** every piece aligns to something already verified correct in the repo (the plugin's agent pattern, bd-commands canon, brainstorming's canonical block, fixing-bugs' own critical rule 3) — minimal invention, maximal consistency.

#### 2. Delete the analyst agent; skill self-contains (rejected)

**What it is:** Remove agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md and the L1070 mention; keep all methodology in the skill.

**Why explored:** One fewer artifact; zero callers today made deletion look free.

**Investigation:** Confirmed the agent has zero dispatch sites — but also confirmed the analysis it describes (read every test file plus production code, line-by-line justifications) is exactly the verbose work the plugin's context-economy doctrine keeps out of the lead.

**Pros:**
- Smallest diff for R4; no dispatch contract to design

**Cons:**
- Analysis runs in the lead's context — heavy and against the established agent pattern
- Forfeits the model-pin lever for analysis cost/quality

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** it resolves the duplication in the direction that damages context economy; the plugin pattern (test-runner, reviewer, ponder) is dispatch, not inline.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** the plugin deprecates the specialized-agent pattern repo-wide.

#### 3. Background dispatch + TaskOutput monitoring story for dispatching-parallel-agents (rejected)

**What it is:** Rewrite the skill's mechanics around background agents with mid-flight TaskOutput polling and cancel/retry of stuck agents.

**Why explored:** TaskOutput is a real tool; mid-flight monitoring is genuinely more capable, and the settled decisions left this option open pending harness verification.

**Investigation:** The repo-wide doctrine (executing-plans, executor dispatch, SRE dispatch, reviewer dispatch) is blocking subagents; nothing else in the plugin does background monitoring.

**Pros:**
- Could cancel a stuck agent instead of waiting for it to return

**Cons:**
- Larger rewrite; must verify and then track harness polling behavior over time
- Diverges from the blocking doctrine used everywhere else — a second dispatch model to keep coherent

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** the skill's job (dispatch 3+ independent investigations, synthesize results) is fully served by blocking dispatch plus post-return triage; the monitoring capability is not worth a doctrine fork.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** the plugin adopts background dispatch as doctrine elsewhere first (e.g., executing-plans moves to it).

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** see Boundaries section above (authoritative list, including the managing-bd-tasks scope add).

**Out of scope:** see Boundaries section above; additionally the exact final line count of the rewritten ATE skill is not a requirement — the requirement is single-owner methodology and one numbering scheme.

### Open Questions
- Exact step numbering labels for the rewritten ATE skill (e.g., "Step 1-5" vs "Phase 1-5") — executor's choice within the one-scheme-everywhere constraint.
- Exact wording of the deliberate-inheritance notes on the two research agents — spec gives required content, executor phrases it.

## Verified Sites (for task creation, HEAD e4b39ee 2026-07-08)
fixing-bugs L130-135, L150-157, L284-291 (RED inversion + erratum; correct reference at L361-374, L493-497), L69 (priority); managing-bd-tasks L59, L69, L80 (priority); analyzing-test-effectiveness L3, L7, L39, L817, L985, L1046, L1068, L1081 (per-task SRE), L13/L17-26/body-headers/L855-886 (three numbering schemes), L558-654 + L693-729 (four templates missing Why/Boundaries), L657-677 (near-canonical SRE block), L1070 (sole analyst mention); dispatching-parallel-agents L152-168 + L617 (blocking mandate), L177-190, L501-515, L512, L618, L658 (monitoring refs); refactoring-diagnosis L58-62 (rewrite verdict), L64-107 (report format, no transport); refactoring-design L33-34 (inputs), L63-99 (spec format, no transport); refactoring-safely L44-50 (Step 0 gate), L67-101 (bd commands), L74 (P2); root-cause-tracing L541 (phantom section ref); debugging-with-tools L114 (Phase 2: Form Hypothesis), L463-479 (integration lists).
