title: Task 6: naming sweep — ponder dispatchers, CLAUDE.md, README, using-hyper routing
design:
## Goal
Every file that names the review chain or ponder's dispatchers describes the reconciled reality.

## Why
ponder authorizes review-implementation by name as an auto-approving UPDATE dispatcher (7 lines), and CLAUDE.md/README/using-hyper document the retired chain — the remaining corners of F1. R6+R7 of bd-iaem.

## Changes (all line-verified on current main)
- skills/ponder/SKILL.md L47 UPDATE trigger -> "The post-build Architecture Impact Check (executing-plans completion, or review-implementation re-verification) answered yes to any question"; L27 and L156-162 example framing -> post-build-check wording. IMPORTANT: the example code block restates 2 of the 5 structural questions verbatim at L160-161 ("Did this work change a component's public interface?" / "Did this work add or remove a dependency between components?") — reword these to outcome summaries (e.g., "Post-build check: YES on interface change — Auth exposes refreshToken(); YES on new dependency — Auth -> TokenStore; questions 3-5 NO") so no question text survives outside architecture-impact-check.md; L336 critical rule 4 authorized dispatchers -> "the post-build architecture check (executing-plans completion / review-implementation re-verification) or Intuition"; L376 integration + L387-388 call chain -> new dispatcher names
- agents/ponder.md line 3 description example -> "The post-build architecture check found that a component's public interface changed" (replace the review-implementation phrasing; note the current text is capitalized "Review-implementation" — match case-insensitively when locating it)
- CLAUDE.md line ~198 (workflow step 4) -> reviewer gate + architecture check + STOP run inside executing-plans' completion; review-implementation described as on-demand re-verification; line ~210 ponder caller -> "executing-plans completion (architecture checklist)"
- README.md line ~20 (skill list) and ~59 (command list) -> re-verification wording for review-implementation
- skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md L42 build row -> "hyperpowers:brainstorming -> hyperpowers:executing-plans -> hyperpowers:finishing-a-development-branch"; adjust the existing "About to claim done" area only if needed; add or reword a row for on-demand epic re-verification -> hyperpowers:review-implementation (decide exact table shape in-task, keep the table consistent)

## Verification
- [ ] grep -rni "review-implementation" skills/ponder/ agents/ponder.md returns only re-verification-role wording (case-insensitive: catches the capitalized "Review-implementation" occurrences in example prose)
- [ ] grep -n "Did this work" skills/ponder/SKILL.md returns nothing (no restated question text in the example)
- [ ] CLAUDE.md/README/using-hyper describe the reconciled chain; no file claims review-implementation is the mandatory step between executing-plans and finishing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Boundaries
Minimal wording changes only; no other CLAUDE.md/README staleness (agent lists, RECOMMENDATIONS.md ref — other epics). No semantic changes to ponder's modes or approval rules beyond dispatcher naming.

title: Task 6: analyzing-test-effectiveness tail alignment + command check
description: Align worked example, rationalizations, red flags, checklist, integration + flow diagram to the new 5-step body; verify commands/analyze-tests.md
design:
Classification: medium

## Goal
Everything after `</the_process>` in skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md uses the new Step 1-5 orchestrator scheme and batch SRE phrasing; commands/analyze-tests.md wording verified.

## Why
R3: the worked example uses the legacy 5-phase labels, the checklist/integration/critical-rules say per-task SRE, the flow diagram still reads "sre-task-refinement (on each task)", and the L1070 agent reference lacks dispatch semantics. Blocked by Task 5 — the tail must match the body Task 5 actually wrote, so read that first.

## Context
Sites verified at HEAD e4b39ee (line numbers will have shifted after Task 5 — re-locate by content, the quoted strings are the anchors): worked example labels "Phase 1 - Inventory" / "Phase 2 - Categorize" / "Phase 3 - Corner cases" / "Phase 5 - Plan" (~L855-886); critical rule "SRE refinement on all tasks" (~L985); checklist "Ran hyperpowers:sre-task-refinement on ALL tasks" (~L1046); integration "This skill calls (MANDATORY): hyperpowers:sre-task-refinement (for ALL bd tasks created)" (~L1068) and "hyperpowers:test-effectiveness-analyst agent (for detailed analysis)" (~L1070); flow diagram line "sre-task-refinement (on each task)" (~L1081). Ownership note (SRE review): the "All tasks reviewed with hyperpowers:sre-task-refinement" checklist (formerly ~L815-819) sits INSIDE `<the_process>` — it is Task 5's territory, not yours; verify Task 5 converted it to batch phrasing, and if it did not, report the defect per your Boundaries rather than editing the body. Read the rewritten body (Task 5's output) before editing anything.

## Implementation
1. Read the new `<the_process>` section; note the exact step names/numbers Task 5 used.
2. Worked example: relabel to the new scheme and reshape the narrative to the orchestrator flow (scope → dispatch analyst → report received → present → bd tree → batch SRE). Compress rather than expand — the example demonstrates orchestration, not methodology.
3. Per-task SRE phrasing → batch phrasing at every tail site: critical rule (~L985), checklist item (~L1046), integration MANDATORY line (~L1068). Batch wording: "batch SRE review against the full task tree (once, as a fresh subagent)".
4. Agent reference (~L1070): → "hyperpowers:test-effectiveness-analyst agent (dispatched in Step 2 — owns the analysis methodology)".
5. Flow diagram (~L1081): "sre-task-refinement (on each task)" → "sre-task-refinement BATCH MODE (full task tree, once)". Check the diagram's other nodes match the new step flow.
6. Rationalizations / red flags: update any phase-number references to the new scheme; if a red flag about restating the agent's methodology in the skill fits naturally, add it.
7. commands/analyze-tests.md (5 lines): confirm the description still matches the restructured skill; adjust only if actually stale.

## Tests
Omit — pure documentation task.

## Verification
- `grep -in "on each task\|on all tasks" skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md` → no output (whole file, both tasks now landed)
- `grep -in "all tasks reviewed with\|applied to each task" skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md` → no output (whole file — if this hits inside the body, report it as a Task 5 defect, do not edit the body)
- Read the reshaped worked example and tail: RED/YELLOW/GREEN appear only as citations of the analyst's categories (counts, category names) — no categorization criteria restated outside agents/test-effectiveness-analyst.md
- `grep -n "Phase [0-9]" skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md` → no output
- `grep -n "Step 5\." skills/analyzing-test-effectiveness/SKILL.md` → no output (grep guard: never end a sentence with the bare string "Step 5." — rephrase to "Step 5 (Batch SRE Review)." or similar)
- Read quick-ref, one body header, and the worked example: single numbering scheme
- `cat commands/analyze-tests.md` → wording consistent with the restructured skill

## Boundaries
Do not restructure the body (Task 5 owns `<the_process>` and everything above it) — if the body itself has a defect, report it, don't fix it. Do not add new worked examples. commands/analyze-tests.md changes only on actual staleness.

title: Task 2: Update skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — lead-side SRE refinement
description: Add lead-side SRE refinement responsibility to executing-plans skill per epic bd-6uw spec. Five edits to one file: dispatch briefing addendum, new Step 4 in Step 3a Validation process, new Critical Rule 10, new Common Rationalization, new Verification Checklist item. Single-file scope with explicit anchors and grep checks.
design:
## Goal

Modify `skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` per epic bd-6uw spec to add lead-side SRE refinement responsibility, complementing the executor.md changes from bd-3nj. Five edits to one file: (a) Step 2 dispatch briefing — add envelope-contract + no-executor-side-SRE clarification; (b) Step 3a — insert new step 4 between Decide and 3b that runs SRE refinement on approved proposals; (c) Critical Rules — add new Rule 10 about lead-side SRE responsibility; (d) Common Rationalizations — add entry rejecting "executor already ran SRE"; (e) Verification Checklist — add new SRE-refinement item under "Before approving any proposal".

This task changes ONLY `skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md`. Coordinated changes to `agents/executor.md` are already done (bd-3nj). Plugin version bump is the next/final task.

## Implementation

### 1. Pre-edit Read (MANDATORY first step)

Before any edits, do a fresh `Read skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` to confirm:
- Step 2 dispatch briefing template is at lines ~65-90 with `## 2. Dispatch Executor` heading and a fenced code block.
- Step 3a "Validate proposed next task — check ALL five" is at line ~119.
- Step 3 "Decide:" is at line ~126.
- Section 3b "Handling ESCALATION Return" starts at line ~131.
- Critical Rules section heading at line ~245; Rule 9 ends at line ~263.
- Common Rationalizations bulleted list at lines ~267-273.
- Verification Checklist "Before approving any proposal" at lines ~279-285.

If structure differs materially (sections renamed, reordered, renumbered), STOP and return ESCALATION — do not guess.

- [ ] All five deliverables verified by GREEN grep checks (run via test-runner agent).
- [ ] Final consistency grep shows all SRE references coherent (lead-side responsibility throughout).
- [ ] Single commit on this task with the deliverables.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass.

## Key Considerations

### Pre-Edit File-Drift Check

Same as bd-3nj. The brainstorm read happened on session-start state; verify line numbers and section names match before editing. If material drift, escalate.

### Edit Tool old_string Uniqueness

`skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md` is long (~318 lines) and has repetitive structure (multiple checklists, bullets, rules). Multi-line `old_string` anchors are mandatory for every Edit in this task. The anchor specifications above use 2-3 line context windows; if any Edit fails on uniqueness, extend the window further.

### Indentation Sensitivity

Step 2 dispatch briefing's "Important context:" bullets are inside a YAML-style `prompt: |` block, which uses 6-space indentation under `prompt:`. The new bullet must match this indentation exactly (4 spaces from prompt: + 2 for nesting under "Important context:"). Verify with: `grep -P "^      - The completion report MUST be one of three envelopes" skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md`.

### Step 4 Insertion Anchor (Deliverable 2)

The Step 3a "Decide" step ends with "REDIRECT — Dispatch fresh executor with a different task. Note why proposal was rejected in the prompt." Then a blank line. Then `### 3b. Handling ESCALATION Return` heading. The new step 4 must slot in BETWEEN the REDIRECT bullet and the blank-line-before-3b. The Edit's `old_string` should include enough context (REDIRECT bullet + blank line + 3b heading) to be unique.

### Critical Rule 10 Numbering

Rule 9 already exists. Adding Rule 10 is straightforward (append at end). Do NOT renumber existing rules. The verification grep `^10\\. \\*\\*Run SRE refinement` confirms correct numbering.

### Common Rationalization Format

Existing entries follow exact pattern: `- "rationalization quote" → correction sentence.` The new entry must match this format precisely. Verification grep checks for the rationalization quote text.

### Inherited Anti-Patterns (Reminder)

From epic bd-6uw, these apply to this task:
- ❌ NO scope creep into `agents/executor.md` — already done in bd-3nj.
- ❌ NO scope creep into `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` — version bump is a separate task.
- ❌ NO modifying the existing 9 Critical Rules' content (only adding rule 10).
- ❌ NO modifying the existing Common Rationalization bullets (only adding a new one at the end).
- ❌ NO modifying the existing Verification Checklist items (only adding a new one).

If during execution you find you can't complete the task without violating one of these, escalate via ESCALATION.

### Cross-Task Coherence

The new Step 4 in Step 3a references "see bd-0he" as motivation. The new Critical Rule 10 references "Established by bd-6uw fix for bd-0he format drift." The new Common Rationalization references "As of bd-6uw (fix for bd-0he)". This explicit attribution helps future readers trace why these rules exist.

## Notes for executor

This task is markdown-only. No code tests run. TDD is grep-based RED/GREEN via the test-runner agent. RED is not optional — confirms the search pattern is correct AND that the change is needed (not already present from a prior session).

Do NOT make any edits to `agents/executor.md` (already done in bd-3nj) or `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (separate next task). Stay scoped.

When proposing the next task in your COMPLETE envelope (note: per the NEW Rule 4 in agents/executor.md, you propose without running SRE — the lead runs SRE on your proposal), the natural next task is: "Bump plugin version to 3.8.1 and run final cross-file consistency check." Final consistency check should verify no stale references remain anywhere across `agents/executor.md`, `skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md`, `commands/`, `agents/reviewer.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `README.md`.

**Output format reminder:** Per the new Final Output Contract in agents/executor.md (just shipped in bd-3nj), your final response MUST start with `## Status: COMPLETE` and contain Task / Done / Commits / Learned / Changed from plan / Proposed next task sections. The Verify Final Output GATE in your Completion Protocol forces this. Do NOT emit SRE-style review content as your final response — the lead runs SRE, not you.
notes:
## SRE Review Addendum (added 2026-04-28)

### Edit Tool Insert-Between-Lines Pattern

Several deliverables (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) require inserting NEW content between two EXISTING lines. The Edit tool replaces `old_string` with `new_string` — there is no native 'insert' operation. The correct pattern:

- `old_string`: lines A + (gap/whitespace) + B (the bracketing context as it currently appears)
- `new_string`: lines A + (gap/whitespace) + NEW CONTENT + (gap/whitespace) + B

The bracketing lines A and B MUST appear in BOTH old_string and new_string — the new content is sandwiched between them in new_string. Common junior mistake: putting only the new content in new_string, which deletes the bracketing lines.

Example for Deliverable 4 (append to Common Rationalizations):
- old_string includes the 'I'll use team dispatch' bullet AND the closing </critical_rules> tag.
- new_string includes the 'I'll use team dispatch' bullet, the new bullet, AND the closing </critical_rules> tag.

If you find yourself uncertain about the Edit pattern: do a Read of the relevant section first, copy-paste the exact lines for old_string, then construct new_string by adding the new content in the right spot.

### Approved by SRE Review

All 8 categories applied. Granularity ✅ (5 deliverables, ~3-4h). Implementability ✅ (verbatim text + multi-line anchors). Success Criteria ✅ (9 grep-verifiable). Dependencies ✅ (parent-child to bd-6uw). Safety ✅ (inherited anti-patterns surfaced). Edge Cases ✅ (file drift, Edit uniqueness, indentation, numbering, insert-between-lines pattern now explicit). Red Flags ✅ (no placeholders). Test Meaningfulness ✅ (specific grep checks).

title: Task 3: Wire review-implementation and brainstorming to Ponder
design:
## Goal
Add architecture checklist to review-implementation and update brainstorming references to use /ponder.

## Implementation Checklist

1. Review-implementation: Add Step 5 — Architecture Update Checklist
   - Insert after Step 4, before </the_process>
   - 5 questions: public interface change? dependency change? new/removed component? responsibility move? new request path?
   - If any yes: dispatch /ponder update with structured description
   - If no model exists (no arch/*.c4): note changes for future model creation, skip ponder dispatch
   - Update quick_reference table to add Step 5 row
   - Update rigidity_level "4-step" to "5-step"
   - Update verification_checklist to include Step 5
   - Update integration section to include ponder in "calls"

2. Brainstorming: Update epic template Architecture Update Checklist
   - Replace "update .c4 files, update linked markdown, propose dynamic view if Q5, confirm with architect." with "invoke /ponder update with description of what changed."

3. Brainstorming: Add Intuition suggestion for structural friction
   - After Dynamic view proposal in Step 3
   - When architect expresses structural friction, suggest /intuition
   - Only in BUILDING mode

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Review-implementation Step 5 exists with 5-question checklist and /ponder update dispatch
- [ ] Review-implementation quick_reference shows Step 5
- [ ] Review-implementation verification_checklist includes Step 5
- [ ] Review-implementation integration lists ponder
- [ ] Brainstorming epic template says /ponder update (not direct .c4 edits)
- [ ] Brainstorming Step 3 has Intuition suggestion for structural friction
- [ ] No "update .c4 files" language remains in brainstorming

## Anti-patterns
- NO "update .c4 files" remaining in brainstorming
- NO auto-updating model without architect confirmation
- NO ponder dispatch when no model exists (update mode requires existing model)

title: Task 4: Update /brainstorm friction-detection + AIC-routing prompts to pass prose focus to /intuition
description: Brainstorm Step 3 friction-detection and Step 4 AIC-routing prompts currently invoke /intuition without passing focus context. Update them to explicitly recommend prose-focus invocation (now that /intuition has the Optional: Prose Focus mechanism from bd-428).
design:
## Goal
Update skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md friction-detection prompt (Step 3, lines ~129-136) and AIC-routing prompt (Step 4, lines ~177-183) so they recommend invoking /intuition WITH prose focus context describing the current design's components or tension areas. /intuition now has an "Optional: Prose Focus" mechanism (added in bd-428); brainstorm should leverage it.

This task is small — ~10-20 lines of prompt rewording. No structural changes to brainstorm; just make the /intuition invocations more concrete.

## This task does NOT touch
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md (already trimmed in bd-428, bd-bkv, bd-5p1)
- skills/ponder/SKILL.md (Task 5 sweep)
- agents/ponder.md
- CLAUDE.md / README.md (Task 5)
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (final-push Task 5)

## This task DOES touch
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md ONLY

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN in this task)
- ❌ NO scope creep beyond brainstorm prompt updates (do NOT touch /intuition, /ponder, etc.)
- ❌ NO removing the "would you like to run /intuition?" architect-question pattern (cybernetic tools signal to the human; never decide autonomously — see saved feedback)
- ❌ NO making /intuition invocation feel mandatory; it remains an offer the architect can decline
- ❌ NO partial commits

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- Read skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:120-140 (Step 3 design-time friction detection prompt)
- Read skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:170-190 (Step 4 AIC routing prompt with N boxes language)
- Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md "Optional: Prose Focus" subsection (added in bd-428) — note the format / examples to align brainstorm wording

### 2. Write tests first (TDD — RED phase)

Save as /tmp/verify-bd-task4.sh, chmod +x, run pre-edit (must FAIL) then post-edit (must PASS):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e
F=skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md

echo "=== brainstorm: friction prompt mentions prose focus ==="
grep -E 'prose focus|prose context|with focus|with the focus' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: brainstorm does not reference prose focus invocation"; exit 1; }

echo "=== brainstorm: friction prompt example shows concrete focus phrasing ==="
grep -E 'examine|focus on|focused on' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: brainstorm lacks concrete prose-focus example wording"; exit 1; }

echo "=== brainstorm: --scope and --epic absent (regression check) ==="
! grep -F -- '--scope' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: --scope flag reference found"; exit 1; }
! grep -F -- '--epic' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: --epic flag reference found"; exit 1; }

echo "=== brainstorm: 'would you like to run /intuition' offer pattern preserved ==="
grep -E 'Would you like to run /intuition' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: architect-offer pattern removed"; exit 1; }

echo "=== brainstorm: architect decides (cybernetic-tool pattern) ==="
grep -F 'The architect decides' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: architect-decides language removed"; exit 1; }

echo "All checks passed."
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Section A: Update Step 3 friction-detection prompt**

- [ ] skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:129-136 (approx). Update the prompt that brainstorm presents to the architect when structural friction is detected. Current text:
  ```
  "This sounds like structural friction — uncertainty about where boundaries
  belong or how components should relate. /intuition can systematically
  examine your architecture for tensions before you commit to this design.

  Would you like to run /intuition first, or continue with the current design?"
  ```
  
  Update to (approximately):
  ```
  "This sounds like structural friction — uncertainty about where boundaries
  belong or how components should relate. /intuition can systematically
  examine your architecture for tensions before you commit to this design.

  If you'd like to run it, pass prose focus describing the area of friction —
  e.g., 'examine the [component-A]/[component-B] boundary for tensions
  this design might interact with' — so /intuition's evidence gathering
  and tension report can target the focused area.

  Would you like to run /intuition with focus on [specific area], or continue
  with the current design?"
  ```
  
  The key additions: (a) explicit recommendation to PASS prose focus, (b) concrete example invocation, (c) the offer-question now mentions the specific focus area.

**Section B: Update Step 4 AIC-routing prompt**

- [ ] skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:177-183 (approx). Update the prompt that brainstorm presents when 1+ AIC boxes are checked. Current text:
  ```
  "This design touches [N] architectural dimension(s). /intuition can examine
  your existing architecture for tensions this change might interact with.

  Would you like to run /intuition before creating the epic, or proceed?"
  ```
  
  Update to (approximately):
  ```
  "This design touches [N] architectural dimension(s). /intuition can examine
  your existing architecture for tensions this change might interact with.

  If you'd like to run it, pass prose focus naming the components or boundaries
  this design touches — e.g., 'examine [list-of-affected-components] for tensions
  this epic will interact with' — so /intuition can target evidence gathering
  and filter the tension report to the relevant area.

  Would you like to run /intuition with focus on [specific components], or
  proceed to epic creation?"
  ```

**Section C: Verify offer pattern + architect-decides language preserved**

- [ ] Confirm "Would you like to run /intuition" appears in BOTH prompts after edit (architect-offer pattern preserved)
- [ ] Confirm "The architect decides" or equivalent language present somewhere in the file (cybernetic-tool signaling pattern)
- [ ] Confirm the prompts are still phrased as OFFERS, not as gates or auto-routes

**Section D: Pre-commit + verify**

- [ ] Run `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task4.sh` against pre-edit file; confirm it FAILS (RED phase).
- [ ] Make edits per Sections A-C.
- [ ] Run `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task4.sh` against post-edit file; confirm "All checks passed."
- [ ] Manual spot-check: read both updated prompts; verify they're (a) explicit about prose focus, (b) concrete (have example invocation), (c) still offers (architect can decline).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass.
- [ ] Commit (single commit; do not amend).

## Success Criteria

**Verification grep checks (must all pass):**
- [ ] `grep -E 'prose focus|prose context|with focus|with the focus' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` succeeds (prose-focus referenced)
- [ ] `grep -E 'examine|focus on|focused on' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` succeeds (concrete example wording)
- [ ] `! grep -F -- '--scope' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` succeeds (no flag references)
- [ ] `! grep -F -- '--epic' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` succeeds
- [ ] `grep -E 'Would you like to run /intuition' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` succeeds (offer pattern preserved)
- [ ] `grep -F 'The architect decides' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` succeeds (architect-decides language preserved)

**Manual checks:**
- [ ] Both prompts contain explicit prose-focus example invocations (not just abstract "pass focus")
- [ ] Both prompts remain framed as offers, not mandates
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Edge Cases & Failure Modes

**Existing wording is more concise than my proposed update:**
- The proposed updates make the prompts longer (adding example wording). If this feels verbose, prefer concision: a single concrete invocation example per prompt is sufficient. Length should not balloon by more than ~6-8 lines per prompt.

**Architect-decides language might already be present elsewhere:**
- If "The architect decides" already appears in skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md (e.g., in the Step 3 friction-detection footer "Do NOT auto-redirect. This is a routing offer, not a gate. The architect decides."), no action needed. Verify with grep.

**[N] / [specific area] / [list-of-affected-components] are template markers:**
- These are placeholders the brainstorm process fills in at runtime. Preserve them as bracketed placeholders so the skill knows to substitute.

**Concurrent edits:**
- Single-author refactor; no concurrency concerns.

## Notes for next task
After this task completes, the next iteration will:
- Task 5 (final): Cross-references sweep + version bump:
  - /ponder/SKILL.md still has Mode 1/Mode 2 cross-references to /intuition (lines ~53, 124, 212, 232, 258, 392 per bd-5p1 learnings) — update or remove these stale cross-references
  - CLAUDE.md mentions of /intuition modes/scopes — update
  - README.md mentions of /intuition modes/scopes — update
  - .claude-plugin/plugin.json minor version bump (e.g., 3.7.0 -> 3.8.0)
  - Final epic-level success-criteria verification (all 16 boxes)

title: Task 5: finishing-branch — tests before close, close after integration choice, honest discard
description: Restructure finishing-a-development-branch: verify tests before any epic close; merge/MR/keep close the epic, discard records a note and never closes (R6)
design:
## Goal
skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md verifies tests BEFORE any epic close, closes the epic only inside the successful integration paths (merge / MR / keep), and the discard path leaves honest bd state: epic stays open with a note recording the discard. commands/finish-branch.md describes the same order.

## Why
Epic bd-k9aa R6. Today Step 1 runs `bd close bd-1` (L72) before Step 2 verifies tests (L77-100) — a test failure STOPs with the epic already closed. And Option 4 (discard, L198-220) contains zero bd commands: the branch is deleted while the epic stays closed, claiming completed work that no longer exists. The command stub commands/finish-branch.md restates the old order ("1. Closes the bd epic / 2. Verifies tests pass" — verified 2026-07-08) and must match the new one.

## Context
Settled design (2026-07-08 brainstorm, epic Key Decisions): epic close moves AFTER the integration choice — no reopen dance, state always matches reality.

Current structure (verified 2026-07-08): Step 1 "Close bd Epic" L47-74 (child-task verification + `bd close bd-1` at L72) · Step 2 "Verify Tests" L77-100 · Step 3 "Determine Base Branch" L104 · Step 4 "Present Options" L114 · Step 5 "Execute Choice" L133 (Option 1 Merge L135, Option 2 MR L153, Option 3 Keep L190, Option 4 Discard L198-220) · Step 6 "Cleanup Worktree" L224. Examples at L244-283 narrate the OLD order ("Step 1: Epic closed ✓ ... After closing epic"); "Rules That Have No Exceptions" L425 and "Common Excuses" L433 may also reference ordering.

## Implementation
1. Step 1: rename to "Verify Epic Tasks Complete" — keep the child-task verification (all child tasks closed in bd), REMOVE the `bd close bd-1` command and any "epic closed" claims. Note that the epic is closed later, in Step 5's integration paths.
2. Step 2 (Verify Tests): position unchanged; its STOP-on-failure path now honestly leaves the epic open — adjust any wording that assumes the epic is already closed.
3. Steps 3-4: unchanged.
4. Step 5, Options 1-3 (Merge / MR / Keep): each gains an explicit `bd close bd-<epic-id>` step at its success point (after merged-result tests pass for Option 1; after MR created for Option 2; at the keep decision for Option 3).
5. Step 5, Option 4 (Discard): after the exact-confirmation flow (unchanged), do NOT close the epic. Add: `bd update bd-<epic-id> --notes "Implementation discarded <date>: <reason>. Branch <branch-name> deleted. Epic remains open — re-attempt or close explicitly."`
6. Update the examples (L244-283) to the new order: the skipped-tests anti-example keeps its teaching point but the narration must match the new step order; the "MANDATORY: Verify tests BEFORE presenting options" example stays valid.
7. Sweep "Rules That Have No Exceptions" and "Common Excuses" for old-order references (e.g., "close the epic first") and align.
8. Update the skill's frontmatter description / overview / quick-reference table if they state the old order.
9. commands/finish-branch.md: rewrite the "What it does" numbered list (verified 2026-07-08, currently "1. Closes the bd epic / 2. Verifies tests pass / 3. Presents integration options / 4. Executes your choice / 5. Cleans up worktree") to: "1. Verifies all epic tasks are closed / 2. Verifies tests pass / 3. Presents integration options (merge locally, create MR, keep as-is, discard) / 4. Executes your choice — merge/MR/keep close the epic; discard records a note and leaves it open / 5. Cleans up worktree as appropriate".

## Tests
None because these are documentation files with no runtime surface; verification is grep + read-through.

## Verification
- `grep -n "bd close" skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md` → matches only inside Step 5 Options 1-3, inside examples narrating the NEW order, or in negative statements; none in Steps 1-2 and none anywhere in Option 4
- `grep -n "bd update.*--notes" skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md` → 1 match, inside Option 4
- Read step headings in order: task verification → tests → base branch → options → choice (close inside 1-3, note inside 4) → cleanup
- Option 4 contains no `bd close`
- `grep -n "Closes the bd epic" commands/finish-branch.md` → zero matches
- Pre-commit hooks pass on the task's commit

## Boundaries
- Only modify skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md and commands/finish-branch.md
- Do not change the exact-confirmation discard safety flow ("Type 'discard' to confirm")
- Do not change worktree-cleanup semantics (Step 6, including the keep-worktree-for-MR-feedback guidance)

title: Rework: Brainstorm partner mode + plan-doc ingestion
description: Step 1 becomes investigate-first partner mode (consider pattern); AskUserQuestion-native questioning; R1 plan-doc ingestion as a second entry with file+SHA provenance, late binding, and idea-first preserved as first-class. Fixes F3.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Step 1 becomes investigate-first partner mode: when the topic touches code, investigate before questioning; share findings; offer opinions; questions carry evidence-grounded recommendations (generalizes the proven consider-skill pattern).
- Questioning guidance is AskUserQuestion-native: the Priority/lettered-options text template is removed; at most 4 questions per round; recommended option listed first; one CRITICAL question at a time preserved.
- R1 ingestion as a second entry, never a replacement: doc provided (path or pasted slice) means ingest; no doc means idea-first, fully supported, never nagged about a missing doc; mode detection is automatic, no interrogation.
- Ingestion behavior: extract binding constraints; play back settled-vs-open; the question round covers only Open-for-design items; settled decisions are never re-asked.
- Provenance: epics record source file plus commit SHA from the planning repo; several epics may cite one slice; untracked or unversioned input falls back to path plus date, flagged unversioned; late binding supported when an idea-first session discovers a governing doc mid-design.
- Re-entry drift check: resuming work tied to provenance flags when the planning repo copy of the file has changed since the recorded SHA.
- Research dispatch parallelism explicit: when both investigators apply, dispatch both in a single message.
- plugin.json version bumped before final push.

## Boundaries
In scope: brainstorming SKILL.md Steps 1-2 plus the new ingestion step; brainstorming/examples.md updated for the new Step 1.
Out of scope: pipeline order and Steps 5-8 (RW1 epic); schema definition (RW5 epic — consumed here); notice emission (RW4 epic).

## Dependencies
Depends on: RW1 epic (step structure) and RW5 epic (slice schema to ingest).

## Context for Brainstorming
Finding F3 in REWORK_PRINCIPLES.md. The proven pattern: consider SKILL.md Steps 1-2 and rules 1-3 (investigate first, share findings, have opinions — partner, not therapist). Friction evidence from this initiative's own planning: Step 1 fought the input doc and required conscious deviation to an ingestion playback; the untracked input doc exposed the need for a provenance fallback; preordain's escalation intake is the existing don't-re-ask pattern this epic generalizes.
notes: GATE STATE (macro lead): RW3 STARTING, lead-direct per D2-AMENDED. Decomposition (async veto open): T1 Step 1-2 partner rewrite — investigate BEFORE questioning when topic touches code (consider pattern), share findings, opine, evidence-grounded questions; parallel dispatch explicit; Step-1-exit sizing gate preserved. T2 AskUserQuestion-native guidance — Priority/lettered template removed, max 4/round, recommended-first, one CRITICAL at a time, expired-box-is-not-an-answer patience rule. T3 plan-doc ingestion as second entry — detect path/paste, extract binding constraints, settled/open playback, question round covers only Open-for-design, idea-first stays default and never nagged, slice shape per skills/common-patterns/brainstormable-unit.md. T4 provenance (file+SHA, untracked fallback flagged), N epics per slice, late binding mid-design, re-entry drift check; Step 5 template gains Provenance line (required by epic requirements; noted as boundary extension). T5 examples.md aligned to new Step 1. T6 sweep + 3.16.0.
close_reason: 6 tasks + gap fixes; reviewer found 4 critical + 1 important, all fixed; merged at v3.16.0.

title: T5: adr-template model-link routing
description: R8 route adr-template model-link through ponder UPDATE instead of direct .c4 edit
design:
## Goal
Reword adr-template's "Link from model" principle so the model link is added through a ponder UPDATE dispatch rather than a direct `.c4` edit (R8).

## Why
R8: adr-template L47 instructs adding a `link ... 'ADR-NNN'` line directly to components' `.c4` files — which violates ponder's single-owner rule (skills/ponder/SKILL.md L328: "No skill, agent, or workflow creates or modifies .c4 files directly. Ponder is the single owner."). ponder's UPDATE mode already supports ADR links (agents/ponder.md L92: "If adding a new ADR: include `link ./docs/arch/adr/adr-NNN.md 'ADR-NNN'` on relevant component(s)"), so the instruction just needs to route there. If skipped: the template invites the exact direct-.c4-edit the single-owner invariant forbids, and R8 exists precisely to close that contradiction.

## Changes
- `skills/common-patterns/adr-template.md` line 47: replace the bullet
  `- **Link from model:** When an ADR relates to specific components, add `link ./docs/arch/adr/adr-NNN.md 'ADR-NNN'` to those components' `.c4` files. This makes the decision navigable from the diagram.`
  with a version that routes through ponder, e.g.:
  `- **Link from model (via ponder):** When an ADR relates to specific components, add the model link through a ponder UPDATE dispatch — ponder places `link ./docs/arch/adr/adr-NNN.md 'ADR-NNN'` on the relevant components (see agents/ponder.md UPDATE mode). Never edit `.c4` files directly; ponder is the single owner of all `.c4` operations.`

## Verification
- `grep -n "to those components' " skills/common-patterns/adr-template.md` → returns nothing (the direct-edit phrasing is gone).
- `grep -n "ponder UPDATE\|single owner" skills/common-patterns/adr-template.md` → the reworded L47 routes through ponder and names it the single owner.
- Cross-check consistency (read-only): skills/ponder/SKILL.md L328 (single-owner rule) and agents/ponder.md L92 (UPDATE supports ADR links) — the reworded instruction agrees with both.

title: T4: preordain rule 7 + AIC durable home
description: R5 make leaf-epic AIC unconditional in rule 7; R7 record initiative AIC in each leaf epic's Context + arch-impact-check Recording line
design:
## Goal
Make preordain's rule 7 agree that leaf epics ALWAYS run the Architecture Impact Check (only the /intuition re-offer is friction-conditional) (R5), and give the initiative-level AIC result a durable home in each leaf epic's Context for Brainstorming section, named consistently in architecture-impact-check.md (R7).

## Why
R5: brainstorming Step 4 runs the AIC unconditionally and is the fixed point. preordain rule 7 (L235) currently says leaf epics run it "only if new friction appears," and the excuse-list entry (L247) actively discourages the per-leaf AIC — both contradict brainstorming. R7: preordain's initiative-level AIC is currently "recorded in session output" (L278, ephemeral) and architecture-impact-check.md L28 names an undefined "initiative record"; the result must be reconstructable from bd alone. Resolved fork: record it into EACH leaf epic's existing Context for Brainstorming section (no new leaf-epic section — brainstormable-unit schema is out of scope). If skipped: the two skills disagree on whether leaf epics run the AIC, and the initiative AIC evaporates when the session ends.

## Context
Read before starting:
- `skills/preordain/SKILL.md`: rule 7 at L235; excuse-list entry at L247; Step 4 (initiative AIC) at L129-135; Step 5 leaf-epic creation template at L148-189 — the `## Context for Brainstorming` section of that template is L182-186; the "Produces" line at L278 (`Architecture Impact Check recorded in session output`); the verification checklist at L257-258.
- `skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md` Recording section at L25-28 (`Record YES/NO for each in the epic's Architecture Impact section (brainstorm) / or the initiative record (preordain).`).
- `skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` Step 4 is the FIXED POINT — do NOT edit it; rule 7 conforms to it.
- preordain rule 4 (L232): preordain creates only leaf epics (no initiative-level bd record) — the rejected R7 option; do not create one.

## Implementation
1. preordain L235 (rule 7): reword so the per-leaf-epic AIC is unconditional and only the /intuition re-offer is friction-gated. Intent: "Architecture analysis happens at BOTH levels — the initiative-level AIC + /intuition offer runs here (once, for the full initiative); each leaf epic still runs its own AIC during brainstorming Step 4 (always), re-offering /intuition only if new friction appears." Keep it one rule entry.
2. preordain L247 (excuse-list entry): the live line reads `- "Each brainstorm session will do its own architecture check" → violates rule 7; architecture impact check happens here at initiative level`. Reword it so it no longer discourages the per-leaf AIC — target skipping the INITIATIVE-level check instead. Intent: "'Each brainstorm session will do its own architecture check, so I can skip the initiative-level one' → violates rule 7; the initiative-level AIC happens here. Leaf epics still run their own AIC during brainstorming Step 4 — that does not replace the initiative-level analysis."
3. preordain Step 4 (L129-135): add a sentence directing where the result is recorded — into each leaf epic's Context for Brainstorming section at creation (forward-reference Step 5).
4. preordain Step 5 template, under `## Context for Brainstorming` (L182-186): add an instruction that preordain writes the initiative-level AIC result here, identically across all leaf epics, so it is reconstructable from any epic. Include a small fill-in block, e.g.:
   ```
   Initiative Architecture Impact (from preordain Step 4 — identical across this initiative's leaf epics):
   - Q1 new component: [YES/NO]  Q2 public interface change: [YES/NO]  Q3 cross-component dependency: [YES/NO]  Q4 new request path: [YES/NO]  Q5 responsibility moved: [YES/NO]
   - Result: [N] YES. /intuition [offered and run / offered and deferred / not offered (0 YES)].
   ```
   This reuses the existing Context section — no new section is added to the leaf-epic template.
5. preordain L278 (Produces): change `Architecture Impact Check recorded in session output` to `Architecture Impact Check result recorded in each leaf epic's Context for Brainstorming section (reconstructable from bd)`.
6. preordain verification checklist (L257): keep the AIC-recorded item but make it consistent — the result is recorded in each leaf epic's Context.
7. `skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md` L27-28 (Recording line): change `Record YES/NO for each in the epic's Architecture Impact section (brainstorm) or the initiative record (preordain).` to name the real home — `Record YES/NO for each in the epic's Architecture Impact section (brainstorm), or — for preordain's initiative-level check — in each leaf epic's Context for Brainstorming section (identical across the initiative's epics, so it is reconstructable from bd alone).`
8. preordain `<skill_overview>` (L7): the live clause reads `Architecture analysis happens here — not in per-leaf-epic brainstorming.` — this contradicts the corrected rule 7 (leaf epics DO always run their own AIC during brainstorming) and is exactly the contradiction R5 exists to eliminate. Reword so the overview agrees with rule 7. Intent: initiative-level architecture analysis happens here; each leaf epic still runs its own Architecture Impact Check during brainstorming. E.g.: `Decomposes large initiatives that exceed a single leaf epic's capacity into independently brainstormable leaf epics with explicit dependencies. Initiative-level architecture analysis happens here; each leaf epic still runs its own Architecture Impact Check during brainstorming.`

## Verification
- `grep -n "only if new friction" skills/preordain/SKILL.md` → this phrase no longer gates the leaf-epic AIC (rule 7 now says leaf epics always run it; friction gates only the /intuition re-offer).
- `grep -n "session output" skills/preordain/SKILL.md` → returns nothing (Produces line updated).
- `grep -n "Context for Brainstorming" skills/preordain/SKILL.md` → shows the initiative-AIC block added to the Step 5 template.
- `grep -n "initiative record" skills/common-patterns/architecture-impact-check.md` → returns nothing; the Recording line now names each leaf epic's Context.
- `grep -n "not in per-leaf-epic" skills/preordain/SKILL.md` → returns nothing; the skill_overview no longer contradicts rule 7.
- Coherence check: the phrase `each leaf epic's Context for Brainstorming` appears in BOTH preordain (Produces + Step 5 template) and architecture-impact-check.md (Recording line) — the R7 home is stated identically.

## Boundaries
- Do NOT edit `skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` Step 4 — it is the fixed point rule 7 conforms to.
- Do NOT add a new section to the leaf-epic template — reuse Context for Brainstorming (brainstormable-unit schema is out of scope).
- Do NOT create an initiative-level bd record (rejected R7 option; preordain creates only leaf epics per rule 4).
notes: Stage 2 PASS WITH CONCERNS on commit dad1548: skill_overview L7 ('Architecture analysis happens here — not in per-leaf-epic brainstorming') contradicts corrected rule 7. Spec amended with step 8; re-dispatched (no promotion — one-line doc fix, not a capability failure).

title: Rework: Pipeline coherence
description: Establish one canonical pipeline shape across the five workflow skills; move sizing gate to entry; reconcile SRE checklist with two-tier templates; make the F5 calibration decisions. Foundation epic - RW2/RW3/RW4/RW6a block on it.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- One canonical pipeline shape, stated identically in brainstorming, writing-plans, sre-task-refinement, executing-plans, CLAUDE.md, README: brainstorm owns task-tree creation; per-task codebase verification (currently writing-plans' distinctive step) runs on the mainline path before specs are final; SRE batch review runs at exactly one defined point.
- Sizing gate evaluated at brainstorm entry, before epic creation (today it sits at Step 6a, after Step 5 has already created the epic).
- One sizing threshold defined in one place and referenced everywhere (today: 20 in brainstorming, 10 and 5-10 in preordain, 10 in CLAUDE.md).
- SRE checklist reconciled with the two-tier task templates: tier-aware criteria; no auto-reject rule that a well-formed simple-tier task trips (current conflicts: 3+ measurable criteria, 3+ checklist items, mandatory anti-patterns section).
- brainstorming/examples.md agrees with the skill: the upfront-task-tree example currently teaches iterative creation as the correction — the opposite of Step 6c.
- Calibration decisions made and encoded in the templates: (a) medium-spec code depth (complete code vs intent plus constraints plus tests); (b) task ceiling (keep 15 min or raise); (c) Stage-2 per-task review placement.
- Small fixes: executing-plans Stage 1 diffs against recorded pre-dispatch HEAD, not HEAD~1; stale Opus 4.1 references removed from CLAUDE.md; Grand planner naming removed from preordain; TodoWrite-vs-bd contradiction resolved in using-hyper text.
- plugin.json version bumped before final push.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Pipeline ownership described identically in all six files; rg finds no claim that writing-plans creates the task tree
- [ ] Sizing gate text lives at Step 1 exit in brainstorming; Step 6a is a re-check-if-grown only
- [ ] skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md exists (gate, leaf target, classification bands, spec-depth rule, executor-promotion flag) and is cited by brainstorming, preordain, writing-plans, sre-task-refinement, CLAUDE.md
- [ ] rg for stale numbers returns zero in skills/, CLAUDE.md, README.md: 'exceed ~20', '3-4 components', '5-15 min' ceilings outside the constants file
- [ ] SRE Categories 1/3/5/7 tier-aware; simple tasks pass without anti-patterns sections or 3-item checklists
- [ ] examples.md example 2 presents upfront verified tree as correct, iterative mid-execution creation as the failure
- [ ] executing-plans records pre-dispatch HEAD and diffs against it; rg 'HEAD~1' in the skill returns zero
- [ ] executing-plans honors 'Executor: opus' promotion, including auto-promotion on re-dispatch after BLOCKED/CONCERNS
- [ ] rg 'Grand planner' and 'Opus 4.1' return zero in skills/, CLAUDE.md, README.md
- [ ] using-hyper checklist rule is tracker-aware (bd when repo uses beads, TodoWrite otherwise) at unchanged strength
- [ ] skills/commands/, INVENTORY.md, REWRITE_COMPLETE.md deleted with no dangling references
- [ ] plugin.json = 3.12.0; all changes committed and pushed

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO touching brainstorming Step 1 questioning style or AskUserQuestion guidance (boundary: RW3/bd-uyc owns partner-mode redesign)
- ❌ NO converting SRE or intuition to subagent dispatch in skill text (boundary: RW2/bd-ope owns dispatch mechanics)
- ❌ NO template single-sourcing beyond the constants file (boundary: RW6a/bd-die owns consolidation)
- ❌ NO softening MUST/REQUIRED language while editing (user standing rule: restructure, never soften)
- ❌ NO inline threshold numbers in edited files — cite pipeline-constants.md (reason: re-embedding numbers recreates the drift this epic exists to kill)
- ❌ NO placeholder text in any edited skill (system rule: placeholders are auto-reject)

## Approach
Approach A ratified: brainstorm owns the mainline. Step 6c gains mandatory pre-create verification (investigator for broad questions, surgical lead reads for exact edit sites); writing-plans re-scopes to the off-mainline spec repair/expansion utility (reviewer gap-fix tasks, mid-flight amendments, externally created tasks); SRE keeps its single post-tree point. Calibrations ratified: medium ceiling 5-30 min (simple 2-5 unchanged); medium specs carry intent + tests + verification + boundaries with exact code only where the planner verified the exact site; per-task review keeps BOTH stages with sharpened non-overlapping lenses — Stage 1 lead reads every DONE diff (against recorded pre-dispatch HEAD) for epic-coherence only (anti-patterns, requirement drift, cross-task consistency); Stage 2 fresh reviewer owns spec-match + code quality. New executor-promotion flag ('Executor: opus') defined in constants, honored by executing-plans, settable by planner, SRE, or lead auto-promotion on re-dispatch failure — one rung before user escalation.

## Architecture
New: skills/common-patterns/pipeline-constants.md (single source: gate >10 tasks / 3+ components, leaf target 5-10, bands simple 2-5 / medium 5-30, spec-depth rule, promotion flag). Modified: brainstorming (gate to Step 1 exit, 6c verification, citations), writing-plans (utility re-scope, spec-depth), sre-task-refinement (tier-aware categories, promotion recommendation), executing-plans (lens split, diff base, promotion honor), preordain (naming, citations), using-hyper (tracker rule, mainline chain), brainstorming/examples.md (example inversion), CLAUDE.md + README.md (pipeline sections). Deleted: skills/commands/ (legacy duplicates), INVENTORY.md, REWRITE_COMPLETE.md (stale status docs).

## Architecture Impact
- [x] Creates a new component/module — YES (pipeline-constants reference)
- [x] Changes public interface of existing component — YES (writing-plans contract, executing-plans review protocol, Step 6c contract)
- [x] Adds/removes cross-component dependency — YES (five files depend on constants; per-task code-reviewer dependency lens changes)
- [ ] Creates new request path through 2+ components — NO (existing paths re-specified)
- [x] Moves responsibility between components — YES (verification writing-plans→brainstorm; review lenses redistributed)

Result: 4 boxes checked. /intuition was offered and deferred per the ratified initiative-level decision (post-rework validation audit).

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The pipeline contradicts itself about its own shape (F2): three different orderings on file, four threshold variants, an examples file teaching the opposite of its skill, and an SRE checklist that auto-rejects the tasks the upstream template mandates. Fresh sessions arbitrate these contradictions silently and differently each time.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- Findings review 2026-07-06 (full corpus read, file:line anchored): F2 contradictions, F5 calibrations, F7 bugs — digested in REWORK_PRINCIPLES.md section 4
- README.md:14 — fourth threshold variant ('~10 tasks or 3-4 components'), discovered this session
- skills/commands/ — legacy 3-file duplicate of commands/, dead since plugin structure landed
- INVENTORY.md (2026-02-14), REWRITE_COMPLETE.md (2026-04-30) — stale root status docs describing superseded pipeline states

**External:**
- None because this is a plugin-internal rework of markdown skill files with no external dependencies.

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Pipeline shape A/B/C | A (ratified via answers operating in its frame) | brainstorm owns mainline; writing-plans becomes repair utility |
| Medium task ceiling | Raise to 30 min | halves dispatch overhead; bands live in constants file |
| Medium spec depth | Intent + tests, code where verified | kills stale-code-in-specs; drift rails are tests + boundaries |
| Stage-2 review | KEEP separate dispatch (user: 'per task review catches things often') | merge recommendation withdrawn; lenses sharpened instead |
| Classification value | 'less important unless we allow promotion to Opus executor' | executor-promotion flag added: planner/SRE/lead-auto-promote setters |
| T12 deletions | Approved ('go') | legacy dir + two stale docs removed |

### Approaches Considered
#### 1. Brainstorm owns mainline; writing-plans as repair utility (chosen)
**What it is:** Verification folds into Step 6c; writing-plans serves spec-less tasks off-mainline; SRE point unchanged.
**Investigation:** 16 writing-plans touchpoints inventoried; verification language exists in writing-plans 2c ready to port.
**Pros:** one spec owner (kills F2 root cause); verification on the path actually used; approval stays at existing two gates.
**Cons:** brainstorming grows (offset by RW3/RW6a offloading); 16 files need consistent re-description.
**Chosen because:** matches observed usage; eliminates dual ownership; no new governor polling.

#### 2. writing-plans as spec factory (rejected)
**What it is:** brainstorm emits stubs; writing-plans expands each with verification and per-task approval.
**Why explored:** clean design/specification separation; natural session boundary for split cadence.
**Pros:** smaller brainstorm sessions.
**Cons:** N per-task approval interruptions per epic (polling — violates gates-at-commitments under P2); dual spec ownership persists (the F2 drift engine).
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** trades the user's top-ranked costs (drift, attention) for a separation the artifacts do not need.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** a second human enters the loop who owns specification separately from design.

#### 3. Minimal sentence patch (rejected)
**What it is:** fix contradictory lines, keep both mainlines documented.
**Why explored:** cheapest option; establishes the floor.
**Pros:** smallest diff.
**Cons:** fails two immutable requirements (mainline verification, one canonical shape).
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** does not satisfy the epic contract.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** the epic's requirements are formally amended.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** skill text of the five pipeline skills; using-hyper tracker rule + chain mentions; examples.md; CLAUDE.md + README pipeline sections; constants file creation; stale artifact deletion; version bump.
**Out of scope:** Step 1 partner-mode redesign and plan-doc ingestion (bd-uyc); dispatch mechanics and agent model policy beyond the promotion flag (bd-ope); gate-state persistence and notices (bd-erq); template single-sourcing and routing table (bd-die); guardrail re-baseline (bd-3j3).

### Open Questions
- None because all four calibration decisions were ratified in-session (2026-07-06) and the promotion mechanism is fully specified in the constants file design.
